title: Ch 1 - Polity - PYQ by PMF IAS

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to the Indian polity, consider the following statements:
1. An Ordinance can amend any Central Act.
2. An Ordinance can abridge a Fundamental Right.
3. An Ordinance can come into effect from a back date.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Indian polity, consider the following statements: 1. An Ordinance can amend any Central Act. 2. An Ordinance can abridge a Fundamental Right. 3. An Ordinance can come into effect from a back date. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I and III only**. An **Ordinance** can amend a **Central Act** and can be implemented **retrospectively** (back date), but it **cannot abridge a Fundamental Right**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Constitution of India explicitly mentions that in certain spheres the Governor of a State acts in his/her own discretion.
2. The President of India can, of his/her own, reserve a bill passed by a State Legislature for his/her consideration without it being forwarded by the Governor of the State concerned.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Constitution of India explicitly mentions that in certain spheres the Governor of a State acts in his/her own discretion. 2. The President of India can, of his/her own, reserve a bill passed by a State Legislature for his/her consideration without it being forwarded by the Governor of the State concerned. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I only**. The **Constitution of India** explicitly mentions the **discretionary powers** of the **Governor** in specific spheres, but the **President** cannot reserve a state bill on their own without the Governor forwarding it.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following pairs:
1. Separation of Judiciary from the Executive in the public services of the State - The Directive Principles of the State Policy
2. Valuing and preserving of the rich heritage of our composite culture - The Fundamental Duties
3. Prohibition of employment of children below the age of 14 years in factories - The Fundamental Rights
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.questionText: Consider the following pairs: 1. Separation of Judiciary from the Executive in the public services of the State - The Directive Principles of the State Policy 2. Valuing and preserving of the rich heritage of our composite culture - The Fundamental Duties 3. Prohibition of employment of children below the age of 14 years in factories - The Fundamental Rights How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **All the three** pairs are correctly matched. **Article 50 (DPSP)** covers separation of judiciary, **Article 51A (Fundamental Duties)** covers heritage, and **Article 24 (Fundamental Rights)** covers prohibition of child labor.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Consider the following statements: With reference to the Constitution of India, if an area in a State is declared as Scheduled Area under the Fifth Schedule:
1. The State Government loses its executive power in such areas and a local body assumes total administration.
2. The Union Government can take over the total administration of such areas under certain circumstances on the recommendations of the Governor.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: With reference to the Constitution of India, if an area in a State is declared as Scheduled Area under the Fifth Schedule: 1. The State Government loses its executive power in such areas and a local body assumes total administration. 2. The Union Government can take over the total administration of such areas under certain circumstances on the recommendations of the Governor. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither I nor II**. The **State Government** does not lose its executive power; instead, its power extends to the **Scheduled Areas** subject to the provisions of the **Fifth Schedule**. The Union does not assume "total" administration.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Consider the following subjects under the Constitution of India:
1. List I Union List, in the Seventh Schedule
2. Extent of the executive power of a State

3. Conditions of the Governor's office For a constitutional amendment with respect to which of the above, ratification by the Legislatures of not less than one-half of the States is required before presenting the bill to the President of India for assent?
questions.questionText: Consider the following subjects under the Constitution of India: 1. List I Union List, in the Seventh Schedule 2. Extent of the executive power of a State 3. Conditions of the Governor's office For a constitutional amendment with respect to which of the above, ratification by the Legislatures of not less than one-half of the States is required before presenting the bill to the President of India for assent?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I and II only**. Under **Article 368**, amendments to the **Seventh Schedule** lists and the **executive power of the Union and States** require **ratification by half the States**. Conditions of the Governor's office do not.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to the Indian polity, consider the following statements:
1. The Governor of a State is not answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of his/her office.
2. No criminal proceedings shall be instituted or continued against the Governor during his/her term of office.
3. Members of a State Legislature are not liable to any proceedings in any court in respect of anything said within the House.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Indian polity, consider the following statements: 1. The Governor of a State is not answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of his/her office. 2. No criminal proceedings shall be instituted or continued against the Governor during his/her term of office. 3. Members of a State Legislature are not liable to any proceedings in any court in respect of anything said within the House. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I, II and III**. **Article 361** grants the **Governor** immunity from court answers and criminal proceedings during their term. **Article 194** grants immunity to **State Legislature members** for statements made in the House.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements with regard to the pardoning power of the President of India:
1. The exercise of this power by the President can be subjected to limited judicial review.
2. The President can exercise this power without the advice of the Central Government.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements with regard to the pardoning power of the President of India: 1. The exercise of this power by the President can be subjected to limited judicial review. 2. The President can exercise this power without the advice of the Central Government. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I only**. The **President's pardoning power (Article 72)** is subject to **limited judicial review** (e.g., if exercised arbitrarily), but the President **must act on the advice** of the **Council of Ministers**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. On the dissolution of the House of the People, the Speaker shall not vacate his/her office until immediately before the first meeting of the House of the People after the dissolution.
2. According to the provisions of the Constitution of India, a Member of the House of the People on being elected as Speaker shall resign from his/her political party immediately.

3. The Speaker of the House of the People may be removed from his/her office by a resolution of the House of the People passed by a majority of all the then Members of the House, provided that no resolution shall be moved unless at least fourteen days' notice has been given of the intention to move the resolution.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. On the dissolution of the House of the People, the Speaker shall not vacate his/her office until immediately before the first meeting of the House of the People after the dissolution. 2. According to the provisions of the Constitution of India, a Member of the House of the People on being elected as Speaker shall resign from his/her political party immediately. 3. The Speaker of the House of the People may be removed from his/her office by a resolution of the House of the People passed by a majority of all the then Members of the House, provided that no resolution shall be moved unless at least fourteen days' notice has been given of the intention to move the resolution. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I and III only**. The **Speaker** remains in office until the new Lok Sabha meets. They can be removed by an **effective majority** with **14 days' notice**. In India, there is **no constitutional provision** requiring the Speaker to resign from their political party.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Consider the following statements:

1. If any question arises as to whether a Member of the House of the People has become subject to disqualification under the 10th Schedule, the President's decision in accordance with the opinion of the Council of Union Ministers shall be final.
2. There is no mention of the word 'political party' in the Constitution of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. If any question arises as to whether a Member of the House of the People has become subject to disqualification under the 10th Schedule, the President's decision in accordance with the opinion of the Council of Union Ministers shall be final. 2. There is no mention of the word 'political party' in the Constitution of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither I nor II**. Disqualification under the **10th Schedule (Anti-Defection Law)** is decided by the **Presiding Officer (Speaker/Chairman)**, not the President. The term **'political party'** is explicitly mentioned in the **10th Schedule**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
Statement I: In India, State Governments have no power for making rules for grant of concessions in respect of extraction of minor minerals even though such minerals are located in their territories.
Statement II: In India, the Central Government has the power to notify minor minerals under the relevant law.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Statement I: In India, State Governments have no power for making rules for grant of concessions in respect of extraction of minor minerals even though such minerals are located in their territories. 2. Statement II: In India, the Central Government has the power to notify minor minerals under the relevant law. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II does not explain Statement I
options.c: Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct
options.d: Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Statement I is not correct but Statement II is correct**. **State Governments** do have the power to make rules for the extraction of **minor minerals**, but the **Central Government** has the authority to notify what constitutes minor minerals.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Panchayats at the intermediate level exist in all States.
2. To be eligible to be a Member of a Panchayat at the intermediate level, a person should attain the age of thirty years.

3. The Chief Minister of a State constitutes a commission to review the financial position of Panchayats at the intermediate levels and to make recommendations regarding the distribution of net proceeds of taxes and duties, leviable by the State, between the State and Panchayats at the intermediate level.
Which of the statements given above are not correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Panchayats at the intermediate level exist in all States. 2. To be eligible to be a Member of a Panchayat at the intermediate level, a person should attain the age of thirty years. 3. The Chief Minister of a State constitutes a commission to review the financial position of Panchayats at the intermediate levels and to make recommendations regarding the distribution of net proceeds of taxes and duties, leviable by the State, between the State and Panchayats at the intermediate level. Which of the statements given above are not correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I, II and III** are not correct. Intermediate panchayats do **not exist in states with a population under 20 lakhs**. The eligible age is **21 years**, not 30. The **State Finance Commission** is constituted by the **Governor**, not the Chief Minister.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements about Lokpal:
1. The power of Lokpal applies to public servants of India, but not to the Indian public servants posted outside India.

2. The Chairperson or a Member shall not be a Member of the Parliament or a Member of the Legislature of any State or Union Territory, and only the Chief Justice of India, whether incumbent or retired, has to be its Chairperson.
3. The Chairperson or a Member shall not be a person of less than forty-five years of age on the date of assuming office as the Chairperson or Member, as the case may be.
4. Lokpal cannot inquire into the allegations of corruption against a sitting Prime Minister of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about Lokpal: 1. The power of Lokpal applies to public servants of India, but not to the Indian public servants posted outside India. 2. The Chairperson or a Member shall not be a Member of the Parliament or a Member of the Legislature of any State or Union Territory, and only the Chief Justice of India, whether incumbent or retired, has to be its Chairperson. 3. The Chairperson or a Member shall not be a person of less than forty-five years of age on the date of assuming office as the Chairperson or Member, as the case may be. 4. Lokpal cannot inquire into the allegations of corruption against a sitting Prime Minister of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

options.d: None of the above statements is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **III only**. Lokpal jurisdiction **applies to public servants outside India**. The Chairperson does **not necessarily** have to be a CJI. The Lokpal **can inquire** into allegations against a sitting Prime Minister (with certain safeguards). The age requirement of **at least 45 years** is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: As per Article 368 of the Constitution of India, the Parliament may amend any provision of the Constitution by way of:
1. Addition
2. Variation
3. Repeal
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: As per Article 368 of the Constitution of India, the Parliament may amend any provision of the Constitution by way of: 1. Addition 2. Variation 3. Repeal Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Article 368**, Parliament has the constituent power to amend any provision of the Constitution by way of **addition, variation, or repeal**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct in respect of a Money Bill in the Parliament?
1. Article 109 mentions a special procedure in respect of Money Bills.
2. A Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Council of States.
3. The Rajya Sabha can either approve the Bill or suggest changes but cannot reject it.
4. Amendments to a Money Bill suggested by the Rajya Sabha have to be accepted by the Lok Sabha.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct in respect of a Money Bill in the Parliament? 1. Article 109 mentions a special procedure in respect of Money Bills. 2. A Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Council of States. 3. The Rajya Sabha can either approve the Bill or suggest changes but cannot reject it. 4. Amendments to a Money Bill suggested by the Rajya Sabha have to be accepted by the Lok Sabha. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. **Article 109** specifies the procedure for Money Bills. They can **only be introduced in the Lok Sabha**. The **Rajya Sabha cannot reject** a Money Bill, but its suggestions are **not binding** on the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The North Eastern Council (NEC) was established by the North Eastern Council Act,
1971. Subsequent to the amendment of NEC Act in 2002, the Council comprises which of the following members?
1. Governor of the Constituent State
2. Chief Minister of the Constituent State
3. Three Members to be nominated by the President of India
4. The Home Minister of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: The North Eastern Council (NEC) was established by the North Eastern Council Act, 1971. Subsequent to the amendment of NEC Act in 2002, the Council comprises which of the following members? 1. Governor of the Constituent State 2. Chief Minister of the Constituent State 3. Three Members to be nominated by the President of India 4. The Home Minister of India Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Following the **2002 amendment**, the NEC comprises the **Governors and Chief Ministers** of constituent states, plus **three members nominated by the President**. The Home Minister acts as the ex-officio Chairman, but the explicit specific members listed in options 1, 2, and 3 define the core body constitution as per the question's parameters.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: How many Delimitation Commissions have been constituted by the Government of India till December 2023?
questions.questionText: How many Delimitation Commissions have been constituted by the Government of India till December 2023?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Four**. Delimitation Commissions have been set up four times in India: **1952, 1963, 1973, and 2002**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVII: Official Language
statementLines: The Constitution (71st Amendment) Act, 1992 amends the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution to include which of the following languages?
1. Konkani
2. Manipuri
3. Nepali
4. Maithili
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: The Constitution (71st Amendment) Act, 1992 amends the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution to include which of the following languages? 1. Konkani 2. Manipuri 3. Nepali 4. Maithili Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **71st Amendment Act (1992)** added **Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali** to the **Eighth Schedule**. Maithili was added later by the 92nd Amendment (2003).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct about the Constitution of India?
1. Powers of the Municipalities are given in Part IXA of the Constitution.
2. Emergency provisions are given in Part XVIII of the Constitution.
3. Provisions related to the amendment of the Constitution are given in Part XX of the Constitution.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct about the Constitution of India? 1. Powers of the Municipalities are given in Part IXA of the Constitution. 2. Emergency provisions are given in Part XVIII of the Constitution. 3. Provisions related to the amendment of the Constitution are given in Part XX of the Constitution. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. **Part IXA** covers Municipalities, **Part XVIII** contains Emergency provisions, and **Part XX** deals with the Amendment to the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is correct as per the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is correct as per the Constitution of India?

options.a: Inter-State trade and commerce is a State subject under the State List.
options.b: Inter-State migration is a State subject under the State List.
options.c: Inter-State quarantine is a Union subject under the Union List.
options.d: Corporation tax is a State subject under the State List.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Inter-State quarantine is a Union subject under the Union List**. Matters requiring uniform national policy like **inter-state quarantine** and **corporation tax** fall under the **Union List**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Under which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India has the Supreme Court of India placed the Right to Privacy?
questions.questionText: Under which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India has the Supreme Court of India placed the Right to Privacy?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 21**. The **Supreme Court** in the **Puttaswamy judgment (2017)** unanimously held that the **Right to Privacy** is an intrinsic part of the right to life and personal liberty under **Article 21**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha are correct?
1. Initially it was an ad-hoc Committee.
2. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint relating to unethical conduct of a member of the Lok Sabha.
3. This Committee cannot take up any matter which is sub-judice.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha are correct? 1. Initially it was an ad-hoc Committee. 2. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint relating to unethical conduct of a member of the Lok Sabha. 3. This Committee cannot take up any matter which is sub-judice. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. The **Ethics Committee** was initially an **ad-hoc committee** before becoming permanent. Anyone can file a complaint against a member if it's routed through another Lok Sabha member, and it **cannot take up sub-judice** matters.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: A Writ of Prohibition is an order issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts to:
questions.questionText: A Writ of Prohibition is an order issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts to:

options.a: A government officer prohibiting him from taking a particular action.
options.b: The Parliament/Legislative Assembly to pass a law on Prohibition.
options.c: The lower court prohibiting continuation of proceedings in a case.
options.d: The Government prohibiting it from following an unconstitutional policy.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the lower court prohibiting continuation of proceedings in a case**. A **Writ of Prohibition** is issued by a higher court to a **lower court or tribunal** to prevent it from exceeding its jurisdiction.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. It is the Governor of the State who recognizes and declares any community of that State as a Scheduled Tribe.
2. A community declared as a Scheduled Tribe in a State need not be so in another State.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. It is the Governor of the State who recognizes and declares any community of that State as a Scheduled Tribe. 2. A community declared as a Scheduled Tribe in a State need not be so in another State. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **President**, not the Governor, first notifies communities as **Scheduled Tribes** under Article 342. A community recognized in **one State may not be recognized in another**.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: With reference to the Union Budget, consider the following statements:
1. The Union Finance Minister, on behalf of the Prime Minister, lays the Annual Financial Statement before both the Houses of Parliament.
2. At the Union level, no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Union Budget, consider the following statements: 1. The Union Finance Minister, on behalf of the Prime Minister, lays the Annual Financial Statement before both the Houses of Parliament. 2. At the Union level, no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **President** (not the Prime Minister) causes the **Annual Financial Statement** to be laid before Parliament. A **demand for grant** requires the **recommendation of the President** under Article 113.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:
While any resolution for the removal of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is under consideration:
1. He/She shall not preside.
2. He/She shall not have the right to speak.
3. He/She shall not be entitled to vote on the resolution in the first instance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, consider the following statements: While any resolution for the removal of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is under consideration: 1. He/She shall not preside. 2. He/She shall not have the right to speak. 3. He/She shall not be entitled to vote on the resolution in the first instance. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. During a resolution for their removal, the **Speaker cannot preside**, but they **retain the right to speak** and can **vote in the first instance** (but not in the case of a tie).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:
1. A bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses on its dissolution.
2. A bill passed by the Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
3. A bill in regard to which the President of India notified his/her intention to summon the Houses to a joint sitting lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements: 1. A bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses on its dissolution. 2. A bill passed by the Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha. 3. A bill in regard to which the President of India notified his/her intention to summon the Houses to a joint sitting lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2**. A bill **pending in the Lok Sabha** or **passed by Lok Sabha but pending in Rajya Sabha** lapses. If the **President notifies a joint sitting** before dissolution, the **bill does not lapse**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:
1. Prorogation of a House by the President of India does not require the advice of the Council of Ministers.
2. Prorogation of a House is generally done after the House is adjourned sine die, but there is no bar to the President of India prorogating a House that is in session.
3. Dissolution of the Lok Sabha is done by the President of India who, save in exceptional circumstances, does so on the advice of the Council of Ministers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements: 1. Prorogation of a House by the President of India does not require the advice of the Council of Ministers. 2. Prorogation of a House is generally done after the House is adjourned sine die, but there is no bar to the President of India prorogating a House that is in session. 3. Dissolution of the Lok Sabha is done by the President of India who, save in exceptional circumstances, does so on the advice of the Council of Ministers. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3**. The **President acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers** for prorogation. Prorogation can technically occur while the House is in session. Dissolution is mostly done on **ministerial advice** unless the government loses its majority.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: In essence, what does 'Due Process of Law' mean?
questions.questionText: In essence, what does 'Due Process of Law' mean?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The principle of natural justice**. **Due Process of Law** inherently includes fairness, equity, and the principles of **natural justice**, moving beyond mere procedural compliance.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Statement-l: In India, prisons are managed by State Governments with their own rules and regulations for the day-to-day administration of prisons.
Statement-II: In India, prisons are governed by the Prisons Act, 1894 which expressly kept the subject of prisons in the control of Provincial Governments.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Statement-l: In India, prisons are managed by State Governments with their own rules and regulations for the day-to-day administration of prisons. 2. Statement-II: In India, prisons are governed by the Prisons Act, 1894 which expressly kept the subject of prisons in the control of Provincial Governments. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-l and Statement-ll are correct and Statement-ll is the correct explanation for Statement-l
options.b: Both Statement-l and Statement-ll are correct and Statement-ll is not the correct explanation for Statement-l
options.c: Statement-l is correct but Statement-ll is incorrect
options.d: Statement-l is incorrect but Statement-ll is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I**. Under the **State List**, prisons are managed by **State Governments**, rooted historically in the **Prisons Act, 1894**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one of the following statements best reflects the chief purpose of the 'Constitution' of a country?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements best reflects the chief purpose of the 'Constitution' of a country?

options.a: It determines the objective for the making of necessary laws.
options.b: It enables the creation of political offices and a government.
options.c: It defines and limits the powers of government.
options.d: It secures social justice, social equality and social security.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It defines and limits the powers of government**. The chief hallmark of constitutionalism is establishing a **limited government** by binding its organs to fundamental rules.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: In India,
which one of the following Constitutional Amendments was widely believed to be enacted to overcome the judicial interpretations of the Fundamental Rights?
questions.questionText: In India, which one of the following Constitutional Amendments was widely believed to be enacted to overcome the judicial interpretations of the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question was **Cancelled** by the UPSC. However, the **1st Amendment** was primarily enacted to overcome judicial rulings relating to fundamental rights (like *Romesh Thapar* and *ChampakamDorairajan* cases).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following organizations/bodies in India:
1. The National Commission for Backward Classes
2. The National Human Rights Commission
3. The National Law Commission
4. The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
How many of the above are constitutional bodies?
questions.questionText: Consider the following organizations/bodies in India: 1. The National Commission for Backward Classes 2. The National Human Rights Commission 3. The National Law Commission 4. The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission How many of the above are constitutional bodies?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Only one**. The **National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC)** is a **constitutional body** under Article 338B. The NHRC, Law Commission, and Consumer Disputes Commission are statutory or executive bodies.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements:

1. If the election of the President of India is declared void by the Supreme Court of India, all acts done by him/her in the performance of duties of his/her office of President before the date of decision become invalid.
2. Election for the post of the President of India can be postponed on the ground that some Legislative Assemblies have been dissolved and elections are yet to take place.
3. When a Bill is presented to the President of India, the Constitution prescribes time limits within which he/she has to declare his/her assent.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. If the election of the President of India is declared void by the Supreme Court of India, all acts done by him/her in the performance of duties of his/her office of President before the date of decision become invalid. 2. Election for the post of the President of India can be postponed on the ground that some Legislative Assemblies have been dissolved and elections are yet to take place. 3. When a Bill is presented to the President of India, the Constitution prescribes time limits within which he/she has to declare his/her assent. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **None**. Acts done by a President before their election is voided **remain valid**. Presidential elections **cannot be postponed** due to dissolved assemblies. The Constitution sets **no specific time limit** for the President to declare assent to a bill.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to Finance Bill and Money Bill in the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:
1. When the Lok Sabha transmits Finance Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it can amend or reject the Bill.
2. When the Lok Sabha transmits Money Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it cannot amend or reject the Bill; it can only make recommendations.
3. In the case of disagreement between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha, there is no joint sitting for Money Bill, but a joint sitting becomes necessary for Finance Bill.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to Finance Bill and Money Bill in the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements: 1. When the Lok Sabha transmits Finance Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it can amend or reject the Bill. 2. When the Lok Sabha transmits Money Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it cannot amend or reject the Bill; it can only make recommendations. 3. In the case of disagreement between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha, there is no joint sitting for Money Bill, but a joint sitting becomes necessary for Finance Bill. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **Only two**. **Statement 1 and 2 are correct**. Rajya Sabha can amend/reject an ordinary Finance Bill but **only recommend changes** to a Money Bill.

**Statement 3 is incorrect** because a joint sitting is *possible* for a Finance Bill, but it is **not "necessary"** (it is an option).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: With reference to 'Scheduled Areas' in India, consider the following statements:
1. Within a State, the notification of an area as Scheduled Area takes place through an Order of the President.
2. The largest administrative unit forming the Scheduled Area is the District and the lowest is the cluster of villages in the Block.
3. The Chief Ministers of the concerned States are required to submit annual reports to the Union Home Ministry on the administration of Scheduled Areas in the States.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to 'Scheduled Areas' in India, consider the following statements: 1. Within a State, the notification of an area as Scheduled Area takes place through an Order of the President. 2. The largest administrative unit forming the Scheduled Area is the District and the lowest is the cluster of villages in the Block. 3. The Chief Ministers of the concerned States are required to submit annual reports to the Union Home Ministry on the administration of Scheduled Areas in the States. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Only two**. The **President** notifies Scheduled Areas. The **Governor** (not the Chief Minister) is required to submit reports to the **President** on the administration of these areas.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following statements:

Statement-I: The Supreme Court of India has held in some judgements that the reservation policies made under Article 16(4) of the Constitution of India would be limited by Article 335 for maintenance of efficiency of administration.
Statement-II: Article 335 of the Constitution of India defines the term 'efficiency of administration'.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Statement-I: The Supreme Court of India has held in some judgements that the reservation policies made under Article 16(4) of the Constitution of India would be limited by Article 335 for maintenance of efficiency of administration. 2. Statement-II: Article 335 of the Constitution of India defines the term 'efficiency of administration'. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-l and Statement-Il are correct and Statement-Il is the correct explanation for Statement-l
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-Il is not the correct explanation for Statement-l
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-Il is incorrect
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: * *Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect**. The Supreme Court has linked**Article 16(4)**reservations to**Article 335**(efficiency). However, the Constitution**does not define** the term "efficiency of administration".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. According to the Constitution of India, the Central Government has a duty to protect States from internal disturbances.
2. The Constitution of India exempts the States from providing legal counsel to a person being held for preventive detention.
3. According to the Prevention of Terrorism Act, 2002, confession of the accused before the police cannot be used as evidence.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. According to the Constitution of India, the Central Government has a duty to protect States from internal disturbances. 2. The Constitution of India exempts the States from providing legal counsel to a person being held for preventive detention. 3. According to the Prevention of Terrorism Act, 2002, confession of the accused before the police cannot be used as evidence. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Only two**. **Article 355** places a duty on the Union to protect states from **internal disturbances**. **Article 22** exempts the state from providing legal counsel for **preventive detention**. Under POTA, confessions to senior police officers **could be used** as evidence (making statement 3 incorrect).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of election to the President of India:
1. The members nominated to either House of the Parliament or the Legislative Assemblies of States are also eligible to be included in the Electoral College.
2. Higher the number of elective Assembly seats, higher is the value of vote of each MLA of that State.
3. The value of vote of each MLA of Madhya Pradesh is greater than that of Kerala.

4. The value of vote of each MLA of Puducherry is higher than that of Arunachal Pradesh because the ratio of total population to total number of elective seats in Puducherry is greater as compared to Arunachal Pradesh.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of election to the President of India: 1. The members nominated to either House of the Parliament or the Legislative Assemblies of States are also eligible to be included in the Electoral College. 2. Higher the number of elective Assembly seats, higher is the value of vote of each MLA of that State. 3. The value of vote of each MLA of Madhya Pradesh is greater than that of Kerala. 4. The value of vote of each MLA of Puducherry is higher than that of Arunachal Pradesh because the ratio of total population to total number of elective seats in Puducherry is greater as compared to Arunachal Pradesh. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **Only one**. Statement 1 is false (nominated members cannot vote). Statement 2 is false (vote value depends on population ratio, not just seat numbers). Statement 3 is false.

**Statement 4 is correct** as Puducherry's population-to-seat ratio yields a higher vote value than Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of the Constitution Day:
1. Statement-l: The Constitution Day is celebrated on 26th November every year to promote constitutional values among citizens.
Statement-II: On 26th November, 1949, the Constituent Assembly of India set up a Drafting Committee under the Chairmanship of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar to prepare a Draft Constitution of India.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of the Constitution Day: 1. Statement-l: The Constitution Day is celebrated on 26th November every year to promote constitutional values among citizens. 2. Statement-II: On 26th November, 1949, the Constituent Assembly of India set up a Drafting Committee under the Chairmanship of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar to prepare a Draft Constitution of India. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-l and Statement-ll are correct and Statement-ll is the correct explanation for Statement-l
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-ll are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-1
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.d: Statement-l is incorrect but Statement-Il is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: * *Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect**. Constitution Day is on**26th November**. However, the**Drafting Committee**was established much earlier on**August 29, 1947**. Nov 26, 1949, was the day the Constitution was**adopted**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Pursuant to the report of H.N. Sanyal Committee, the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971 was passed.
2. The Constitution of India empowers the Supreme Court and the High Courts to punish for contempt of themselves.
3. The Constitution of India defines Civil Contempt and Criminal Contempt.
4. In India, the Parliament is vested with the powers to make laws on Contempt of Court.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Pursuant to the report of H.N. Sanyal Committee, the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971 was passed. 2. The Constitution of India empowers the Supreme Court and the High Courts to punish for contempt of themselves. 3. The Constitution of India defines Civil Contempt and Criminal Contempt. 4. In India, the Parliament is vested with the powers to make laws on Contempt of Court. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. The **H.N. Sanyal Committee** led to the 1971 Act. **Articles 129 and 215** empower SC and HCs to punish for contempt. Parliament has the power to make laws. The **Constitution does not define** civil and criminal contempt; the Act does.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. Government law officers and legal firms are recognised as advocates, but corporate lawyers and patent attorneys are excluded from recognition as advocates.
2. Bar Councils have the power to lay down the rules relating to legal education and recognition of law colleges.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Government law officers and legal firms are recognised as advocates, but corporate lawyers and patent attorneys are excluded from recognition as advocates. 2. Bar Councils have the power to lay down the rules relating to legal education and recognition of law colleges. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **2 only**. The **Bar Council of India** regulates legal education and recognizes law colleges.

Statement 1 is incorrect as the Advocates Act does not strictly exclude corporate lawyers from being recognized as advocates if they are enrolled on the state rolls.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. A bill amending the Constitution requires a prior recommendation of the President of India.
2. When a Constitution Amendment Bill is presented to the President of India, it is obligatory for the President of India to give his/her assent.
3. A Constitution Amendment Bill must be passed by both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha by a special majority and there is no provision for joint sitting.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. A bill amending the Constitution requires a prior recommendation of the President of India. 2. When a Constitution Amendment Bill is presented to the President of India, it is obligatory for the President of India to give his/her assent. 3. A Constitution Amendment Bill must be passed by both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha by a special majority and there is no provision for joint sitting. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. A Constitution Amendment Bill **does not require prior recommendation**. The President **must give assent** (per the 24th Amendment), and it **requires a special majority** without the provision of a joint sitting.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister.
2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister. 2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Constitution does not classify** ministers into ranks (this is a parliamentary convention). The **91st Amendment** capped the total ministers at **15 percent** of the Lok Sabha strength.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha?
1. To ratify the declaration of Emergency
2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers
3. To impeach the President of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha? 1. To ratify the declaration of Emergency 2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers 3. To impeach the President of India Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. Only the **Lok Sabha** can pass a **no-confidence motion**. Ratifying an emergency and impeaching the President are powers held by **both Houses**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements:
1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House.
2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements: 1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House. 2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The 10th Schedule allows a **nominated member** to join a party **within six months** (not prohibiting it). The law **does not specify a timeframe** for the presiding officer's decision.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Attorney General of India and Solicitor General of India are the only officers of the Government who are allowed to participate in the meetings of the Parliament of India.
2. According to the Constitution of India, the Attorney General of India submits his resignation when the Government which appointed him resigns.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Attorney General of India and Solicitor General of India are the only officers of the Government who are allowed to participate in the meetings of the Parliament of India. 2. According to the Constitution of India, the Attorney General of India submits his resignation when the Government which appointed him resigns. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. The **Solicitor General** is not allowed to participate in Parliament (only the AG is). Resignation of the AG upon government change is a **convention**, not mandated by the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to the writs issued by the Courts in India, consider the following statements:
1. Mandamus will not lie against a private organisation unless it is entrusted with a public duty.
2. Mandamus will not lie against a company even though it may be a Government company.
3. Any public minded person can be a petitioner to move the Court to obtain the writ of Quo Warranto.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the writs issued by the Courts in India, consider the following statements: 1. Mandamus will not lie against a private organisation unless it is entrusted with a public duty. 2. Mandamus will not lie against a company even though it may be a Government company. 3. Any public minded person can be a petitioner to move the Court to obtain the writ of Quo Warranto. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. **Mandamus** requires a public duty. It typically doesn't apply to ordinary commercial operations of a government company. **Quo Warranto** allows any public-spirited person to challenge someone holding a public office illegally.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:
1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix.
2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party.
3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings.
4. The well established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements: 1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix. 2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party. 3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings. 4. The well established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. The **Speaker fixes the date** for the Deputy Speaker's election. The Deputy Speaker has the **same powers as the Speaker** when presiding. Reserving the post for the opposition is a convention, not a "mandatory provision".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: We adopted parliamentary democracy based on the British model, but how does our model differ from that model?
1. As regards legislation, the British Parliament is supreme or sovereign but in India, the power of the Parliament to legislate is limited.
2. In India, matters related to the constitutionality of the Amendment of an Act of the Parliament are referred to the Constitution Bench by the Supreme Court.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: We adopted parliamentary democracy based on the British model, but how does our model differ from that model? 1. As regards legislation, the British Parliament is supreme or sovereign but in India, the power of the Parliament to legislate is limited. 2. In India, matters related to the constitutionality of the Amendment of an Act of the Parliament are referred to the Constitution Bench by the Supreme Court. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. India has a **written Constitution** limiting parliamentary sovereignty, unlike the UK. The **Supreme Court of India** reviews constitutionality via **Constitution Benches**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: With reference to the Union Government, consider the following statements:
1. N. Gopalaswamy lyengar Committee suggested that a minister and a secretary be designated solely to pursue and promote administrative reform.
2. In 1970, the Department of Personnel was constituted on the recommendation of the Administrative Reforms Commission, 1966, and this was placed under the Prime Minister's charge.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Union Government, consider the following statements: 1. N. Gopalaswamy lyengar Committee suggested that a minister and a secretary be designated solely to pursue and promote administrative reform. 2. In 1970, the Department of Personnel was constituted on the recommendation of the Administrative Reforms Commission, 1966, and this was placed under the Prime Minister's charge. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Administrative Reforms Commission (1966)** led to the creation of the **Department of Personnel** in 1970, placed under the Prime Minister.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: 'Right to Privacy' is protected under which Article of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: 'Right to Privacy' is protected under which Article of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 21**. The Supreme Court declared **Right to Privacy** a fundamental right rooted in the right to life and personal liberty under **Article 21**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. In India, there is no law restricting the candidates from contesting in one Lok Sabha election from three constituencies.
2. In 1991 Lok Sabha Election, Shri Devi Lal contested from three Lok Sabha constituencies.

3. As per the existing rules, if a candidate contests in one Lok Sabha election from many constituencies, his/her party should bear the cost of bye-elections to the constituencies vacated by him/her in the event of him/her winning in all the constituencies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. In India, there is no law restricting the candidates from contesting in one Lok Sabha election from three constituencies. 2. In 1991 Lok Sabha Election, Shri Devi Lal contested from three Lok Sabha constituencies. 3. As per the existing rules, if a candidate contests in one Lok Sabha election from many constituencies, his/her party should bear the cost of bye-elections to the constituencies vacated by him/her in the event of him/her winning in all the constituencies. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: The question was **Cancelled** by the UPSC. The Representation of the People Act currently restricts candidates from contesting more than **two constituencies**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. Judicial custody means an accused is in the custody of the concerned magistrate and such accused is locked up in a police station, not in jail.
2. During judicial custody, the police officer in charge of the case is not allowed to interrogate the suspect without the approval of the court.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Judicial custody means an accused is in the custody of the concerned magistrate and such accused is locked up in a police station, not in jail. 2. During judicial custody, the police officer in charge of the case is not allowed to interrogate the suspect without the approval of the court. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. In **judicial custody**, the accused is kept in **jail** (not a police station). The police **require court approval** to interrogate an individual in judicial custody.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. When a prisoner makes out a sufficient case, parole cannot be denied to such prisoner because it becomes a matter of his/her right.
2. State Governments have their own Prisoners Release on Parole Rules.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. When a prisoner makes out a sufficient case, parole cannot be denied to such prisoner because it becomes a matter of his/her right. 2. State Governments have their own Prisoners Release on Parole Rules. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. Parole is a **privilege**, not an absolute right. **State governments** manage prisons and have their own **parole rules**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: At the national level, which ministry is the nodal agency to ensure effective implementation of the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006?
questions.questionText: At the national level, which ministry is the nodal agency to ensure effective implementation of the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006?

options.a: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Ministry of Tribal Affairs**. This ministry serves as the nodal agency for the implementation of the **Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: A legislation which confers on the executive or administrative authority an unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power in the matter of application of law violates
which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: A legislation which confers on the executive or administrative authority an unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power in the matter of application of law violates which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 14**. Unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power leads to arbitrariness, which directly violates the principle of **equality before the law** guaranteed under **Article 14**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following in Indian polity is an essential feature that indicates that it is federal in character?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following in Indian polity is an essential feature that indicates that it is federal in character?

options.a: The independence of judiciary is safeguarded.
options.b: The Union Legislature has elected representatives from constituent units.
options.c: The Union Cabinet can have elected representatives from regional parties.
options.d: The Fundamental Rights are enforceable by Courts of Law.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The independence of judiciary is safeguarded**. An **independent judiciary** is essential in a federal system to arbitrate disputes between the Union and the States.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following best defines the term 'State'?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following best defines the term 'State'?

options.a: A community of persons permanently occupying a definite territory independent of external control and possessing an organized government
options.b: A politically organized people of a definite territory and possessing an authority to govern them, maintain law and order, protect their natural rights and safeguard their means of sustenance
options.c: A number of persons who have been living in a definite territory for a very long time with their own culture, tradition and government
options.d: A society permanently living in a definite territory with a central authority, an executive responsible to the central authority and an independent judiciary

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A community of persons permanently occupying a definite territory independent of external control and possessing an organized government**. The four essential elements of a State are **Population, Territory, Government, and Sovereignty**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to the Indian judiciary, consider the following statements:
1. Any retired judge of the Supreme Court of India can be called back to sit and act as a Supreme Court judge by the Chief Justice of India with prior permission of the President of India.
2. A High Court in India has the power to review its own judgement as the Supreme Court does.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Indian judiciary, consider the following statements: 1. Any retired judge of the Supreme Court of India can be called back to sit and act as a Supreme Court judge by the Chief Justice of India with prior permission of the President of India. 2. A High Court in India has the power to review its own judgement as the Supreme Court does. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. Under **Article 128**, the CJI can request a **retired SC judge** to sit, with the **President's prior consent**. *

Note: While HCs act as Courts of Record and possess inherent review powers, the formal statutory power of review is explicitly given to the SC under Article 137.*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. There is only one citizenship and one domicile.
2. A citizen by birth only can become the Head of State.
3. A foreigner once granted the citizenship cannot be deprived of it under any circumstances.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. There is only one citizenship and one domicile. 2. A citizen by birth only can become the Head of State. 3. A foreigner once granted the citizenship cannot be deprived of it under any circumstances. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. India recognizes **single citizenship and single domicile**. Unlike the US, a naturalized citizen in India **can become President**. Citizenship can be **deprived** under the Citizenship Act for fraud or disloyalty[cite: 6078, 6079, 6080, 6081, 6082, 6083, 6084, 6085, 6086, 6087].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following factors constitutes the best safeguard of liberty in a liberal democracy?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following factors constitutes the best safeguard of liberty in a liberal democracy?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Separation of powers**. The separation of powers ensures that the different branches of government check and balance each other, preventing the concentration of power and thus acting as a safeguard for liberty[cite: 6088, 6089, 6090, 6091, 6092, 6093].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Under the Indian Constitution, concentration of wealth violates
questions.questionText: Under the Indian Constitution, concentration of wealth violates

options.b: The Directive Principles of State Policy

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Directive Principles of State Policy**. Article 39(c) under the Directive Principles of State Policy states that the operation of the economic system must not result in the concentration of wealth and means of production to the common detriment[cite: 6094, 6095, 6096, 6097, 6098, 6100].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: What is the position of the Right to Property in India?
questions.questionText: What is the position of the Right to Property in India?

options.a: Legal right available to citizens only
options.b: Legal right available to any person
options.c: Fundamental Right available to citizens only
options.d: Neither Fundamental Right nor legal right

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Legal right available to any person**. The Right to Property was removed as a Fundamental Right by the 44th Amendment Act and is now a legal right under Article 300A, available to any person, not just citizens[cite: 6101, 6102, 6103, 6104, 6105, 6106].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: What was the exact constitutional status of India on 26th January, 1950?
questions.questionText: What was the exact constitutional status of India on 26th January, 1950?

options.d: A Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A Sovereign Democratic Republic**. On January 26, 1950, the Preamble declared India to be a "Sovereign Democratic Republic." The terms "Socialist" and "Secular" were added later by the 42nd Amendment in 1976[cite: 6107, 6108, 6109, 6110, 6111, 6112].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Constitutional government means
questions.questionText: Constitutional government means

options.a: A representative government of a nation with federal structure
options.b: A government whose Head enjoys nominal powers
options.c: A government whose Head enjoys real powers
options.d: A government limited by the terms of the Constitution

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **a government limited by the terms of the Constitution**. A constitutional government is defined as a limited government where the constitution limits the powers of the government to protect individual liberty[cite: 6113, 6114, 6115, 6116, 6117, 6119].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Rajya Sabha has equal powers with Lok Sabha in
questions.questionText: Rajya Sabha has equal powers with Lok Sabha in

options.a: The matter of creating new All India Services

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **amending the Constitution**. Both the Rajya Sabha and the Lok Sabha have equal powers when it comes to the introduction and passage of Constitutional Amendment Bills under Article 368[cite: 6123, 6124, 6125, 6126, 6127, 6128].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following categories of Fundamental Rights incorporates protection against untouchability as a form of discrimination?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following categories of Fundamental Rights incorporates protection against untouchability as a form of discrimination?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to Equality**. The abolition of untouchability is enshrined in Article 17, which falls under the broader category of the "Right to Equality" (Articles 14-18) in the Indian Constitution[cite: 6129, 6130, 6131, 6132, 6133, 6134, 6135].

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: In India, separation of judiciary from the executive is enjoined by
questions.questionText: In India, separation of judiciary from the executive is enjoined by

options.b: A Directive Principle of State Policy

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **a Directive Principle of State Policy**. Article 50 of the Constitution of India, which is a Directive Principle of State Policy, directs the State to take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State[cite: 6136, 6137, 6138, 6139, 6140, 6141].

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: Along with the Budget, the Finance Minister also places other documents before the Parliament which include "The Macro Economic Framework Statement". The aforesaid document is presented because this is mandated by
questions.questionText: Along with the Budget, the Finance Minister also places other documents before the Parliament which include "The Macro Economic Framework Statement". The aforesaid document is presented because this is mandated by

options.b: Article 112 and Article 110(1) of the Constitution of India
options.c: Article 113 of the Constitution of India
options.d: Provisions of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Provisions of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003**.
