questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: What will follow if a Money Bill is substantially amended by the Rajya Sabha?
questions.questionText: What will follow if a Money Bill is substantially amended by the Rajya Sabha?

options.a: The Lok Sabha may still proceed with the Bill, accepting or not accepting the recommendations of the Rajya Sabha
options.b: The Lok Sabha cannot consider the Bill further
options.c: The Lok Sabha may send the Bill to the Rajya Sabha for reconsideration
options.d: The President may call a joint sitting for passing the Bill

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Lok Sabha may still proceed with the Bill, accepting or not accepting the recommendations of the Rajya Sabha.** In the case of a **Money Bill**, the Rajya Sabha can only make non-binding recommendations within 14 days. The **Lok Sabha holds the ultimate power** to either accept or reject them.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: With reference to Indian History, the Members of the Constituent Assembly from the Provinces were:
questions.questionText: With reference to Indian History, the Members of the Constituent Assembly from the Provinces were:

options.a: Directly elected by the people of those Provinces
options.b: Nominated by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League
options.c: Elected by the Provincial Legislative Assemblies
options.d: Selected by the Government for their expertise in constitutional matters

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **elected by the Provincial Legislative Assemblies**. Members of the Constituent Assembly representing the Provinces were **indirectly elected** by the members of the lower houses of the **Provincial Legislative Assemblies** using a single transferable vote.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. An amendment to the Constitution of India can be initiated by an introduction of a bill in the Lok Sabha only.
2. If such an amendment seeks to make changes in the federal character of the Constitution, the amendment also requires to be ratified by the legislature of all the States of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. An amendment to the Constitution of India can be initiated by an introduction of a bill in the Lok Sabha only. 2. If such an amendment seeks to make changes in the federal character of the Constitution, the amendment also requires to be ratified by the legislature of all the States of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. A Constitution Amendment Bill can be initiated in **either House of Parliament** (Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha). If it affects federal features, it requires ratification by the legislatures of **not less than one-half** of the States (not "all" states).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
Attorney General of India can
1. take part in the proceedings of the Lok Sabha
2. be a member of a committee of the Lok Sabha
3. speak in the Lok Sabha
4. vote in the Lok Sabha
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: Attorney General of India can 1. take part in the proceedings of the Lok Sabha 2. be a member of a committee of the Lok Sabha 3. speak in the Lok Sabha 4. vote in the Lok Sabha Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Article 88**, the **Attorney General** has the right to **speak in**, **take part in the proceedings** of, and **be named a member of any committee** of Parliament. However, they are explicitly **not entitled to vote**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following bodies does not/do not find mention in the Constitution?
1. National Development Council
2. Planning Commission
3. Zonal Councils
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following bodies does not/do not find mention in the Constitution? 1. National Development Council 2. Planning Commission 3. Zonal Councils Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. None of these bodies find mention in the Constitution. The **NDC and Planning Commission** were executive bodies created by cabinet resolutions, while **Zonal Councils** are statutory bodies set up under the States Reorganisation Act, 1956.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: The Parliament can make any law for whole or any part of India for implementing international treaties
questions.questionText: The Parliament can make any law for whole or any part of India for implementing international treaties

options.a: With the consent of all the States
options.b: With the consent of the majority of States
options.c: With the consent of the States concerned
options.d: Without the consent of any State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **without the consent of any State**. Under **Article 253**, Parliament has the overarching power to make laws for the whole or any part of India for **implementing international treaties, agreements, or conventions** without needing the consent of any state.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: The Government enacted the Panchayat Extension to Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act in 1996.
Which one of the following is not identified as its objective?
questions.questionText: The Government enacted the Panchayat Extension to Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act in 1996. Which one of the following is not identified as its objective?

options.c: To create autonomous regions in tribal areas
options.d: To free tribal people from exploitation

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To create autonomous regions in tribal areas**. Creating **autonomous regions** is an objective primarily related to the **Sixth Schedule** areas (like in Assam, Meghalaya, etc.). **PESA** (applicable to Fifth Schedule areas) aims at providing self-governance, recognizing traditional rights, and stopping exploitation via Gram Sabhas.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Under the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006, who shall be the authority to initiate the process for determining the nature and extent of individual or community forest rights or both?
questions.questionText: Under the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006, who shall be the authority to initiate the process for determining the nature and extent of individual or community forest rights or both?

options.c: Tahsildar / Block Development Officer / Mandal Revenue Officer

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Gram Sabha**. Under the **Forest Rights Act, 2006**, the **Gram Sabha** is legally designated as the initial authority to initiate the process for determining the nature and extent of forest rights for individuals and communities.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: 'Economic Justice' as one of the objectives of the Indian Constitution has been provided in
questions.questionText: 'Economic Justice' as one of the objectives of the Indian Constitution has been provided in

options.b: The Preamble and the Directive Principles of State Policy
options.c: The Fundamental Rights and the Directive Principles of State Policy

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Preamble and the Directive Principles of State Policy**. The **Preamble** explicitly secures to all citizens "Justice, social, **economic** and political." Additionally, the **DPSP (Part IV)** elaborates heavily on policies necessary to achieve economic justice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: According to the Constitution of India, which of the following are fundamental for the governance of the country?
questions.questionText: According to the Constitution of India, which of the following are fundamental for the governance of the country?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Directive Principles of State Policy**. **Article 37** of the Constitution explicitly states that the **Directive Principles of State Policy** are "fundamental in the governance of the country," and it shall be the duty of the State to apply these principles in making laws.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements: The Parliamentary Committee on Public Accounts
1. consists of not more than 25 Members of the Lok Sabha
2. scrutinizes appropriation and finance accounts of the Government
3. examines the report of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: The Parliamentary Committee on Public Accounts 1. consists of not more than 25 Members of the Lok Sabha 2. scrutinizes appropriation and finance accounts of the Government 3. examines the report of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** scrutinizes appropriation accounts and examines the **CAG report**. However,

Statement 1 is incorrect because the PAC has a maximum of **22 members** (15 from Lok Sabha and 7 from Rajya Sabha), not 25.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: In the context of India, which of the following principles is/are implied institutionally in the parliamentary government?
1. Members of the Cabinet are Members of the Parliament.
2. Ministers hold the office till they enjoy confidence in the Parliament.
3. Cabinet is headed by the Head of the State.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.questionText: In the context of India, which of the following principles is/are implied institutionally in the parliamentary government? 1. Members of the Cabinet are Members of the Parliament. 2. Ministers hold the office till they enjoy confidence in the Parliament. 3. Cabinet is headed by the Head of the State. Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. In a Parliamentary government, **Ministers must be MPs** (or become so within 6 months), and they hold office due to **majority confidence** in the lower house.
Statement 3 is incorrect because the Cabinet is headed by the **Head of Government (Prime Minister)**, not the Head of State (President).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Council of Ministers in the Centre shall be collectively responsible to the Parliament.
2. The Union Ministers shall hold the office during the pleasure of the President of India.
3. The Prime Minister shall communicate to the President about the proposals for legislation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Council of Ministers in the Centre shall be collectively responsible to the Parliament. 2. The Union Ministers shall hold the office during the pleasure of the President of India. 3. The Prime Minister shall communicate to the President about the proposals for legislation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The Council of Ministers is **collectively responsible exclusively to the Lok Sabha** (House of the People), not the entire Parliament (making statement 1 technically incorrect). Ministers hold office during the **President's pleasure**, and the **PM must communicate** legislative proposals to the President under Article 78.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. National Development Council is an organ of the Planning Commission.
2. The Economic and Social Planning is kept in the Concurrent List in the Constitution of India.
3. The Constitution of India prescribes that Panchayats should be assigned the task of preparation of plans for economic development and social justice.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. National Development Council is an organ of the Planning Commission. 2. The Economic and Social Planning is kept in the Concurrent List in the Constitution of India. 3. The Constitution of India prescribes that Panchayats should be assigned the task of preparation of plans for economic development and social justice. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. **Economic and social planning** is placed in the **Concurrent List**. The Constitution (Article 243G) empowers **Panchayats** to prepare plans for local economic development. The **NDC is an independent body** (not an organ of the Planning Commission), though it interacts closely with it.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Chairman and the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha are not the members of that House.
2. While the nominated members of the two Houses of the Parliament have no voting right in the presidential election, they have the right to vote in the election of the Vice President.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Chairman and the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha are not the members of that House. 2. While the nominated members of the two Houses of the Parliament have no voting right in the presidential election, they have the right to vote in the election of the Vice President. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Chairman (Vice President)** is not a member of the Rajya Sabha, but the **Deputy Chairman** absolutely must be a member of the House. **Nominated members** do not vote in the President's election but **can vote** in the Vice President's election.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: With reference to National Legal Services Authority, consider the following statements:
1. Its objective is to provide free and competent legal services to the weaker sections of the society on the basis of equal opportunity.
2. It issues guidelines for the State Legal Services Authorities to implement the legal programmes and schemes throughout the country.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to National Legal Services Authority, consider the following statements: 1. Its objective is to provide free and competent legal services to the weaker sections of the society on the basis of equal opportunity. 2. It issues guidelines for the State Legal Services Authorities to implement the legal programmes and schemes throughout the country. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. **NALSA** is constituted under the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987, to ensure that **free and competent legal services** are provided to weaker sections, and it **lays down binding policies/guidelines** for State Authorities to implement effectively.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following constitute the National Development Council?
1. The Prime Minister
2. The Chairman, Finance Commission
3. Ministers of the Union Cabinet
4. Chief Ministers of the States
Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.questionText: Who among the following constitute the National Development Council? 1. The Prime Minister 2. The Chairman, Finance Commission 3. Ministers of the Union Cabinet 4. Chief Ministers of the States Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4 only**. The **National Development Council (NDC)** typically comprises the **Prime Minister** (Chairman), all **Chief Ministers**, administrators of UTs, all **Cabinet Ministers**, and members of the Planning Commission. The **Chairman of the Finance Commission is not** a member.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: In the areas covered under the Panchayat Extension to the Scheduled Areas Act, 1996, what is the role/power of Gram Sabha?
1. Gram Sabha has the power to prevent alienation of land in the Scheduled Areas.
2. Gram Sabha has the ownership of minor forest produce.
3. Recommendation of Gram Sabha is required for granting prospecting licence or mining lease, for any mineral in the Scheduled Areas.
Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: In the areas covered under the Panchayat Extension to the Scheduled Areas Act, 1996, what is the role/power of Gram Sabha? 1. Gram Sabha has the power to prevent alienation of land in the Scheduled Areas. 2. Gram Sabha has the ownership of minor forest produce. 3. Recommendation of Gram Sabha is required for granting prospecting licence or mining lease, for any mineral in the Scheduled Areas. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. Under **PESA**, the **Gram Sabha** explicitly has power to **prevent land alienation** and holds **ownership of minor forest produce**. Regarding minerals, recommendations are required only for "minor minerals", not "any mineral" overall. *(

Note: official keys often debated this, but 'b' is the marked correct answer as per the provided text).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: In the Parliament of India, the purpose of an adjournment motion is
questions.questionText: In the Parliament of India, the purpose of an adjournment motion is

options.a: To allow a discussion on a definite matter of urgent public importance
options.b: To let opposition members collect information from the ministers
options.c: To allow a reduction of specife amount in demand for grant
options.d: To postpone the proceedings to check the inappropriate or violent behaviour on the part of some members

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **to allow a discussion on a definite matter of urgent public importance**. An **Adjournment Motion** is an extraordinary parliamentary device introduced to set aside the regular business of the House to discuss a **specific and urgent matter of profound public importance**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Consider the following provisions under the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in the Constitution of India
1. Securing for citizens of India a uniform civil code
2. Organizing village Panchayats
3. Promoting cottage industries in rural areas
4. Securing for all the workers reasonable leisure and cultural opportunities
Which of the above are the Gandhian Principles that are reflected in the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.questionText: Consider the following provisions under the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in the Constitution of India 1. Securing for citizens of India a uniform civil code 2. Organizing village Panchayats 3. Promoting cottage industries in rural areas 4. Securing for all the workers reasonable leisure and cultural opportunities Which of the above are the Gandhian Principles that are reflected in the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The **Gandhian principles** incorporated into the DPSP directly include **organizing village Panchayats (Article 40)** and **promoting cottage industries (Article 43)**. The UCC (Article 44) is a Liberal-Intellectual principle, and securing leisure (Article 43) represents a Socialistic principle.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Union Territories are not represented in the Rajya Sabha
2. It is within the purview of the Chief Election Commissioner to adjudicate the election disputes.
3. According to the Constitution of India, the Parliament consists of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only.
Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Union Territories are not represented in the Rajya Sabha 2. It is within the purview of the Chief Election Commissioner to adjudicate the election disputes. 3. According to the Constitution of India, the Parliament consists of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **None**. Certain UTs (like Delhi, Puducherry) **are represented** in the Rajya Sabha. Election disputes are adjudicated by the **High Courts**, not the CEC. The Parliament constitutionally consists of the **President**, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Regarding the office of the Lok Sabha Speaker, consider the following statements
1. He/She holds the office during the pleasure of the President.
2. He/She need not be a member of the House at the time of his/her election but has to become a member of the House within six months from the date of his/her election.
3. If he/she intends to resign, the letter of his/her resignation has to be addressed to the Deputy Speaker.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Regarding the office of the Lok Sabha Speaker, consider the following statements 1. He/She holds the office during the pleasure of the President. 2. He/She need not be a member of the House at the time of his/her election but has to become a member of the House within six months from the date of his/her election. 3. If he/she intends to resign, the letter of his/her resignation has to be addressed to the Deputy Speaker. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. The Speaker does **not** hold office at the pleasure of the President (they can only be removed by an effective majority resolution). The Speaker **must absolutely be a member** of the House prior to election. If they resign, the letter must be given to the **Deputy Speaker**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following are included in the original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court?
1. A dispute between the Government of India and one or more States
2. A dispute regarding elections to either House of Parliament or that of Legislature of a State
3. A dispute between the Government of India and a Union Territory
4. A dispute between two or more States
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following are included in the original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court? 1. A dispute between the Government of India and one or more States 2. A dispute regarding elections to either House of Parliament or that of Legislature of a State 3. A dispute between the Government of India and a Union Territory 4. A dispute between two or more States Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 4**. Under **Article 131**, the Supreme Court holds **original jurisdiction** strictly in disputes involving federal parties: **Government of India vs. States** and **States vs. States**. Election disputes are handled by High Courts, and UTs are not covered under original federal dispute jurisdiction.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Human Resource Development
statementLines: With reference to National Rural Health Mission, which of folowing are the jobs of ASHA, a trained community health worker?
1. Accompanying women to the health facility for antenatal care checkup
2. Using pregnancy test kits for early detection of pregnancy
3. Providing information on nutrition and immunization
4. Conducting the delivery of baby
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: With reference to National Rural Health Mission, which of folowing are the jobs of ASHA, a trained community health worker? 1. Accompanying women to the health facility for antenatal care checkup 2. Using pregnancy test kits for early detection of pregnancy 3. Providing information on nutrition and immunization 4. Conducting the delivery of baby Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Under NRHM, an **ASHA worker** functions as a community mobilizer and health educator. She assists with **antenatal care, pregnancy tests, and nutrition/immunization info**, but she is **not clinically trained to independently conduct deliveries**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following special powers have been conferred on the Rajya Sabha by the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which of the following special powers have been conferred on the Rajya Sabha by the Constitution of India?

options.a: To change the existing territory of a State and to change the name of a State
options.b: To pass a resolution empowering the Parliament to make laws in the State List and to create one or more All India Services
options.c: To amend the election procedure of the President and ot determine the pension of the President after his/her retirement
options.d: To determine the functions of the Election Commission and to determine the number of Election Commissioners

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To pass a resolution empowering the Parliament to make laws in the State List and to create one or more All India Services**. The **Rajya Sabha** possesses exclusive powers under **Article 249** (legislating on State List items) and **Article 312** (authorizing the creation of new All India Services).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following are the methods of Parliamentary control over public finance in India?
1. Placing Annual Financial Statement before the Parliament
2. Withdrawal of moneys from Consolidated Fund of India only after passing the Appropriation Bill
3. Provisions of supplementary grants and vote-on-account
4. A periodic or at least a mid-year review of programme of the Government against macroeconomic forecasts and expenditure by a Parliamentary Budget Office
5. Introducing Finance Bill in the Parliament
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the methods of Parliamentary control over public finance in India? 1. Placing Annual Financial Statement before the Parliament 2. Withdrawal of moneys from Consolidated Fund of India only after passing the Appropriation Bill 3. Provisions of supplementary grants and vote-on-account 4. A periodic or at least a mid-year review of programme of the Government against macroeconomic forecasts and expenditure by a Parliamentary Budget Office 5. Introducing Finance Bill in the Parliament Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 5 only**. India **does not have a statutory Parliamentary Budget Office** providing mid-year reviews as seen in some other countries. All other listed procedures (Annual Financial Statement, Appropriation Bill, Supplementary Grants, and Finance Bill) are established constitutional methods of financial control.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India have a bearing on Education?
1. Directive Principles of State Policy
2. Rural and Urban Local Bodies
3. Fifth Schedule
4. Sixth Schedule
5. Seventh Schedule
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India have a bearing on Education? 1. Directive Principles of State Policy 2. Rural and Urban Local Bodies 3. Fifth Schedule 4. Sixth Schedule 5. Seventh Schedule Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3, 4 and 5**. Education is comprehensively touched upon by the Constitution: **DPSP** (Article 45), local bodies (11th & 12th schedules mention education), **Fifth/Sixth Schedules** empower local councils regarding education/schools in tribal areas, and the **Seventh Schedule** explicitly places education in the Concurrent List.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: In India, other than ensuring that public funds are used efficiently and for intended purpose, what is the importance of the office of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG)?
1. CAG exercises exchequer control on behalf of the Parliament when the President of India declares national emergency/financial emergency.
2. CAG reports on the execution of projects or programmes by the ministries are discussed by the Public Accounts Committee.
3. Information from CAG reports can be used by investigating agencies to press charges against those who have violated the law while managing public finances.
4. While dealing with the audit and accounting of government companies, CAG has certain judicial powers for prosecuting those who violate the law.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: In India, other than ensuring that public funds are used efficiently and for intended purpose, what is the importance of the office of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG)? 1. CAG exercises exchequer control on behalf of the Parliament when the President of India declares national emergency/financial emergency. 2. CAG reports on the execution of projects or programmes by the ministries are discussed by the Public Accounts Committee. 3. Information from CAG reports can be used by investigating agencies to press charges against those who have violated the law while managing public finances. 4. While dealing with the audit and accounting of government companies, CAG has certain judicial powers for prosecuting those who violate the law. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The **CAG audits** accounts, but the office **does not exercise exchequer control** (i.e., money can be withdrawn without CAG approval). The CAG **does not have judicial or prosecution powers**. The CAG's reports are indeed scrutinized by the **PAC** and can highlight irregularities used by investigation agencies.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: The Prime Minister of India, at the time of his/her appointment
questions.questionText: The Prime Minister of India, at the time of his/her appointment

options.a: Need not necessarily be a member of one of the Houses of the Parliament but must become a member of one of the Houses within six months
options.b: Need not necessarily be a member of one of the Houses of the Parliament but must become a member of the Lok Sabha within six months
options.c: Must be a member of one of the Houses of the Parliament
options.d: Must be a member of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **need not necessarily be a member of one of the Houses of the Parliament but must become a member of one of the Houses within six months**. Under the Constitution, a non-member can be appointed Prime Minister (or Minister) provided they secure **membership in either the Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha within a six-month period**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: With reference to the Delimitation Commission, consider the following statements:
1. The orders of the Delimitation Commission cannot be challenged in a Court of Law.
2. When the orders of the Delimitation Commission are laid before the Lok Sabha or State Legislative Assembly, they cannot effect any modifications in the orders.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Delimitation Commission, consider the following statements: 1. The orders of the Delimitation Commission cannot be challenged in a Court of Law. 2. When the orders of the Delimitation Commission are laid before the Lok Sabha or State Legislative Assembly, they cannot effect any modifications in the orders. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. To prevent election delays, the Constitution ensures that the orders of the **Delimitation Commission** have the force of law and **cannot be challenged in court**. Although laid before Parliament/Assemblies, the legislatures **cannot modify** the commission's orders.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: According to the Constitution of India, it is the duty of the President of India to cause to be laid before the Parliament which of the following?
1. The Recommendations of the Union Finance Commission
2. The Report of the Public Accounts Committee
3. The Report of the Comptroller and Auditor General
4. The Report of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: According to the Constitution of India, it is the duty of the President of India to cause to be laid before the Parliament which of the following? 1. The Recommendations of the Union Finance Commission 2. The Report of the Public Accounts Committee 3. The Report of the Comptroller and Auditor General 4. The Report of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4 only**. The **President** causes the reports of the **Finance Commission, CAG, and National Commission for SCs** to be laid before Parliament. The **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** submits its report directly to the **Speaker of the Lok Sabha**, not through the President.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A deadlock between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha calls for a joint sitting of the Parliament during the passage of
1. Ordinary Legislation
2. Money Bill
3. Constitution Amendment Bill
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: A deadlock between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha calls for a joint sitting of the Parliament during the passage of 1. Ordinary Legislation 2. Money Bill 3. Constitution Amendment Bill Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. A **Joint Sitting (Article 108)** can only be summoned to resolve a deadlock over an **Ordinary Bill**. It is not applicable to a **Money Bill** (Lok Sabha has overriding powers) or a **Constitution Amendment Bill** (must be passed by both houses separately).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following is/are among the Fundamental Duties of citizens laid down in the Indian Constitution?
1. To preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture
2. To protect the weaker sections from social injustice
3. To develop the scientific temper and spirit of inquiry
4. To strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are among the Fundamental Duties of citizens laid down in the Indian Constitution? 1. To preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture 2. To protect the weaker sections from social injustice 3. To develop the scientific temper and spirit of inquiry 4. To strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4 only**. Protecting the weaker sections from social injustice is a **Directive Principle of State Policy (Article 46)**, not a Fundamental Duty of citizens. The other three are explicit **Fundamental Duties** under **Article 51A**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: What is the provision to safeguard the autonomy of the Supreme Court of India?
1. While appointing the Supreme Court Judges, the President of India has to consult the Chief Justice of India.
2. The Supreme Court Judges can be removed by the Chief Justice of India only.
3. The salaries of the Judges are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India to which the legislature does not have to vote.
4. All appointments of officers and staffs of the Supreme Court of India are made by the Government only after consulting the Chief Justice of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: What is the provision to safeguard the autonomy of the Supreme Court of India? 1. While appointing the Supreme Court Judges, the President of India has to consult the Chief Justice of India. 2. The Supreme Court Judges can be removed by the Chief Justice of India only. 3. The salaries of the Judges are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India to which the legislature does not have to vote. 4. All appointments of officers and staffs of the Supreme Court of India are made by the Government only after consulting the Chief Justice of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. The **President must consult the CJI** for appointments, and salaries are **charged on the Consolidated Fund**. Judges are removed by the **President upon a parliamentary resolution**, not the CJI. Appointments of court staff are made by the **CJI independently**, not by the government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: The Constitution (Seventy-Third Amendment) Act 1992, which aims at promoting the Panchayati Raj Institutions in the country, provides for which of the following?
1. Constitution of District Planning Committees.
2. State Election Commissions to conduct all panchayat elections.
3. Establishment of State Finance Commissions.
Select the correct answer using codes given below:
questions.questionText: The Constitution (Seventy-Third Amendment) Act 1992, which aims at promoting the Panchayati Raj Institutions in the country, provides for which of the following? 1. Constitution of District Planning Committees. 2. State Election Commissions to conduct all panchayat elections. 3. Establishment of State Finance Commissions. Select the correct answer using codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The **73rd Amendment** mandated the establishment of **State Election Commissions** and **State Finance Commissions**. The **District Planning Committees (DPCs)** were mandated by the **74th Amendment Act** (Municipalities).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: In India, if a religious sect/community is given the status of a national minority, what special advantages it is entitled to?
1. It can establish and administer exclusive educational institutions.
2. The President of India automatically nominates a representative of the community to Lok Sabha.
3. It can derive benefits from the Prime Minister's 15 Point Programme.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: In India, if a religious sect/community is given the status of a national minority, what special advantages it is entitled to? 1. It can establish and administer exclusive educational institutions. 2. The President of India automatically nominates a representative of the community to Lok Sabha. 3. It can derive benefits from the Prime Minister's 15 Point Programme. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. Minorities have the right to establish **educational institutions under Article 30**, and they benefit from the **PM's 15 Point Programme for Minorities**. There is **no provision** for the President to automatically nominate religious minority representatives to the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: India is home to lakhs of persons with disabilities. What are the benefits available to them under the law?
1. Free schooling till the age of 18 years in government-run schools.
2. Preferential allotment of land for setting up the business.
3. Ramps in public buildings.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: India is home to lakhs of persons with disabilities. What are the benefits available to them under the law? 1. Free schooling till the age of 18 years in government-run schools. 2. Preferential allotment of land for setting up the business. 3. Ramps in public buildings. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under the Persons with Disabilities (Equal Opportunities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act, 1995 (and later legislation), PwD are entitled to **free education up to 18 years**, **preferential land allotment**, and a barrier-free environment like **ramps in public buildings**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The authorization for the withdrawal of funds from the Consolidated Fund of India must come from
questions.questionText: The authorization for the withdrawal of funds from the Consolidated Fund of India must come from
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Parliament of India**. Under **Article 114**, no money can be withdrawn from the **Consolidated Fund of India** except under appropriation made by law passed by the **Parliament**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: All revenues received by the Union Government by way of taxes and other receipts for the conduct of Government business are credited to the
questions.questionText: All revenues received by the Union Government by way of taxes and other receipts for the conduct of Government business are credited to the
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Consolidated Fund of India**. According to **Article 266**, all revenues received, loans raised, and moneys received in repayment of loans by the Union Government are credited to the **Consolidated Fund of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Among the following, who are eligible to benefit from the "Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act"?
questions.questionText: Among the following, who are eligible to benefit from the "Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act"?

options.a: Adult members of only the scheduled caste and scheduled tribe households
options.b: Adult members of below poverty line (BPL) households
options.c: Adult members of households of all backward communities

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Adult members of any household**. MGNREGA provides a legal guarantee for 100 days of employment to **adult members of any rural household** willing to do public work-related unskilled manual labour, regardless of their APL/BPL status.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: When the annual Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha,
questions.questionText: When the annual Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha,

options.a: The Budget is modified and presented again
options.b: The Budget is referred to the Rajya Sabha for suggestions
options.c: The Union Finance Minister is asked to resign
options.d: The Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers**. The defeat of the Union Budget in the Lok Sabha implies a **loss of confidence** in the government, necessitating the **resignation of the entire Council of Ministers**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Under the Constitution of India,
which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?
questions.questionText: Under the Constitution of India, which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?

options.d: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To vote in public elections**. While the Swaran Singh Committee recommended making voting a duty, it was **not included** in the list of **Fundamental Duties under Article 51A**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: With reference to the Finance Commission of India, which of the following statements is correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Finance Commission of India, which of the following statements is correct?

options.a: It encourages the inflow of foreign capital for infrastructure development
options.b: It facilitates the proper distribution of finances among the Public Sector Undertakings
options.c: It ensures transparency in financial administration
options.d: None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is correct in this context

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is correct in this context**. The primary function of the **Finance Commission (Article 280)** is to recommend the **distribution of net proceeds of taxes** between the Union and the States, not managing foreign capital, PSUs, or administrative transparency.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following:
1. Right to education.
2. Right to equal access to public service.
3. Right to food.
Which of the above is/are Human Right/Human Rights under "Universal Declaration of Human Rights"?
questions.questionText: Consider the following: 1. Right to education. 2. Right to equal access to public service. 3. Right to food. Which of the above is/are Human Right/Human Rights under "Universal Declaration of Human Rights"?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR)** recognizes the **Right to Education (Article 26)**, **Right to equal access to public service (Article 21)**, and **Right to an adequate standard of living, including food (Article 25)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Consider the following statements: In India, a Metropolitan Planning Committee
1. is constituted under the provisions of the Constitution of India.
2. prepares the draft development plans for metropolitan area.
3. has the sole responsibility for implementing Goverment sponsored schemes in the metropolitan area.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: In India, a Metropolitan Planning Committee 1. is constituted under the provisions of the Constitution of India. 2. prepares the draft development plans for metropolitan area. 3. has the sole responsibility for implementing Goverment sponsored schemes in the metropolitan area. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. A **Metropolitan Planning Committee (MPC)** is a constitutional body under **Article 243ZE** responsible for preparing **draft development plans**. However, the **implementation** of schemes is carried out by respective municipalities, parastatals, and government departments, not solely by the MPC.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: What is the difference between "vote-on-account" and "interim budget"?
1. The provision of a "vote-on-account" is used by a regular Government, while an "interim budget" is a provision used by a caretaker Government.
2. A "vote-on-account" only deals with the expenditure in the government's budget, while an "interim budget" includes both expenditure and receipts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: What is the difference between "vote-on-account" and "interim budget"? 1. The provision of a "vote-on-account" is used by a regular Government, while an "interim budget" is a provision used by a caretaker Government. 2. A "vote-on-account" only deals with the expenditure in the government's budget, while an "interim budget" includes both expenditure and receipts. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. A **vote-on-account** deals exclusively with the **expenditure side** to keep the government functioning until the budget is passed. An **interim budget** is a complete set of accounts, including both **expenditure and receipts**. Statement 1 is wrong because a regular government can also present an interim budget in an election year.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which of the following Constitution Amendment Acts introduced GST (Goods and Services Tax) in course of inter-state trade or commerce?
questions.questionText: Which of the following Constitution Amendment Acts introduced GST (Goods and Services Tax) in course of inter-state trade or commerce?

options.a: Constitution (One Hundred and First Amendment) Act
options.b: Constitution (One Hundred and Second Amendment) Act
options.c: Constitution (One Hundred and Fourth Amendment) Act
options.d: Constitution (One Hundred and Sixth Amendment) Act

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Constitution (One Hundred and First Amendment) Act**. The **101st Constitutional Amendment Act (2016)** introduced the **Goods and Services Tax (GST)** in India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements about Central Vigilance Commission (CVC):
1. Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.
2. The Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner has the Minister of Home Affairs as one of the members.
3. In certain circumstances the Leader of the single largest group in opposition in Lok Sabha can be on the Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner.
How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about Central Vigilance Commission (CVC): 1. Central Vigilance Commissioner is appointed by the President of India. 2. The Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner has the Minister of Home Affairs as one of the members. 3. In certain circumstances the Leader of the single largest group in opposition in Lok Sabha can be on the Committee to recommend the appointment of Central Vigilance Commissioner. How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3**. All statements are correct. The **President appoints** the CVC on the recommendation of a committee consisting of the **Prime Minister, the Home Minister, and the Leader of the Opposition** (or the leader of the single largest opposition party) in the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the NITI Aayog is/are correct?
1. Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of its Governing Council.
2. Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh is a member of its Governing Council.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the NITI Aayog is/are correct? 1. Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of its Governing Council. 2. Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh is a member of its Governing Council. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **Prime Minister is the Chairperson** of NITI Aayog. Its Governing Council comprises all Chief Ministers of States/UTs with legislatures and **Lt. Governors of other UTs** (like Ladakh).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements about National Commission for Women are correct?
1. It is a statutory body.
2. It has the mandate to review the constitutional and legal safeguards for women.
3. It has the mandate to advise the Government on all policy matters affecting women.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about National Commission for Women are correct? 1. It is a statutory body. 2. It has the mandate to review the constitutional and legal safeguards for women. 3. It has the mandate to advise the Government on all policy matters affecting women. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **National Commission for Women (NCW)** was set up as a **statutory body** in 1992 under the NCW Act. Its mandate includes **reviewing constitutional/legal safeguards** and **advising the government on policy matters** affecting women.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVII: Official Language
statementLines: Which, amongst the following, is the latest language to be included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which, amongst the following, is the latest language to be included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Maithili**. Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali were added by the **71st Amendment Act (1992)**. Maithili (along with Bodo, Dogri, and Santhali) was added later by the **92nd Amendment Act (2003)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following states has the highest number of seats in the Council of States?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following states has the highest number of seats in the Council of States?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Tamil Nadu**. In the **Rajya Sabha (Council of States)**, Tamil Nadu holds **18 seats**, which is the highest among the given options (Bihar has 16, MP has 11, AP has 11).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct as per the Constitution of India?
1. The Constitution (Eighty-sixth Amendment) Act has added the Right to Education to the chapter of Directive Principles of State Policy.
2. Right to Work and Right to Education are both, Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct as per the Constitution of India? 1. The Constitution (Eighty-sixth Amendment) Act has added the Right to Education to the chapter of Directive Principles of State Policy. 2. Right to Work and Right to Education are both, Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. The 86th Amendment added Article 21A (**Fundamental Right** to education), and modified Article 45 (which was *already* in the DPSP, but altered its subject matter to early childhood care). The **Right to Work (Article 41)** is only a DPSP, not a Fundamental Right.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: As defined in Article 12 of the Constitution of India, 'State' includes:
1. Parliament of India
2. State Governments
3. State Legislatures
4. Local authorities in India Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: As defined in Article 12 of the Constitution of India, 'State' includes: 1. Parliament of India 2. State Governments 3. State Legislatures 4. Local authorities in India Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. According to **Article 12**, the 'State' includes the Government and **Parliament of India**, Government and **State Legislatures**, and all **local or other authorities** within the territory of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following statements about an adjournment motion in the Parliament of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements about an adjournment motion in the Parliament of India is not correct?

options.a: Its objective is to draw attention of the House to any recent matter of urgent public importance.
options.b: Adjournment motion is an extraordinary procedure.
options.c: This motion can be invoked on any matter of urgent importance that may even be within the jurisdiction of a State Government.
options.d: The Chair has absolute discretion to refuse consent.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **This motion can be invoked on any matter of urgent importance that may even be within the jurisdiction of a State Government.** An adjournment motion **cannot be framed on matters falling under the jurisdiction of a State Government**; it must exclusively relate to the direct responsibilities of the Union Government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. First Law Commission recommended Indian Penal Code, 1860.
2. Indian Penal Code, 1860 has been completely replaced by the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. First Law Commission recommended Indian Penal Code, 1860. 2. Indian Penal Code, 1860 has been completely replaced by the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023. Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **First Law Commission** (chaired by Lord Macaulay) drafted the **Indian Penal Code (IPC) 1860**. The IPC has recently been entirely replaced by the new criminal code, the **Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements with regard to the setting up of the Backward Class Commission in India:
1. The First Backward Class Commission, set up by the Government of India, is popularly known as Kaka Kalelkar Commission.
2. The Third Backward Class Commission, set up by the Government of India, is popularly known as Mandal Commission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements with regard to the setting up of the Backward Class Commission in India: 1. The First Backward Class Commission, set up by the Government of India, is popularly known as Kaka Kalelkar Commission. 2. The Third Backward Class Commission, set up by the Government of India, is popularly known as Mandal Commission. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. The **First Backward Class Commission** was the **Kaka Kalelkar Commission (1953)**. The **Mandal Commission (1979)** was the **Second** Backward Class Commission, not the Third.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one amongst the following cannot be a feature of a 'totalitarian regime'?
questions.questionText: Which one amongst the following cannot be a feature of a 'totalitarian regime'?

options.a: State control of the political and often personal realms of individual life
options.b: A monopoly of the means of mass communication

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Autonomy of civil society**. A totalitarian regime seeks to control all aspects of public and private life, effectively **crushing the autonomy of civil society**. Features like a one-party state and mass communication monopoly are hallmark characteristics.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by the action of a government authority.

2. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by not only the action of a government authority, but also by its inaction.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by the action of a government authority. 2. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by not only the action of a government authority, but also by its inaction. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Fundamental Rights under **Part III** protect individuals against arbitrary **actions** of the State, and constitutional courts can also enforce these rights by issuing writs to remedy the State's **inaction** (failure to perform a public duty) that violates these rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements with respect to the right to have a legal aid is/are correct?
1. Under the Constitution of India, it is given under the Directive Principles of State Policy.
2. It is the duty of the police to inform the nearest Legal Aid Committee immediately after the arrest of a person.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with respect to the right to have a legal aid is/are correct? 1. Under the Constitution of India, it is given under the Directive Principles of State Policy. 2. It is the duty of the police to inform the nearest Legal Aid Committee immediately after the arrest of a person. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. **Free legal aid** is explicitly directed under **Article 39A (DPSP)**. Under Supreme Court guidelines and criminal jurisprudence, it is the duty of the police/magistrate to inform arrested individuals of their right to legal aid and notify the legal aid committee.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. In India, the Special Marriage Act protects an individual if one enters into an interreligious marriage.
2. Right to marry a person of one's choice is an integral aspect of one's Fundamental Rights under Article 21 of the Constitution of India.
3. The Universal Declaration of Human Rights also resonates marital rights of every adult consenting individuals.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. In India, the Special Marriage Act protects an individual if one enters into an interreligious marriage. 2. Right to marry a person of one's choice is an integral aspect of one's Fundamental Rights under Article 21 of the Constitution of India. 3. The Universal Declaration of Human Rights also resonates marital rights of every adult consenting individuals. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Special Marriage Act** provides a civil framework for interfaith marriages. The Supreme Court has affirmed the **right to marry a person of one's choice** under **Article 21 (Hadiya case)**. The **UDHR (Article 16)** also protects the right to marry for consenting adults.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding GST is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding GST is not correct?

options.a: Amendment 115 to the Constitution of India kept alcohol for human use and five petroleum products outside the ambit of GST.
options.b: Amendment 122 to the Constitution of India kept only alcohol for human use outside the ambit of GST.
options.c: Precious metals are taxed at a rate of 1% under GST.
options.d: Unworked diamond is taxed at a rate of 0.25%.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Precious metals are taxed at a rate of 1% under GST.** Under the GST structure, precious metals like gold and silver are generally taxed at a special rate of **3%**, not 1%.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Human Resource Development
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the Report of Tendulkar Committee (2009) on poverty estimates is/are correct?
1. The Committee had used an all-India urban poverty line basket as a reference to derive both rural and urban poverty levels.
2. The Committee had anchored the poverty line to the official food calorie norms.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the Report of Tendulkar Committee (2009) on poverty estimates is/are correct? 1. The Committee had used an all-India urban poverty line basket as a reference to derive both rural and urban poverty levels. 2. The Committee had anchored the poverty line to the official food calorie norms. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. The **Tendulkar Committee** moved away from the outdated calorie-based anchoring (making statement 2 incorrect) and instead used a uniform **all-India urban poverty line basket** to compute poverty levels for both rural and urban areas.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one among the following is not under the jurisdiction of the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not under the jurisdiction of the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Human Rights Council**. The **Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993** provides for the constitution of the **NHRC, SHRCs, and Human Rights Courts** in districts. The "Human Rights Council" is a UN body, not a statutory creation under this Indian Act.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: According to B. R. Ambedkar, which one among the following Articles is the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: According to B. R. Ambedkar, which one among the following Articles is the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 32**. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described **Article 32** (Right to Constitutional Remedies) as the very soul of the Constitution and its very heart, without which the Constitution would be a nullity.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Who among the following filed the Writ Petition that led to the famous verdict of the Supreme Court of India recognising the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right?
questions.questionText: Who among the following filed the Writ Petition that led to the famous verdict of the Supreme Court of India recognising the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Justice K. S. Puttaswamy**. The landmark 2017 Supreme Court judgment unanimously declaring the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right under Article 21 was delivered in the case of **Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs. Union of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Through the Forty Second Amendment to the Constitution of India, which of the following was/were inserted into its Preamble?
1. 'Liberty of thought, expression and belief' was substituted by Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship'.
2. The expression 'Unity of the Nation' was substituted by 'Unity and Integrity of the Nation'.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Through the Forty Second Amendment to the Constitution of India, which of the following was/were inserted into its Preamble? 1. 'Liberty of thought, expression and belief' was substituted by Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship'. 2. The expression 'Unity of the Nation' was substituted by 'Unity and Integrity of the Nation'. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The 42nd Amendment added "Socialist", "Secular", and changed "Unity of the Nation" to **"Unity and Integrity of the Nation"**. Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith, and worship was **already present in the original Preamble**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: As per the State List under the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India, the States have jurisdiction over agricultural land in connection with which of the following subjects?
1. Taxes on agricultural income
2. Duties in respect of succession of agricultural land
3. Estate duty in respect of agricultural land
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: As per the State List under the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India, the States have jurisdiction over agricultural land in connection with which of the following subjects? 1. Taxes on agricultural income 2. Duties in respect of succession of agricultural land 3. Estate duty in respect of agricultural land Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **State List (List II)** of the Seventh Schedule exclusively grants States the power to levy taxes on **agricultural income**, **estate duty in respect of agricultural land**, and duties in respect of **succession to agricultural land**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which among the following is/are the objective/objectives of the NITI Aayog?
1. Imposing policies on the States/UTs
2. Allocation of funds at National and State levels
3. Design strategies and long-term policies and programme frameworks
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which among the following is/are the objective/objectives of the NITI Aayog? 1. Imposing policies on the States/UTs 2. Allocation of funds at National and State levels 3. Design strategies and long-term policies and programme frameworks Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. **NITI Aayog** serves as a think-tank to **design long-term strategic policies** and foster cooperative federalism. Unlike the erstwhile Planning Commission, it **does not have the power to allocate funds** or unilaterally impose policies on states.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following conditions is/are necessary for the issue of a writ of certiorari in India?
1. There should be a tribunal or an officer having legal authority to determine questions affecting rights of subjects and having a duty to act judicially.
2. Such tribunal or officer has acted without jurisdiction.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following conditions is/are necessary for the issue of a writ of certiorari in India? 1. There should be a tribunal or an officer having legal authority to determine questions affecting rights of subjects and having a duty to act judicially. 2. Such tribunal or officer has acted without jurisdiction. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. A writ of **Certiorari** is issued by a higher court to a lower judicial or quasi-judicial body (tribunal) to quash its order if it has **acted without jurisdiction** or exceeded its jurisdictional authority.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Who among the following Chief Justices of India ordered the constitution of a Special Bench called 'Social Justice Bench'?
questions.questionText: Who among the following Chief Justices of India ordered the constitution of a Special Bench called 'Social Justice Bench'?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Justice H. L. Dattu**. Former Chief Justice of India **H. L. Dattu** constituted the 'Social Justice Bench' in 2014 to exclusively hear cases concerning socially marginalized groups and ensure speedy delivery of justice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one among the following Departments/Authorities is not under the Ministry of Home Affairs?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following Departments/Authorities is not under the Ministry of Home Affairs?

options.c: The National Authority Chemical Weapons Convention
options.d: The Department of Jammu, Kashmir and Ladakh Affairs

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The National Authority Chemical Weapons Convention**. This authority operates under the administrative control of the **Cabinet Secretariat**, not the Ministry of Home Affairs.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Who among the following is given discretionary powers under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India to set up a Tribes Advisory Council in a State which has Scheduled Tribes but not Scheduled Areas?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is given discretionary powers under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India to set up a Tribes Advisory Council in a State which has Scheduled Tribes but not Scheduled Areas?

options.a: The Governor of the concerned State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The President of India**. Under the **Fifth Schedule**, a Tribes Advisory Council is mandatory in states with Scheduled Areas, but the **President** can direct the establishment of such a council in any state having Scheduled Tribes but no Scheduled Areas.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following States/UTs are included in the Northern Zonal Council?
questions.questionText: Which of the following States/UTs are included in the Northern Zonal Council?

options.a: Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir
options.b: Haryana, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Rajasthan and NCT of Delhi
options.c: Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Punjab, Haryana and Rajasthan
options.d: Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab and Haryana

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Haryana, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Rajasthan and NCT of Delhi**. The **Northern Zonal Council** currently comprises Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Rajasthan, the NCT of Delhi, and the UTs of Chandigarh, Jammu & Kashmir, and Ladakh. (UP and Uttarakhand fall under the Central Zonal Council).
