questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Solicitor General of India (SGI) is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Solicitor General of India (SGI) is not correct?

options.a: The SGI is the Government's chief legal advisor.
options.b: The SGI is the secondary law officer of the country.
options.c: The post of the SGI is a constitutional post.
options.d: The SGI is appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: Both **(a) and (c)** are technically incorrect. The Attorney General is the chief legal advisor, and the post of the SGI is **statutory/executive**, not a constitutional post (Article 76 only mentions the Attorney General).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following committees does not have members from the Rajya Sabha?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following committees does not have members from the Rajya Sabha?

options.d: The Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Estimates Committee**. The Estimates Committee is the largest parliamentary committee with 30 members, and all of its members are drawn **exclusively from the Lok Sabha**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the election of the President of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the election of the President of India is not correct?

options.a: A Union Minister shall not be eligible for election as the President.
options.b: The President is elected by the elected members of the Parliament and the Legislative Assemblies of States and Union Territories.
options.c: The Electoral College does not include elected members of the Legislative Councils.
options.d: The system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote is followed.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A Union Minister shall not be eligible for election as the President.** Article 58 explicitly states that a sitting **President, Vice-President, Governor, or Union/State Minister** is not deemed to hold an office of profit and is therefore **eligible to contest** the Presidential election.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: The Citizenship Act, 1955 deals with the determination of citizenship on or after
questions.questionText: The Citizenship Act, 1955 deals with the determination of citizenship on or after
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **26th January, 1950**. The Constitution (Articles 5-11) dealt with citizenship strictly up to its commencement on **26th January 1950**. The **Citizenship Act, 1955** regulates the acquisition and loss of citizenship after this date.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a part of Article 51 of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a part of Article 51 of the Constitution of India?

options.b: Maintain just and honourable relations between nations
options.c: Use of UN peacekeeping force for resolution of international disputes
options.d: Encourage settlement of international disputes by arbitration

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Use of UN peacekeeping force for resolution of international disputes**. Article 51 explicitly directs the State to promote international peace, maintain honourable relations, and encourage arbitration. The use of a **UN peacekeeping force** is not explicitly mentioned in the text of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 is correct?

options.a: It ensures 175 days of employment to needy persons in rural areas.
options.b: It gives higher wages to women workers.
options.c: People are generally given both skilled and unskilled jobs.
options.d: This is implemented only in rural areas of India.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **This is implemented only in rural areas of India.** MGNREGA provides 100 days (not 175) of guaranteed wage employment for **unskilled** manual work, strictly targeting the **rural areas** of the country.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Which one of the following is not mentioned as a form of Emergency in the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not mentioned as a form of Emergency in the Constitution of India?

options.b: State Emergency in terms of Proclamation of President's Rule in a State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Health Emergency**. The Constitution categorizes emergencies into three types: **National Emergency (Article 352)**, **State Emergency/President's Rule (Article 356)**, and **Financial Emergency (Article 360)**. There is no constitutional provision for a "Health Emergency".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a provision related to a Money Bill?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a provision related to a Money Bill?

options.a: Imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax
options.b: Appropriation of moneys out of the Consolidated Fund of India
options.c: Imposition of fines by local authority for local purpose
options.d: Custody of the Consolidated Fund of India or the Contingency Fund of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Imposition of fines by local authority for local purpose**. Under **Article 110**, a bill is not deemed a Money Bill merely if it provides for the imposition of **fines or pecuniary penalties**, or if a tax is imposed by a **local authority for local purposes**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Committee on Estimates is an ad hoc committee of the Parliament.
2. Committee on Railway Convention is a standing committee of the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Committee on Estimates is an ad hoc committee of the Parliament. 2. Committee on Railway Convention is a standing committee of the Parliament. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. The **Estimates Committee** is a permanent **Standing Committee** (financial), not ad hoc. Conversely, the **Railway Convention Committee** is set up periodically to review dividend rates and operates as an **ad hoc committee**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following is not an Indian form of Socialism?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not an Indian form of Socialism?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Radical Socialism**. Indian socialism is a unique blend of Marxism and Gandhism, leaning heavily towards **Democratic Socialism** (and Nehruvian models) rather than violent or totalitarian "Radical Socialism".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of Indian Federalism?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of Indian Federalism?

options.a: The federating units consented to form a union
options.b: Residuary powers vest with the Centre
options.d: An extensive Union and Concurrent list

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The federating units consented to form a union**. Unlike the American federation, the **Indian Federation is not the result of an agreement** among states. The country is an indestructible Union of destructible states.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India, as it stands amended, includes which of the following?
1. Freedom of speech and expression
2. Assemble peaceably and without arms
3. To acquire and dispose property
4. To move freely throughout the territory of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India, as it stands amended, includes which of the following? 1. Freedom of speech and expression 2. Assemble peaceably and without arms 3. To acquire and dispose property 4. To move freely throughout the territory of India Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4 only**. The right to **acquire, hold, and dispose of property [Article 19(1)(f)]** was deleted from the list of Fundamental Rights by the **44th Amendment Act (1978)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Election Commission is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Election Commission is not correct?

options.a: It conducts elections to the office of the Vice President of India.
options.b: An Election Commissioner can be removed from his office without the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.
options.c: Election Commissioners are appointed by the President of India.
options.d: Governor of a State is constitutionally bound to provide support staff to the Election Commission, if required.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **An Election Commissioner can be removed from his office without the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.** According to the Constitution, an Election Commissioner or Regional Commissioner **cannot be removed from office except on the specific recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. Equality of status and equality of opportunity find mention in the Preamble.
2. The expression 'unity and integrity of the Nation' was not there in the Preamble since the beginning.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. Equality of status and equality of opportunity find mention in the Preamble. 2. The expression 'unity and integrity of the Nation' was not there in the Preamble since the beginning. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The Preamble promises **Equality of status and of opportunity**. The word "integrity" was not originally present; the phrase "unity of the Nation" was amended to **"unity and integrity of the Nation"** by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: In Part-IVA of the Constitution of India,
which one of the following is not mentioned as a duty of every citizen of India?
questions.questionText: In Part-IVA of the Constitution of India, which one of the following is not mentioned as a duty of every citizen of India?

options.a: To render national service when called upon to do so
options.b: To renounce practices derogatory to the dignity of women
options.c: To value and preserve a unitary national culture
options.d: To develop the spirit of inquiry and reform

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To value and preserve a unitary national culture**. Article 51A states the duty to value and preserve the rich heritage of our **"composite culture"**, not a "unitary national culture".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: According to the Constitution of India,
which one of the following statements is not correct?
questions.questionText: According to the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.a: The executive powers of the Union shall be vested in the President of India.
options.b: Parliament, by law, can confer functions on authorities other than the President of India.
options.c: The President of India is elected by an electoral college consisting of all Members of Parliament and all Members of all State Legislatures.
options.d: A person, having held office as the President, shall be eligible for election to that office for the second term.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The President of India is elected by an electoral college consisting of all Members of Parliament and all Members of all State Legislatures.** The electoral college consists only of the **elected members** of Parliament and **elected members** of the Legislative Assemblies (nominated members and Legislative Council members do not participate).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: National Disaster Management Authority is headed by
questions.questionText: National Disaster Management Authority is headed by
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Prime Minister**. The **National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)**, established under the **Disaster Management Act, 2005**, is statutorily headed by the **Prime Minister of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding the 'casting vote' in the Parliament is/are correct?
1. It is cast by the speaker or a person acting as such.
2. It is cast in addition to voting in the first instance.
3. It is cast in the case of equality of votes.
4. It is always cast to maintain the status quo.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding the 'casting vote' in the Parliament is/are correct? 1. It is cast by the speaker or a person acting as such. 2. It is cast in addition to voting in the first instance. 3. It is cast in the case of equality of votes. 4. It is always cast to maintain the status quo. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. A **casting vote** is exercised by the **Speaker or the Presiding Officer** exclusively to resolve a **deadlock (equality of votes)**. The Speaker does **not vote in the first instance**, and it is not constitutionally mandated to strictly maintain the status quo, though it is a convention.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Equality before the law or equal protection of the laws within the territory of India is guaranteed under
which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Equality before the law or equal protection of the laws within the territory of India is guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 14**. **Article 14** of the Constitution declares that the State shall not deny to any person **equality before the law** or the **equal protection of the laws** within the territory of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following said that Democracy means a system of 'Government by Consent'?
questions.questionText: Who among the following said that Democracy means a system of 'Government by Consent'?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **John Locke**. The English philosopher **John Locke** pioneered the concept that a legitimate government relies on the **consent of the governed**, a foundational idea of democratic theory.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one among the following is not a feature of centralization?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not a feature of centralization?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Liberty**. While centralization often promotes **national unity and uniformity** in laws/administration, it inherently restricts local autonomy and individual **liberty** by concentrating power at the apex.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following is not an objective of NITI Aayog?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not an objective of NITI Aayog?

options.a: It provides a critical direction and strategic input for development process.
options.b: It functions as a 'think tank' in providing key elements of policy.
options.c: It monitors and evaluates the implementation of the programmes.
options.d: It offers a platform for resolution of inter-state conflicts as 'provider of first and last resort'.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It offers a platform for resolution of inter-state conflicts as 'provider of first and last resort'.** While NITI Aayog fosters **cooperative federalism**, the primary constitutional body designated for resolving inter-state disputes is the **Inter-State Council** or the **Supreme Court (under original jurisdiction)**. NITI Aayog is a **policy think tank**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Article 231 of the Constitution of India grants power to establish a common High Court for two or more states to
questions.questionText: Article 231 of the Constitution of India grants power to establish a common High Court for two or more states to
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Parliament.** Under **Article 231**, it is the **Parliament of India** that has the exclusive legislative authority to establish a **common High Court** for two or more States or for two or more States and a Union territory.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following motions cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in the Parliament?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following motions cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in the Parliament?

options.a: That the Bill be taken into consideration
options.b: That the Bill be circulated for the purpose of eliciting public opinion
options.c: That the Bill be referred to a Select Committee
options.d: That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House**. A **Joint Committee** inherently consists of members from **both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha**, thus requiring the explicit **concurrence of the other House** to be constituted.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one among the following was eliminated by the 44th Amendment Act of the Parliament?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following was eliminated by the 44th Amendment Act of the Parliament?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to Property**. The **44th Amendment Act of 1978** abolished the **Right to Property** as a Fundamental Right by repealing Article 19(1)(f) and Article 31, converting it into a mere constitutional right under **Article 300A**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following was the Chairman of the National Commission for Review of the Working of the Constitution (2000)?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was the Chairman of the National Commission for Review of the Working of the Constitution (2000)?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Justice M.N. Venkatachaliah**. The NDA government set up the **National Commission to Review the Working of the Constitution (NCRWC)** in the year 2000 under the chairmanship of former Chief Justice of India, **M.N. Venkatachaliah**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: M.
M. Punchhi Commission on Centre-State Relations has recommended the disposal of a bill reserved for the consideration of the Union Executive within
questions.questionText: M. M. Punchhi Commission on Centre-State Relations has recommended the disposal of a bill reserved for the consideration of the Union Executive within
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **six months.** To curb the indefinite delay by the President on state bills, the **Punchhi Commission** explicitly recommended that a bill reserved for the President's consideration should be disposed of within a maximum period of **six months**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the creation of a new state or alteration of boundaries of states is/are correct?
1. An amendment has to be moved in the Parliament under Article 368 of the Constitution of India.
2. Ratification by one half of the states is necessary after the amendment is made by the Parliament.
3. The legislation can be passed by a simple majority of both Houses of Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the creation of a new state or alteration of boundaries of states is/are correct? 1. An amendment has to be moved in the Parliament under Article 368 of the Constitution of India. 2. Ratification by one half of the states is necessary after the amendment is made by the Parliament. 3. The legislation can be passed by a simple majority of both Houses of Parliament. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. Under **Article 3**, the Parliament can create new states or alter boundaries by passing a law with a **simple majority**. **Article 4** explicitly states that such laws are **not to be considered constitutional amendments under Article 368**, meaning they require neither a special majority nor state ratification.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Panchayati Raj System are correct?
1. All seats in a Panchayat are filled by persons chosen by direct election.
2. The 'Gram Sabha' consists of persons registered in the village electoral rolls.
3. The Chairperson of a Panchayat is elected in accordance with a law passed by each state.
4. All states in India have a three-tier system of Panchayats.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Panchayati Raj System are correct? 1. All seats in a Panchayat are filled by persons chosen by direct election. 2. The 'Gram Sabha' consists of persons registered in the village electoral rolls. 3. The Chairperson of a Panchayat is elected in accordance with a law passed by each state. 4. All states in India have a three-tier system of Panchayats. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Under the **73rd Amendment**, seats at all levels are filled by **direct election**, the **Gram Sabha** comprises registered voters of the village, and the election of the **Chairperson** is determined by state legislature laws.
Statement 4 is incorrect because states with a population of less than **20 lakhs** may have a **two-tier system** (omitting the intermediate level).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The elections to the Panchayat are conducted by the State Election Commission.
2. The State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Chief Election Commissioner of India.
3. The State Legislatures have the power to make laws on all matters relating to Panchayat elections.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. The elections to the Panchayat are conducted by the State Election Commission. 2. The State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Chief Election Commissioner of India. 3. The State Legislatures have the power to make laws on all matters relating to Panchayat elections. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. The **State Election Commission** conducts panchayat elections, and the **State Legislature** has the power to make laws concerning these elections. However, the **State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Governor**, not by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a correct description of the Union Cabinet?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a correct description of the Union Cabinet?

options.a: It is part of the Parliament.
options.b: It is responsible to the Parliament.
options.c: It remains in power till it enjoys the confidence of the Parliament.
options.d: A person from outside the Parliament can never be appointed a member of the Cabinet.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question was officially **Cancelled**. However, statement (d) is definitively incorrect because a **non-MP can be appointed as a Cabinet Minister** provided they secure a seat in Parliament within **six months**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the composition of the Parliament is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the composition of the Parliament is not correct?

options.a: Representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha are elected directly by the people.
options.b: Representatives from Union Territories in Lok Sabha are chosen by direct elections.
options.d: Lok Sabha has seats reserved for SCs and STs.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha are elected directly by the people.** The Rajya Sabha (Council of States) members are elected **indirectly** by the elected members of the State Legislative Assemblies using the system of **proportional representation by means of a single transferable vote**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which among the following fundamental rights is/are available to non-citizens?
1. Freedom of speech
2. Protection against self-incrimination
3. Freedom of conscience
4. Non-discrimination in matters of employment
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which among the following fundamental rights is/are available to non-citizens? 1. Freedom of speech 2. Protection against self-incrimination 3. Freedom of conscience 4. Non-discrimination in matters of employment Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. Fundamental Rights exclusively available to **citizens only** are Articles **15, 16 (employment), 19 (speech), 29, and 30**. Protection against self-incrimination (Article 20) and freedom of conscience (Article 25) are available to **both citizens and non-citizens**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Vice-President of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Vice-President of India is not correct?

options.a: He is not eligible for re-election.
options.b: He must not be less than 35 years of age.
options.c: He is elected by members of an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament.
options.d: His term of office is five years.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **He is not eligible for re-election.** Under the Indian Constitution, the **Vice-President** is perfectly **eligible for re-election** to the office for any number of terms (e.g., Dr. S. Radhakrishnan and Hamid Ansari).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A motion of no-confidence is moved against
questions.questionText: A motion of no-confidence is moved against
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Council of Ministers.** Under **Article 75**, the **Council of Ministers** is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha. Therefore, a **no-confidence motion** can only be moved against the entire Council of Ministers, not an individual minister.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the functioning of the Panchayats is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the functioning of the Panchayats is not correct?

options.a: Panchayats may levy, collect and appropriate taxes, duties, tolls, etc.
options.b: A person who has attained the age of 25 years will be eligible to be a member of a Panchayat.
options.c: Every Panchayat shall ordinarily continue for five years from the date of its first meeting.
options.d: A Panchayat reconstituted after premature dissolution shall continue only for the remainder of the full period.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A person who has attained the age of 25 years will be eligible to be a member of a Panchayat.** The minimum qualifying age to contest Panchayat elections is **21 years**, not 25 years.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Overseas Indians can exercise franchise in an election to the Lok Sabha under which of the following conditions?
1. They must be citizens of India.
2. Their names must figure in the electoral roll.
3. They must be present in India to vote.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Overseas Indians can exercise franchise in an election to the Lok Sabha under which of the following conditions? 1. They must be citizens of India. 2. Their names must figure in the electoral roll. 3. They must be present in India to vote. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) who are **citizens of India** and have their names registered in the **electoral rolls** of their original constituency are allowed to vote, but they must be **physically present** at the polling booth in India (proxy/postal voting for general NRIs is not fully implemented).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following was added as a fundamental duty through the Constitution (86th Amendment) Act, 2002?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following was added as a fundamental duty through the Constitution (86th Amendment) Act, 2002?

options.a: To strive towards excellence in individual and collective activity
options.b: To provide opportunities for education to one's child between the age of 6 and 14 years
options.c: To work for the welfare of women and children

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To provide opportunities for education to one's child between the age of 6 and 14 years**. The **86th Amendment Act (2002)** added the 11th Fundamental Duty under **Article 51A(k)**, making it a duty for parents or guardians to provide educational opportunities to children aged **6 to 14 years**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India protects a person against double jeopardy?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India protects a person against double jeopardy?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 20**. **Article 20(2)** specifically prohibits **double jeopardy**, stating that no person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Which one of the following Articles was defended by Dr. B. R. Ambedkar on the plea that it would be used as 'a matter of last resort'?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Articles was defended by Dr. B. R. Ambedkar on the plea that it would be used as 'a matter of last resort'?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 356**. While debating the provision for **President's Rule (Article 356)** in the Constituent Assembly, Dr. B.R. Ambedkar famously hoped that these drastic powers to take over a state government would remain a **dead letter** and be used only as a **matter of last resort**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: What is the ground on which the Supreme Court can refuse relief under Article 32?
questions.questionText: What is the ground on which the Supreme Court can refuse relief under Article 32?

options.a: The aggrieved person can get remedy from another court
options.b: That disputed facts have to be investigated
options.c: That no fundamental right has been infringed
options.d: That the petitioner has not asked for the proper writ applicable to his/her case

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **That no fundamental right has been infringed**. **Article 32** strictly guarantees the right to move the Supreme Court exclusively for the **enforcement of Fundamental Rights**. If no fundamental right is violated, the Supreme Court can refuse relief under this Article.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises consists of
questions.questionText: The Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises consists of

options.a: The Department of Heavy Industry and the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
options.b: The Department of Public Enterprises and the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
options.c: The Department of Scientific and Industrial Research and the Department of Heavy Industry
options.d: The Department of Heavy Industry and the Department of Public Enterprises

questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **the Department of Heavy Industry and the Department of Public Enterprises**. As of the time of the exam, the **Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises** was composed of two distinct departments: **Heavy Industry** and **Public Enterprises**. *(

Note: Public Enterprises has since been moved to the Ministry of Finance).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The First Delimitation Commission in India was constituted in
questions.questionText: The First Delimitation Commission in India was constituted in
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1952**. Following the Delimitation Commission Act of 1952, the **First Delimitation Commission** was officially constituted in **1952** to redraw parliamentary and assembly constituencies.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Who among the following stated in the Constituent Assembly that on 26th January, 1950, India was going to enter a life of contradictions?
questions.questionText: Who among the following stated in the Constituent Assembly that on 26th January, 1950, India was going to enter a life of contradictions?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Dr. B. R. Ambedkar**. In his final speech to the Constituent Assembly, **Dr. B.R. Ambedkar** warned that in politics India would have equality (one man, one vote), but in its social and economic structure, it would continue to have gross inequalities, marking a **life of contradictions**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The power of the Supreme Court to decide in the case of a dispute between two or more States is called
questions.questionText: The power of the Supreme Court to decide in the case of a dispute between two or more States is called
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **original jurisdiction**. Under **Article 131**, the Supreme Court has exclusive **original jurisdiction** over any inter-state disputes or federal disputes between the Union and States.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: The power to legislate on all matters relating to elections to Panchayats lies with
questions.questionText: The power to legislate on all matters relating to elections to Panchayats lies with
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the State Legislatures**. Under **Article 243K(4)**, the **State Legislature** is granted the exclusive authority to make provisions with respect to all matters relating to or in connection with **elections to the Panchayats**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: The 11th Schedule of the Constitution of India distributes powers between
questions.questionText: The 11th Schedule of the Constitution of India distributes powers between

options.a: The Union and the State Legislatures
options.b: The State Legislatures and the Panchayat
options.c: The Municipal Corporation and the Panchayat
options.d: The Gram Sabha and the Panchayat

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the State Legislatures and the Panchayat**. The **11th Schedule**, added by the 73rd Amendment, contains 29 functional items over which the **State Legislatures** devolve powers and responsibilities to the **Panchayats**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of formation of the Commissions starting from the earliest?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct sequence of formation of the Commissions starting from the earliest?

options.a: Finance Commission, Planning Commission, Investment Commission, Election Commission
options.b: Election Commission, Planning Commission, Finance Commission, Investment Commission
options.c: Planning Commission, Election Commission, Finance Commission, Investment Commission
options.d: Investment Commission, Finance Commission, Planning Commission, Election Commission

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Election Commission, Planning Commission, Finance Commission, Investment Commission**. The **Election Commission** was established on 25 Jan 1950. The **Planning Commission** was formed in March 1950. The First **Finance Commission** was constituted in 1951. The **Investment Commission** was established much later in 2004.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The formulation of policy in respect to Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) is the responsibility of
questions.questionText: The formulation of policy in respect to Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) is the responsibility of

options.a: The Ministry of Law and Justice
options.b: The Department of Science and Technology
options.c: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
options.d: The Ministry of Human Resource Development

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade**. The **DPIIT** (formerly DIPP) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry is the nodal department responsible for the formulation and administration of **Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs)** policy in India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: The Inter-State Council was set up in 1990 on the recommendation of
questions.questionText: The Inter-State Council was set up in 1990 on the recommendation of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Sarkaria Commission**. Acting on the core recommendation of the **Sarkaria Commission** (which examined center-state relations), the V.P. Singh government established the **Inter-State Council** in 1990 under Article 263.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which among the following writs is issued to quash the order of a Court or Tribunal?
questions.questionText: Which among the following writs is issued to quash the order of a Court or Tribunal?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Certiorari**. The writ of **Certiorari** is issued by a higher court to a lower court or tribunal to transfer a case or to **quash an order/decision** that has already been passed without jurisdiction or with an error of law.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which among the following statements about the power to change the basic structure of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. It falls outside the scope of the amending powers of the Parliament.
2. It can be exercised by the people through representatives in a Constituent Assembly.
3. It falls within the constituent powers of the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which among the following statements about the power to change the basic structure of the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. It falls outside the scope of the amending powers of the Parliament. 2. It can be exercised by the people through representatives in a Constituent Assembly. 3. It falls within the constituent powers of the Parliament. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2**. According to the *Kesavananda Bharati* ruling, the **basic structure falls outside the amending powers of the Parliament** under Article 368. However, a sovereign body like a newly convened **Constituent Assembly** representing the ultimate will of the people could theoretically rewrite or change the basic structure.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: When a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation, the right to move a Court for the enforcement of all Fundamental Rights remains suspended, except
questions.questionText: When a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation, the right to move a Court for the enforcement of all Fundamental Rights remains suspended, except
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 20 and Article 21**. Following the **44th Amendment Act (1978)**, the President cannot suspend the right to move the court for the enforcement of fundamental rights guaranteed under **Article 20** (protection in respect of conviction) and **Article 21** (right to life and personal liberty) during any emergency.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India lays down that no citizen can be denied the use of wells, tanks and bathing Ghats maintained out of State funds?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India lays down that no citizen can be denied the use of wells, tanks and bathing Ghats maintained out of State funds?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 15**. **Article 15(2)** explicitly states that no citizen shall be subject to any disability or restriction with regard to the use of **wells, tanks, bathing ghats**, roads, and places of public resort maintained wholly or partly out of State funds.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a change brought about by the Indian Independence Act of 1947?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a change brought about by the Indian Independence Act of 1947?

options.a: The Government of India Act, 1935 was amended to provide an interim Constitution.
options.b: India ceased to be a dependency.
options.c: The Crown was the source of authority till new Constitution was framed.
options.d: The Governor-General was the constitutional head of Indian Dominion.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Crown was the source of authority till new Constitution was framed.** The Indian Independence Act fundamentally transferred all sovereignty away from the British Crown. The **Constituent Assembly** became the sovereign source of legal and constitutional authority, completely severing dependency on the Crown.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a correct statement regarding the provision of Legislative Council in the State Legislature?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a correct statement regarding the provision of Legislative Council in the State Legislature?

options.a: The States of Bihar and Telangana have Legislative Councils.
options.b: The total number of members in the Legislative Council of a State shall not exceed one-third of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.
options.c: One-twelfth of all members shall be elected by electorates consisting of local bodies and authorities.
options.d: One-twelfth of all members shall be elected by graduates residing in the Stat

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **One-twelfth of all members shall be elected by electorates consisting of local bodies and authorities.** Under Article 171, **one-third (not one-twelfth)** of the members of the Legislative Council are elected by electorates consisting of members of **local bodies** (municipalities, district boards, etc.).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which one of the following is not correct about the Panchayats as laid down in Part IX of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not correct about the Panchayats as laid down in Part IX of the Constitution of India?

options.a: The Chairperson of a Panchayat needs to be directly elected by people in order to exercise the right to vote in the Panchayat meetings.
options.b: The State Legislature has the right to decide whether or not offices of the Chairpersons in the Panchayats are reserved for SCs, STs or women.
options.c: Unless dissolved earlier, every Panchayat continues for a period of five years.
options.d: The State Legislature may by law make provisions for audit of accounts of the Panchayats.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Chairperson of a Panchayat needs to be directly elected by people in order to exercise the right to vote in the Panchayat meetings.** Chairpersons at the intermediate and district levels are elected **indirectly**, yet they still retain full voting rights in the meetings. The method of election (direct/indirect) does not deprive them of voting rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following is not correct about Administrative Tribunals?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not correct about Administrative Tribunals?

options.a: The Parliament may by law constitute Administrative Tribunals both at the Union and State levels.
options.b: Tribunals may look into disputes and complaints with respect to recruitment and conditions of service of persons appointed to public services.
options.c: Tribunals established by a law of the Parliament can exclude the jurisdiction of all Courts to allow for special leave to appeal.
options.d: The law establishing the Tribunals may provide for procedures including rules of evidence to be followed.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Tribunals established by a law of the Parliament can exclude the jurisdiction of all Courts to allow for special leave to appeal.** In the landmark *Chandra Kumar case (1997)*, the Supreme Court ruled that tribunals **cannot exclude the writ jurisdiction of High Courts (Article 226/227)** or the **Supreme Court's jurisdiction under Article 136** (Special Leave to Appeal), as judicial review is part of the basic structure.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements about Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar, as a drafting member of the Constitution of India, are correct?
1. He favoured the role of the Supreme Court in taking important decisions related to the interpretation of the Constitution of India.
2. He felt that the Supreme Court had to draw the line between liberty and social control.
3. He believed in the dominance of the executive over the judiciary.
4. He favoured a dictatorial form of governance.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar, as a drafting member of the Constitution of India, are correct? 1. He favoured the role of the Supreme Court in taking important decisions related to the interpretation of the Constitution of India. 2. He felt that the Supreme Court had to draw the line between liberty and social control. 3. He believed in the dominance of the executive over the judiciary. 4. He favoured a dictatorial form of governance. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. **Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar** was a prominent jurist and member of the Drafting Committee who robustly defended an **independent Supreme Court** tasked with interpreting the Constitution and balancing **individual liberty against social control**. He never favored executive dominance or a dictatorship.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following States of India is not covered by Flood Forecasting Stations set up by the Central Water Commission?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following States of India is not covered by Flood Forecasting Stations set up by the Central Water Commission?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Himachal Pradesh**. At the time the question was framed, the **Central Water Commission (CWC)** network of flood forecasting stations did not comprehensively cover the hilly, flash-flood-prone terrain of **Himachal Pradesh**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Prime Minister's National Relief Fund is operated by
which one of the following bodies?
questions.questionText: The Prime Minister's National Relief Fund is operated by which one of the following bodies?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Prime Minister's Office (PMO)**. The **Prime Minister's National Relief Fund (PMNRF)** operates entirely under the direction and management of the **PMO** without any budgetary support from the government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the National Green Tribunal is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the National Green Tribunal is not correct?

options.a: It was set up in the year 2010.
options.b: It is involved in effective and expeditious disposal of cases relating to environmental protection and conservation of forests.
options.c: It may consider giving relief and compensation for damages to persons and property.
options.d: It is bound by the procedures laid down under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It is bound by the procedures laid down under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.** The **National Green Tribunal (NGT)** is explicitly **not bound by the strict procedures of the CPC 1908 or the Indian Evidence Act**. Instead, it is guided by the **principles of natural justice**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the provisions of the Constitution of India with regard to the State of Jammu and Kashmir is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the provisions of the Constitution of India with regard to the State of Jammu and Kashmir is not correct?

options.a: The Directive Principles of State Policy do not apply.
options.b: Article 35A gives some special rights to the permanent residents of the State with regard to employment, settlement and property.
options.d: Article 368 is not applicable for the amendment of Constitution of the Stat

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 19(1)(f) has been omitted.** Prior to the abrogation of Article 370 in 2019, the Fundamental Right to Property (**Article 19(1)(f)** and Article 31) **was still applicable to J&K**, even though it had been deleted for the rest of India by the 44th Amendment.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following are the functions of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)?
1. Inquiry at its own initiative on the violation of human rights
2. Inquiry on a petition presented to it by a victim
3. Visit to jails to study the condition of the inmates
4. Undertaking and promoting research in the field of human rights
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the functions of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC)? 1. Inquiry at its own initiative on the violation of human rights 2. Inquiry on a petition presented to it by a victim 3. Visit to jails to study the condition of the inmates 4. Undertaking and promoting research in the field of human rights Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. The **Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993** empowers the **NHRC** to inquire *suo motu* or on petitions, undertake jail visits to inspect living conditions, and comprehensively promote/research human rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: A person is disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a Member of either House of the Parliament if the person
1. holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State other than an office declared by the Parliament by law not to disqualify its holder
2. is an undischarged insolvent
3. is so disqualified under the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution of India
4. is of unsound mind and stands so declared by a competent Court
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: A person is disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a Member of either House of the Parliament if the person 1. holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State other than an office declared by the Parliament by law not to disqualify its holder 2. is an undischarged insolvent 3. is so disqualified under the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution of India 4. is of unsound mind and stands so declared by a competent Court Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. Under **Article 102**, an MP is disqualified if they hold an **office of profit**, are of **unsound mind**, or are an **undischarged insolvent**. Additionally, disqualification on grounds of defection under the **Tenth Schedule** applies universally to members of either house.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: According to the Election Commission of India, in order to be recognized as a 'National Party', a political party must be treated as a recognized political party in how many States?
questions.questionText: According to the Election Commission of India, in order to be recognized as a 'National Party', a political party must be treated as a recognized political party in how many States?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **At least four States**. Under the Election Symbols (Reservation and Allotment) Order, 1968, one of the criteria for recognizing a political party as a **National Party** is that it must be independently recognized as a state party in **at least four different states**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following Amendments to the Constitution of India has prescribed that the Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15 percent of total number of members of the House of the People or Legislative Assembly in the States?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Amendments to the Constitution of India has prescribed that the Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15 percent of total number of members of the House of the People or Legislative Assembly in the States?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **91st Amendment**. The **91st Constitutional Amendment Act (2003)** introduced Article 75(1A) and 164(1A) which strictly cap the total size of the Council of Ministers at **15 percent** of the total strength of the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies respectively.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements about Attorney General of India is/are not correct?
1. He is the first Law Officer of the Government of India.
2. He is entitled to the privileges of a Member of the Parliament.
3. He is a whole-time counsel for the Government.
4. He must have the same qualifications as are required to be a judge of the Supreme Court.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about Attorney General of India is/are not correct? 1. He is the first Law Officer of the Government of India. 2. He is entitled to the privileges of a Member of the Parliament. 3. He is a whole-time counsel for the Government. 4. He must have the same qualifications as are required to be a judge of the Supreme Court. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. The Attorney General is **not a full-time government servant** (whole-time counsel); they are allowed to take up private legal practice, provided they don't advise against the Government of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Under
which one of the following Amendment Acts was Sikkim admitted into the Union of India?
questions.questionText: Under which one of the following Amendment Acts was Sikkim admitted into the Union of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **36th**. The **36th Constitutional Amendment Act (1975)** formally elevated Sikkim to the status of a full-fledged **state** within the Union of India, repealing the earlier "associate state" status introduced by the 35th Amendment.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following statements in respect of the States of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements in respect of the States of India is not correct?

options.a: States in India cannot have their own Constitutions.
options.b: The State of Jammu and Kashmir has its own Constitution.
options.c: States in India do not have the right to secede from the Union of India.
options.d: The maximum number of members in the Council of Ministers of Delhi can be 15 percent of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The maximum number of members in the Council of Ministers of Delhi can be 15 percent of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly.** Under Article 239AA, the maximum strength of the Council of Ministers in Delhi is capped strictly at **10 percent** of the total strength of the Legislative Assembly, not 15%.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following States of India has the largest number of seats in its Legislative Assembly?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following States of India has the largest number of seats in its Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **West Bengal**. Among the given options, **West Bengal** holds the highest number of Legislative Assembly seats (**294**), followed by Bihar (243), Tamil Nadu (234), and Madhya Pradesh (230).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Ordinance-making power of the Governor is/are correct?
1. It is a discretionary power.
2. The Governor himself is not competent to withdraw the Ordinance at any time.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Ordinance-making power of the Governor is/are correct? 1. It is a discretionary power. 2. The Governor himself is not competent to withdraw the Ordinance at any time. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. The ordinance-making power (Article 213) is **not a discretionary power**; the Governor must act on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers. Furthermore, the Governor **is fully competent** to withdraw an ordinance at any time on the advice of the council.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The judgement of the Supreme Court of India in the Vishakha Case pertains to
questions.questionText: The judgement of the Supreme Court of India in the Vishakha Case pertains to
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **sexual harassment in the workplace**. The landmark *Vishaka v. State of Rajasthan (1997)* judgment laid down binding guidelines for employers to prevent and address **sexual harassment of women at the workplace**, ultimately leading to the POSH Act, 2013.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is correct in relation to the GST Bill passed by the Rajya Sabha in August 2016?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is correct in relation to the GST Bill passed by the Rajya Sabha in August 2016?

options.a: It will replace all central taxes, duties, etc., only by a single tax.
options.b: It will subsume central as well as State taxes, duties, etc.
options.c: GST will be levied on alcoholic liquor for human consumption at a uniform rate of 25 percent.
options.d: Petroleum and petroleum products shall not be subjected to the levy of GST.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It will subsume central as well as State taxes, duties, etc.** The introduction of the **Goods and Services Tax (GST)** fundamentally overhauled the indirect tax regime by comprehensively **subsuming a myriad of Central and State taxes** (like Excise, VAT, Service Tax) into a unified structure.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: To be eligible to contest election under the Haryana Panchayati Raj (Amendment) Act, 2015, a candidate should
1. have a functional toilet at home
2. have payment slips of power bills
3. not be a cooperative loan defaulter
4. have studied minimum matriculation irrespective of category
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: To be eligible to contest election under the Haryana Panchayati Raj (Amendment) Act, 2015, a candidate should 1. have a functional toilet at home 2. have payment slips of power bills 3. not be a cooperative loan defaulter 4. have studied minimum matriculation irrespective of category Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. The 2015 Haryana amendment imposed criteria such as having a **functional toilet**, **clearing electricity bills**, and **not being a loan defaulter**. The educational criteria were introduced but varied by category (e.g., lower requirements for SC women), so matriculation "irrespective of category" is incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Arrange the following Commissions chronologically on the basis of their date of setting:
1. The Second Administrative Reforms Commission
2. The Eleventh Finance Commission
3. Punchhi Commission
4. Sarkaria Commission
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Arrange the following Commissions chronologically on the basis of their date of setting: 1. The Second Administrative Reforms Commission 2. The Eleventh Finance Commission 3. Punchhi Commission 4. Sarkaria Commission Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **4, 2, 1, 3**. **Sarkaria Commission** was set up in 1983. The **Eleventh Finance Commission** was constituted in 1998. The **Second Administrative Reforms Commission** was formed in 2005. The **Punchhi Commission** on Centre-State relations was set up last in 2007.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to Panchayats in India are correct?
1. Seats in a Panchayat are filled by direct election from the territorial constituencies in the Panchayat area.
2. The Gram Sabha is the body of persons registered in the electoral rolls relating to a village within the Panchayat area.
3. The Panchayats work on the principle of constitutional autonomy.
4. The State Legislature may by law endow the Panchayats with the power and authority to enable them to function.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to Panchayats in India are correct? 1. Seats in a Panchayat are filled by direct election from the territorial constituencies in the Panchayat area. 2. The Gram Sabha is the body of persons registered in the electoral rolls relating to a village within the Panchayat area. 3. The Panchayats work on the principle of constitutional autonomy. 4. The State Legislature may by law endow the Panchayats with the power and authority to enable them to function. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. Under the **73rd Constitutional Amendment Act**, seats in Panchayats are filled by **direct elections**. The **Gram Sabha** consists of all registered voters in the village. The **State Legislature** is responsible for endowing Panchayats with powers and authority. Panchayats do not possess absolute **constitutional autonomy**; their powers are subject to devolution by the state legislature.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding Indian federal system is/are correct?
1. All States have equal representation in the Rajya Sabha.
2. Consent of a State is not required for altering its boundaries.
3. There is no dual citizenship in India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding Indian federal system is/are correct? 1. All States have equal representation in the Rajya Sabha. 2. Consent of a State is not required for altering its boundaries. 3. There is no dual citizenship in India. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. India provides for **single citizenship** (no dual citizenship). Under **Article 3**, the Parliament can alter state boundaries without the binding consent of the state.
Statement 1 is incorrect because **Rajya Sabha representation is based on population**, not equality of states.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following are the powers of the Supreme Court of India?
1. Original jurisdiction in a dispute between the Government of India and one or more States
2. The power to hear appeals from the High Courts
3. Passing decrees and orders for doing justice in any matter before it
4. Render advice to the President of India in matters of law
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the powers of the Supreme Court of India? 1. Original jurisdiction in a dispute between the Government of India and one or more States 2. The power to hear appeals from the High Courts 3. Passing decrees and orders for doing justice in any matter before it 4. Render advice to the President of India in matters of law Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. The Supreme Court enjoys **Original Jurisdiction (Article 131)** for federal disputes, **Appellate Jurisdiction** for civil/criminal appeals, power to pass decrees for **complete justice (Article 142)**, and **Advisory Jurisdiction (Article 143)** to advise the President.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a component of Revenue Receipts of the Union Government?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a component of Revenue Receipts of the Union Government?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Disinvestment receipts**. **Disinvestment** involves the sale of government assets (like PSU shares), which reduces the government's assets. Therefore, it is classified as a **Capital Receipt**, not a Revenue Receipt.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the powers of the President of India is/are correct?
1. The executive power of the Union shall be vested in the President.
2. The executive power shall be exercised by the President only through officers subordinate to him.
3. The supreme command of the defence forces of the Union shall be vested in the President.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the powers of the President of India is/are correct? 1. The executive power of the Union shall be vested in the President. 2. The executive power shall be exercised by the President only through officers subordinate to him. 3. The supreme command of the defence forces of the Union shall be vested in the President. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. Under **Article 53**, the **executive power** and the **supreme command of the defence forces** are vested in the President.
Statement 2 is incorrect because the executive power can be exercised by the President either **directly or through officers subordinate to him** (not *only* through officers).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding Article 21 of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. Article 21 is violated when the under-trial prisoners are detained under judicial custody for an indefinite period.
2. Right to life is one of the basic human rights and not even the State has the authority to violate that right.
3. Under Article 21, the right of a woman to make reproductive choices is not a dimension of personal liberty.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding Article 21 of the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. Article 21 is violated when the under-trial prisoners are detained under judicial custody for an indefinite period. 2. Right to life is one of the basic human rights and not even the State has the authority to violate that right. 3. Under Article 21, the right of a woman to make reproductive choices is not a dimension of personal liberty. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. Detaining under-trials indefinitely violates the right to a **speedy trial** inherent in Article 21. The right to life is absolute against arbitrary State action.
Statement 3 is incorrect because the Supreme Court has ruled that a woman's right to make **reproductive choices IS a fundamental dimension of personal liberty** under Article 21.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Right to Education in India?
1. Free and compulsory education should be provided to all children of the age of six to fourteen years.
2. The imperative of the provision of the Right to Education Act, 2009 is that schools must have qualified teachers and basic infrastructure.
3. There should be quality education without any discrimination on the ground of economic, social and cultural background.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Right to Education in India? 1. Free and compulsory education should be provided to all children of the age of six to fourteen years. 2. The imperative of the provision of the Right to Education Act, 2009 is that schools must have qualified teachers and basic infrastructure. 3. There should be quality education without any discrimination on the ground of economic, social and cultural background. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Right to Education (Article 21A and RTE Act, 2009)** mandates **free and compulsory education** for children aged 6-14, ensuring **quality, non-discriminatory education** supported by **qualified teachers and adequate infrastructure**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding freedom to manage religious affairs as per the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding freedom to manage religious affairs as per the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: Every religious denomination shall have the right to manage its own affairs in matters of religion except some minor communities.
options.b: Every religion or any section thereof shall have the right to own and acquire movable and immovable property.
options.c: Every religious community has the right to establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes.
options.d: Every community has the right to manage its own affairs in matters of religion.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Every religious denomination shall have the right to manage its own affairs in matters of religion except some minor communities. Article 26** grants the freedom to manage religious affairs to **every religious denomination**, without any exception for "minor communities." The rights are subject only to public order, morality, and health.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements relating to protection against arrest and detention of individuals under Article 22 is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements relating to protection against arrest and detention of individuals under Article 22 is not correct?

options.a: No person who is arrested shall be detained in custody without being informed of the grounds for such arrest.
