options.b: No person shall be denied the right to consult, and be defended by, a legal practitioner of his/her choice.
options.c: Every person who is arrested and detained in custody shall be produced before the nearest Magistrate within a period of one week of such arrest.
options.d: The right to protection against arrest is not available to a person in jail pursuant to a judicial order.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Every person who is arrested and detained in custody shall be produced before the nearest Magistrate within a period of one week of such arrest.** Under **Article 22(2)**, an arrested person must be produced before the nearest Magistrate within **24 hours** of arrest, not one week.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
1. The Preamble by itself is not enforceable in a Court of Law.
2. The Preamble states the objectives which the Constitution seeks to establish and promote.
3. The Preamble indicates the source from which the Constitution derives its authority.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the Preamble of the Indian Constitution? 1. The Preamble by itself is not enforceable in a Court of Law. 2. The Preamble states the objectives which the Constitution seeks to establish and promote. 3. The Preamble indicates the source from which the Constitution derives its authority. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The Preamble derives its authority from the people ("We, the People"), outlines the core **objectives (Justice, Liberty, Equality, Fraternity)**, and is **non-justiciable** (cannot be directly enforced in a court of law).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements relating to cultural and educational rights in India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements relating to cultural and educational rights in India is not correct?

options.a: Every section of the citizens has the right to conserve its language, script or culture.
options.b: No citizen shall be denied admission into any educational institution maintained by the State or receiving aid out of State funds on grounds of religion, race or language.
options.c: The State shall, in granting aid to educational institutions, discriminate against any educational institution on the ground that it is under the management of a majority community.
options.d: All minorities, whether based on religion or language, shall have the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choic

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The State shall, in granting aid to educational institutions, discriminate against any educational institution on the ground that it is under the management of a majority community. Article 30(2)** specifically prohibits the State from discriminating against any educational institution (whether minority or majority managed) while granting aid.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following statements relating to the Directive Principles of State Policy is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements relating to the Directive Principles of State Policy is not correct?

options.a: The provisions contained in Part IV of the Constitution of India shall not be enforceable by any Court.
options.b: The Directive Principles of State Policy are fundamental in the governance of the country.
options.c: It shall be the duty of the State to apply the Directive Principles in making laws.
options.d: The Directive Principles are directed in making India an advanced capitalist country of the world.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Directive Principles are directed in making India an advanced capitalist country of the world.** The **DPSP** are fundamentally aimed at establishing a **welfare state** based on socialistic, Gandhian, and liberal-intellectual principles, not at building an advanced capitalist country.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not correct regarding the Office of the Vice President of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct regarding the Office of the Vice President of India?

options.a: The Vice President is elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both the Houses of the Parliament.
options.b: The Vice President is elected in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote.
options.c: The Vice President shall not be a member of either House of the Parliament or of a House of the Legislature of any State.
options.d: The Vice President of India shall be ex officio Chairman of the Council of States and shall not hold any office of profit.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Vice President is elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both the Houses of the Parliament.** The electoral college for the Vice-President consists of **both elected AND nominated members** of both Houses of Parliament, unlike the President's electoral college which excludes nominated members.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following constitutional authorities inquires and decides in case of doubts and disputes arising out of election of the President and Vice President of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following constitutional authorities inquires and decides in case of doubts and disputes arising out of election of the President and Vice President of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Supreme Court of India**. Under **Article 71**, all doubts and disputes arising out of or in connection with the election of a President or Vice-President shall be inquired into and decided exclusively by the **Supreme Court**, whose decision is final.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following is / are Constitutional Body / Bodies?
1. National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
2. National Commission for Women
3. National Commission for Minorities
4. National Human Rights Commission
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following is / are Constitutional Body / Bodies? 1. National Commission for Scheduled Tribes 2. National Commission for Women 3. National Commission for Minorities 4. National Human Rights Commission Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. Only the **National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (Article 338A)** is a constitutional body. The NCW, NCM, and NHRC are all **statutory bodies** created by respective acts of Parliament.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements :
1. The President of India shall have the power to appoint and remove the Speaker of Lok Sabha

2. The Speaker has to discharge the functions of his office himself throughout his term and cannot delegate his functions to the Deputy Speaker during his absence from the station or during his illness
Which of the statements given above is / are correct ?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements : 1. The President of India shall have the power to appoint and remove the Speaker of Lok Sabha 2. The Speaker has to discharge the functions of his office himself throughout his term and cannot delegate his functions to the Deputy Speaker during his absence from the station or during his illness Which of the statements given above is / are correct ?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **2 only**. The Speaker is **elected and removed by the members of the Lok Sabha**, not the President. While the Deputy Speaker presides when the Speaker is absent from a sitting, the overarching administrative functions of the Speaker's office are non-delegable during general absences. *(

Note: The UPSC official key marked (b) as correct, despite nuanced parliamentary conventions).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Second Administrative Reforms Commission (2005) was concerned with :
questions.questionText: The Second Administrative Reforms Commission (2005) was concerned with :

options.a: Reforms in institutional arrangements for good governance
options.b: Reforms in the Indian Penal Code and the Criminal Justice System
options.c: Creating an ombudsman mechanism for reduction of corruption in public life
options.d: Devising new measures for urban governance and management

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **reforms in institutional arrangements for good governance**. The **Second Administrative Reforms Commission (ARC)**, constituted in 2005 under Veerappa Moily, was established to prepare a detailed blueprint for revamping the public administration system to achieve **good governance**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: As per the Constitution of India, the Writ of Prohibition relates to an order :
1. issued against judicial and quasi-judicial authority
2. to prohibit an inferior Court from proceeding in a particular case where it has no jurisdiction to try
3. to restrain a person from holding a public office to which he is not entitled
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: As per the Constitution of India, the Writ of Prohibition relates to an order : 1. issued against judicial and quasi-judicial authority 2. to prohibit an inferior Court from proceeding in a particular case where it has no jurisdiction to try 3. to restrain a person from holding a public office to which he is not entitled Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The **Writ of Prohibition** is issued exclusively against **judicial and quasi-judicial authorities** to stop them from exceeding their jurisdiction. Restraining a person from holding a public office unlawfully is achieved through the writ of **Quo Warranto**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Who among the following Prime Ministers of India were defeated by a vote of No Confidence ?
1. Morarji Desai
2. Viswanath Pratap Singh
3. H.D. Deve Gowda
4. Atal Bihari Vajpayee
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Who among the following Prime Ministers of India were defeated by a vote of No Confidence ? 1. Morarji Desai 2. Viswanath Pratap Singh 3. H.D. Deve Gowda 4. Atal Bihari Vajpayee Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2, 3 and 4 only**. **V.P. Singh, H.D. Deve Gowda, and Atal Bihari Vajpayee** were defeated on the floor of the House by a vote of no confidence. **Morarji Desai** resigned before the no-confidence motion could be put to a vote.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding Rajya Sabha is / are correct ?
1. The maximum permissible strength of Rajya Sabha is 250
2. In Rajya Sabha, 238 members are elected indirectly from the States and Union Territories
3. It shares legislative powers equally with Lok Sabha in matters such as creation of All India Services
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding Rajya Sabha is / are correct ? 1. The maximum permissible strength of Rajya Sabha is 250 2. In Rajya Sabha, 238 members are elected indirectly from the States and Union Territories 3. It shares legislative powers equally with Lok Sabha in matters such as creation of All India Services Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The **maximum strength is 250** (238 indirectly elected and 12 nominated).

Statement 3 is incorrect because the Rajya Sabha does not share "equal" power regarding All India Services; under **Article 312**, the Rajya Sabha has **exclusive power** to authorize the creation of a new All India Service.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the office of the President of India are correct ?
1. The President has the power to grant pardon to a criminal in special cases
2. The President can promulgate ordinances even when the Parliament is in session
3. The President can dissolve the Rajya Sabha during emergency
4. The President has the power to nominate two members in the Lok Sabha from the Anglo Indian community
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the office of the President of India are correct ? 1. The President has the power to grant pardon to a criminal in special cases 2. The President can promulgate ordinances even when the Parliament is in session 3. The President can dissolve the Rajya Sabha during emergency 4. The President has the power to nominate two members in the Lok Sabha from the Anglo Indian community Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 4 only**. The President can grant **pardons (Article 72)** and, prior to the 104th Amendment, could **nominate two Anglo-Indians** to the Lok Sabha. The President **cannot** promulgate ordinances while Parliament is in session, and the **Rajya Sabha is a permanent house** that cannot be dissolved, even during an emergency.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the statements given below is / are correct ?
1. The ideal of a common civil code is set forth in Article 44 of the Constitution of India
2. In certain respects, the High Courts in India have been given more extensive powers than the Supreme Court
3. The Supreme Court of India, the first fully independent Court for the country, was set up under the Constitution of India in 1950
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the statements given below is / are correct ? 1. The ideal of a common civil code is set forth in Article 44 of the Constitution of India 2. In certain respects, the High Courts in India have been given more extensive powers than the Supreme Court 3. The Supreme Court of India, the first fully independent Court for the country, was set up under the Constitution of India in 1950 Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Uniform Civil Code is in Article 44**. High Courts have **wider writ jurisdiction (Article 226)** than the Supreme Court (which is restricted to Fundamental Rights under Article 32). The **Supreme Court was formally established in 1950** as the apex independent court.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which one of the following Articles / Schedules in the Constitution of India deals with Autonomous District Councils ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Articles / Schedules in the Constitution of India deals with Autonomous District Councils ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Sixth Schedule**. The **Sixth Schedule** provides for the administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram through the creation of **Autonomous District Councils (ADCs)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements about Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) are correct ?
1. The CAG will hold office for a period of six years from the date he assumes the office. He shall vacate office on attaining the age of 65 years, if earlier than the expiry of the 6 year term
2. The powers of CAG are derived from the Constitution of India
3. The CAG is a multi-member body appointed by the President of India in consultation with the Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers
4. The CAG may be removed by the President only on an address from both Houses of Parliament, on the grounds of proved misbehavior or incapacity
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) are correct ? 1. The CAG will hold office for a period of six years from the date he assumes the office. He shall vacate office on attaining the age of 65 years, if earlier than the expiry of the 6 year term 2. The powers of CAG are derived from the Constitution of India 3. The CAG is a multi-member body appointed by the President of India in consultation with the Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers 4. The CAG may be removed by the President only on an address from both Houses of Parliament, on the grounds of proved misbehavior or incapacity Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. The **CAG** holds a tenure of **6 years or up to age 65**, derives powers from the **Constitution (Article 148)**, and is removed in the **same manner as a Supreme Court judge**.
Statement 3 is incorrect because the CAG is a **single-member institution**, not a multi-member body.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 are correct ?
1. The Act recognizes forest rights of forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes who have been occupying the forest land before October 25, 1980
2. The onus of implementation of the Act lies at the level of the State / UT Governments
3. The Act seeks to recognize and vest certain forest rights in the forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and other traditional forest dwellers
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006 are correct ? 1. The Act recognizes forest rights of forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes who have been occupying the forest land before October 25, 1980 2. The onus of implementation of the Act lies at the level of the State / UT Governments 3. The Act seeks to recognize and vest certain forest rights in the forest dwelling Scheduled Tribes and other traditional forest dwellers Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Forest Rights Act 2006** vests and recognizes traditional forest rights, establishes a cutoff date, and places the crucial **onus of implementation on the State/UT Governments** starting at the Gram Sabha level.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: In
which one of the following judgments of the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court of India, the 'rarest of rare' principle in the award of death penalty was first laid down ?
questions.questionText: In which one of the following judgments of the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court of India, the 'rarest of rare' principle in the award of death penalty was first laid down ?

options.a: Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab (1980)
options.c: Dr. Upendra Baxi v. State of UP (1983)

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab (1980)**. The landmark Supreme Court judgment in **Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab** established the famous **'rarest of rare' doctrine**, limiting the imposition of the death penalty to highly exceptional cases.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Consider the following statements about the President of India:
1. The President has the right to address and send message to the Council of Ministers to elicit specific information
2. The President can call for information relating to proposals for legislation
3. All decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to administration of the Union must be communicated to the President
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about the President of India: 1. The President has the right to address and send message to the Council of Ministers to elicit specific information 2. The President can call for information relating to proposals for legislation 3. All decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to administration of the Union must be communicated to the President Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The President has the constitutional right to be informed. Under **Article 78**, it is the duty of the Prime Minister to **communicate all administrative decisions**, furnish **information relating to legislation**, and the President can **send messages** to either House or the Council of Ministers.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: In
which one of the following cases, the Constitutional validity of the Muslim Women (Protection of Rights of Divorce) Act 1986, was upheld by the Supreme Court of India ?
questions.questionText: In which one of the following cases, the Constitutional validity of the Muslim Women (Protection of Rights of Divorce) Act 1986, was upheld by the Supreme Court of India ?

options.a: Muhammad Ahmed Khan v. Shah Bano Begum
options.b: Danial Latifi v. Union of India
options.c: Mary Roy v. State of Kerala
options.d: Shankari Prasad v. Union of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Danial Latifi v. Union of India**. In the **Danial Latifi case (2001)**, the Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of the **Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act, 1986**, ruling that a Muslim husband's liability to provide maintenance extends beyond the iddat period.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to preventive detention in India is / are correct ?
1. The detenue has no rights other than those mentioned in clauses (4) and (5) of Article 22 of Constitution of India
2. The detenue has a right to challenge the detention order on the ground that he was already in jail when the detention order was passed
3. The detenue can claim bail on the ground that he has been in prison beyond twenty-four hours without an order of the magistrate
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to preventive detention in India is / are correct ? 1. The detenue has no rights other than those mentioned in clauses (4) and (5) of Article 22 of Constitution of India 2. The detenue has a right to challenge the detention order on the ground that he was already in jail when the detention order was passed 3. The detenue can claim bail on the ground that he has been in prison beyond twenty-four hours without an order of the magistrate Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. A detenue can legally challenge a detention order if already incarcerated (as it shows non-application of mind). The right to be presented before a magistrate within **24 hours strictly applies to punitive detention**, not preventive detention (making statement 3 incorrect).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: A Member of Lok Sabha does not become disqualified to continue as a Member of the House if the Member:
questions.questionText: A Member of Lok Sabha does not become disqualified to continue as a Member of the House if the Member:

options.a: Voluntarily gives up his / her membership of the political party from which he / she was elected
options.b: Is expelled by the political party from which he / she had been elected to the House
options.c: Joins a political party after being elected as an independent candidate
options.d: Abstains from voting contrary to the direction by his / her political party

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **is expelled by the political party from which he / she had been elected to the House**. Under the **Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law)**, expulsion from the party by the high command does **not automatically disqualify** an MP from their seat in the Lok Sabha, though voluntarily giving up membership or defying a whip does.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVII: Official Language
statementLines: Which one of the following languages is not recognized in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following languages is not recognized in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **English**. The **Eighth Schedule** currently recognizes 22 official languages. **English is not included** in the Eighth Schedule, whereas Sanskrit, Urdu, and Nepali are recognized.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: The category of 'Overseas Citizens of India' was entered in the Citizenship Act of India through an amendment in the year :
questions.questionText: The category of 'Overseas Citizens of India' was entered in the Citizenship Act of India through an amendment in the year :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2003**. The **Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2003** formally introduced the category of **Overseas Citizens of India (OCI)**, granting certain privileges to Persons of Indian Origin (PIOs) from specified countries.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: The Right to Education was added to the Fundamental Rights in the Constitution of India through the :
questions.questionText: The Right to Education was added to the Fundamental Rights in the Constitution of India through the :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Constitution (86th Amendment) Act, 2002**. The **86th Amendment** inserted **Article 21A**, formally making the right to free and compulsory education for children aged 6 to 14 a Fundamental Right.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court rule that Constitutional Amendments were also laws under Article 13 of the Constitution of India, which could be declared void for being inconsistent with Fundamental Rights?
questions.questionText: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court rule that Constitutional Amendments were also laws under Article 13 of the Constitution of India, which could be declared void for being inconsistent with Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Golaknath Case**. In the historic **Golaknath v. State of Punjab (1967)** case, the Supreme Court ruled that a constitutional amendment under Article 368 constitutes a "law" within the meaning of **Article 13**, and therefore, Parliament cannot abridge Fundamental Rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which of the following statement(s) is/are not correct for the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India?
1. It was inserted by the first amendment in 1951.
2. It includes those laws which are beyond the purview of judicial review.
3. It was inserted by the 42nd Amendment.
4. The laws in the Ninth Schedule are primarily those which pertain to the matters of national security.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statement(s) is/are not correct for the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India? 1. It was inserted by the first amendment in 1951. 2. It includes those laws which are beyond the purview of judicial review. 3. It was inserted by the 42nd Amendment. 4. The laws in the Ninth Schedule are primarily those which pertain to the matters of national security. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 and 4**. The **Ninth Schedule** was inserted by the **First Amendment (1951)** (not the 42nd) and primarily pertained to **land reforms and agrarian laws** (not national security) to protect them from judicial review. Therefore, statements 3 and 4 are incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following categories of persons is not treated at par so far as the availability of Fundamental Rights is concerned?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following categories of persons is not treated at par so far as the availability of Fundamental Rights is concerned?

options.b: Members of the forces charged with the responsibility of maintenance of public order
options.c: Members of the forces employed in connection with the communication systems set up in the country
options.d: Members of the forces employed in connection with the communication systems set up for maintenance of public order

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Members of the forces employed in connection with the communication systems set up in the country**. Under **Article 33**, Parliament can restrict Fundamental Rights for armed forces, police, and personnel employed in communication systems *specifically set up for these forces/public order*, but **not general civilian communication systems** in the country.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Notification regarding commencement or cessation of a state of war is the responsibility of
questions.questionText: Notification regarding commencement or cessation of a state of war is the responsibility of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Ministry of External Affairs**. Diplomatic notifications, including the formal declaration of the **commencement or cessation of a state of war** with a foreign nation, fall under the executive domain of the **Ministry of External Affairs**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: A Parliamentary Democracy is one where
1. a balance of popular participation and elite rule takes place.
2. the government is responsible not to the public but to the elected representatives.
3. the parliamentarians are delegated the responsibility of thinking and acting on behalf of their constituents.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: A Parliamentary Democracy is one where 1. a balance of popular participation and elite rule takes place. 2. the government is responsible not to the public but to the elected representatives. 3. the parliamentarians are delegated the responsibility of thinking and acting on behalf of their constituents. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. In a **Parliamentary Democracy**, representatives are elected to act on behalf of constituents, creating a balance of participation. Crucially, the executive (government) is **directly responsible to the legislature (elected representatives)**, and only indirectly to the general public.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: When martial law is imposed, Parliament cannot make law in respect of
which one of the following matters?
questions.questionText: When martial law is imposed, Parliament cannot make law in respect of which one of the following matters?

options.a: Indemnify any person in respect of any act done by him in connection with the maintenance of order in the area where martial law was in force
options.b: Parliament can by law validate any sentence passed when martial law was in force in the area
options.c: A law of Parliament can validate forfeiture ordered when martial law was in force in the area
options.d: Any act done under martial law can be validated by Parliament by law

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Any act done under martial law can be validated by Parliament by law**. Under **Article 34**, Parliament has sweeping powers to indemnify officers and validate sentences/forfeitures made under martial law. However, interpreting the strict bounds of the option framing, the official answer key determined (d).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which among the following features of a federal system is not found in the Indian Political System?
questions.questionText: Which among the following features of a federal system is not found in the Indian Political System?

options.b: Distribution of powers between the Federal and the State Governments
options.d: Authority of the Courts to interpret the Constitution

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Dual citizenship**. Unlike traditional federations like the USA, the Indian Constitution establishes a federal structure but strictly provides for a **single citizenship** for the entire country to promote fraternity.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following is not true of Article 32 of the Indian Constitution?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is not true of Article 32 of the Indian Constitution?

options.a: It gives the Supreme Court and the High Courts the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights.
options.b: It is included in Part III of the Indian Constitution and is therefore itself a Fundamental Right.
options.c: Dr. Ambedkar called it the 'very soul of the Indian Constitution'.
options.d: An aggrieved person has no right to complain under Article 32 where a Fundamental Right has not been violated.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It gives the Supreme Court and the High Courts the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Article 32** explicitly grants writ jurisdiction *only* to the **Supreme Court**. The writ jurisdiction of the High Courts is separately derived from **Article 226**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.a: The Central Government is empowered to issue a notification to specify any service in a State as a service of vital importance to the community.
options.b: Such a notification remains valid for six months.
options.c: Every command given by a superior officer casts a duty on all persons subject to the Army Act, 1950, the Air Force Act, 1950 or the Navy Act, 1957 to obey the command when such a notification is in force.
options.d: The provisions of the Armed Forces (Emergency Duties) Act, 1947 are applicable in connection with vital services imposed in an emergency on the armed forces.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Such a notification remains valid for six months.** Under the relevant emergency service frameworks, initial notifications declaring a service as vital typically have a much shorter validity period (e.g., 1 month), preventing prolonged arbitrary suspensions.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Central Industrial Security Force is under the administrative control of which of the following ministries?
questions.questionText: The Central Industrial Security Force is under the administrative control of which of the following ministries?

options.a: Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises
options.b: Headquarters of the Integrated Joint Staff

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Ministry of Home Affairs**. The **Central Industrial Security Force (CISF)** is one of the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) and functions under the administrative control of the **Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which of the following is/are not the characteristic(s) of the First Past The Post System (FPTP)?
1. It is a majoritarian system where minorities are likely to remain unrepresented.
2. A candidate may win an election even if he/she gets less than the majority of the votes cast.
3. It generates proportionality between the votes cast and the seats won.
4. It always leads to a two party system and a stable and accountable government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not the characteristic(s) of the First Past The Post System (FPTP)? 1. It is a majoritarian system where minorities are likely to remain unrepresented. 2. A candidate may win an election even if he/she gets less than the majority of the votes cast. 3. It generates proportionality between the votes cast and the seats won. 4. It always leads to a two party system and a stable and accountable government. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 and 4**. The FPTP system does **not generate proportionality** (a party can win a majority of seats with a minority of total votes), and it **does not always lead to a two-party system**, as visibly demonstrated by India's multi-party democracy.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are not true for the category of the Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) inserted by the amendment to the Citizenship Act of India in 2003?
1. It gives dual citizenship to Persons of Indian Origin (PIO) who are citizens of another country.
2. It gives Persons of Indian Origin (PIO) who are citizens of another country, an OCI card without citizenship.
3. It permits the OCI to vote in general elections in India.
4. It allows the OCI to travel to India without visa.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are not true for the category of the Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) inserted by the amendment to the Citizenship Act of India in 2003? 1. It gives dual citizenship to Persons of Indian Origin (PIO) who are citizens of another country. 2. It gives Persons of Indian Origin (PIO) who are citizens of another country, an OCI card without citizenship. 3. It permits the OCI to vote in general elections in India. 4. It allows the OCI to travel to India without visa. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3**. The OCI scheme does **not grant dual citizenship** (India strictly prohibits dual citizenship). Consequently, an OCI cardholder is **not permitted to vote** in general elections in India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The power to decide the date of an election to a State Legislative Assembly rests with the
questions.questionText: The power to decide the date of an election to a State Legislative Assembly rests with the
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Election Commission of India**. Under **Article 324**, the superintendence, direction, and control of elections, including deciding the specific **dates and schedules** for State Legislative Assembly elections, is vested entirely in the **Election Commission of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Information under the RTI Act, 2005 can be provided in respect of
questions.questionText: Information under the RTI Act, 2005 can be provided in respect of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Border Road Organisation**. While intelligence and security agencies like the Assam Rifles and National Security Council Secretariat are largely exempted under the Second Schedule of the **RTI Act, 2005**, the **Border Road Organisation (BRO)** generally falls under its purview for providing information (subject to specific exemptions).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who acts as the chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee?
questions.questionText: Who acts as the chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee?

options.a: The President of India in his capacity as the commander-in-chief

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The senior-most Chief of Staff**. Historically and structurally, the Chairman of the **Chiefs of Staff Committee (CoSC)** is the **senior-most Chief of Staff** among the three defense services (Army, Navy, Air Force).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: The Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions for the administration of Tribal areas. Which of the following States is not covered under this Schedule?
questions.questionText: The Sixth Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions for the administration of Tribal areas. Which of the following States is not covered under this Schedule?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Manipur**. The **Sixth Schedule** contains special provisions for the administration of tribal areas exclusively in the four states of **Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram** (AMTM). It does not cover Manipur.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Supreme Court guidelines issued in the Vishaka Case pertain to
questions.questionText: The Supreme Court guidelines issued in the Vishaka Case pertain to

options.c: Sexual harassment at the work place

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **sexual harassment at the work place**. The landmark **Vishaka guidelines (1997)** were formulated by the Supreme Court to provide a legal framework and procedures to prevent and address **sexual harassment of women at the workplace**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Fundamental Rights, enshrined in Part III of the Constitution of India, "are inherent and cannot be extinguished by any constitutional and statutory provision". This was made clear in the case of
questions.questionText: The Fundamental Rights, enshrined in Part III of the Constitution of India, "are inherent and cannot be extinguished by any constitutional and statutory provision". This was made clear in the case of

options.a: A.K. Gopalan v. State of Madras
options.b: I.C. Golak Nath v. State of Punjab
options.c: State of West Bengal v. Committee for Protection of Democratic Rights, West Bengal
options.d: Shankari Prasad Singh Deo v. Union of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **I.C. Golak Nath v. State of Punjab**. In the historic **Golak Nath case (1967)**, the Supreme Court ruled that **Fundamental Rights are inherent** and hold a transcendental position, meaning Parliament has no power to amend Part III to take away or abridge these rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following principles is/are taken into consideration by the Speaker while recognizing a parliamentary party or group?
1. An association of members who have an organization both inside and outside the House
2. An association of members who shall have at least one-third of the total number of members of the House
3. An association of members who have a distinct programme of parliamentary work
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following principles is/are taken into consideration by the Speaker while recognizing a parliamentary party or group? 1. An association of members who have an organization both inside and outside the House 2. An association of members who shall have at least one-third of the total number of members of the House 3. An association of members who have a distinct programme of parliamentary work Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. To be recognized as a parliamentary group, the association of members must have an **organization both inside and outside the House** and a **distinct programme of parliamentary work**. The numerical requirement of "one-third" is not a condition for basic recognition as a party/group.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding the Departmental Committee of the Parliament of India on the empowerment of women is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding the Departmental Committee of the Parliament of India on the empowerment of women is correct?

options.a: The Committee will consist of members of the Lok Sabha only
options.b: A Cabinet Minister can be a member of the Committee
options.c: The term of office of the members of the Committee shall not exceed two years
options.d: It reports on the working of welfare programmes for the women

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It reports on the working of welfare programmes for the women**. The **Committee on Empowerment of Women** acts as a Joint Parliamentary Committee with members from both Houses. A **Minister is expressly barred** from being a member. Its key mandate is to scrutinize and report on the working of welfare programmes for women.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The Annual Financial Statement of the Government of India in respect of each financial year shall be presented to the House on such day as the
questions.questionText: The Annual Financial Statement of the Government of India in respect of each financial year shall be presented to the House on such day as the
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **President of India may direct**. Under **Article 112**, the **President of India** shall cause the **Annual Financial Statement** (Budget) to be laid before both Houses of Parliament on such day as he/she may direct.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The subject matter of an adjournment motion in the Parliament
1. must be directly related to the conduct of the Union Government
2. may involve failure of the Government of India to perform its duties in accordance with the Constitution
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The subject matter of an adjournment motion in the Parliament 1. must be directly related to the conduct of the Union Government 2. may involve failure of the Government of India to perform its duties in accordance with the Constitution Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. An **Adjournment Motion** is moved to discuss a definite matter of urgent public importance. It is not exclusively restricted to the "direct conduct" of the Union Government but crucially involves the **failure of the Government of India to perform its duties** in accordance with the Constitution or law.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Political theory
1. deals with the ideas and principles that shape Constitutions
2. clarifies the meaning of freedom, equality and justice
3. probes the significance of principles of rule of law, separation of power and judicial review
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Political theory 1. deals with the ideas and principles that shape Constitutions 2. clarifies the meaning of freedom, equality and justice 3. probes the significance of principles of rule of law, separation of power and judicial review Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. **Political theory** provides the philosophical foundation of politics. It comprehensively deals with the ideas shaping **Constitutions**, defines core values like **freedom, equality, and justice**, and analytically probes structural concepts like the **rule of law and separation of powers**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements about Democracy:
1. It consists with the formation of a government elected by the people.
2. In democracy, those currently in power have a fair chance of losing.
3. Each vote has one value.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about Democracy: 1. It consists with the formation of a government elected by the people. 2. In democracy, those currently in power have a fair chance of losing. 3. Each vote has one value. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. A robust **democracy** is characterized by a **government elected by the people**, competitive elections where incumbents have a **fair chance of losing**, and political equality ensuring **each vote has one equal value**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: The citizenship means
1. full civil and political rights of the citizens
2. the right of suffrage for election to the House of the People (of the Union) and the Legislative Assembly of every State
3. the right to become a Member of the Parliament and Member of Legislative Assemblies
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The citizenship means 1. full civil and political rights of the citizens 2. the right of suffrage for election to the House of the People (of the Union) and the Legislative Assembly of every State 3. the right to become a Member of the Parliament and Member of Legislative Assemblies Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. In the Indian context, holding **citizenship** confers **full civil and political rights**, guarantees the **right of suffrage (voting)** in elections, and explicitly grants the right to **contest and become a member** of Parliament or State Legislatures.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The Committee on Public Accounts under the Constitution of India is meant for
1. the examination of accounts showing the appropriation of sums granted by the House for the expenditure of the Government of India
2. scrutinising the report of the Comptroller and Auditor General
3. suggesting the form in which estimates shall be presented to the Parliament
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The Committee on Public Accounts under the Constitution of India is meant for 1. the examination of accounts showing the appropriation of sums granted by the House for the expenditure of the Government of India 2. scrutinising the report of the Comptroller and Auditor General 3. suggesting the form in which estimates shall be presented to the Parliament Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The primary function of the **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** is to examine the **appropriation accounts** and to deeply scrutinize the audit reports submitted by the **Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG)**. Suggesting the form of estimates is the duty of the **Estimates Committee**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which among the following was the reason of the resignations of the Indian ministers in all the provinces in the year 1939?
questions.questionText: Which among the following was the reason of the resignations of the Indian ministers in all the provinces in the year 1939?

options.a: The Governors refused to act as constitutional heads
options.b: The Centre did not provide the required financial help to provinces
options.c: The Governor-General converted Indian administration from federal to unitary one because of the beginning of the Second World War
options.d: India was declared a party to the Second World War without the consent of the provincial governments

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **India was declared a party to the Second World War without the consent of the provincial governments**. In 1939, the Viceroy Lord Linlithgow unilaterally declared India a belligerent in World War II. In protest against this lack of consultation, the **Congress ministries in the provinces tendered their resignations**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: The legislative power of the Parliament includes making laws
1. on matters not enumerated in the Concurrent List and State List
2. in respect of entries in the State List if two or more State Legislatures consider it desirable
3. for implementing any treaty, agreement or convention with any country even if it falls in the State List
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The legislative power of the Parliament includes making laws 1. on matters not enumerated in the Concurrent List and State List 2. in respect of entries in the State List if two or more State Legislatures consider it desirable 3. for implementing any treaty, agreement or convention with any country even if it falls in the State List Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The Parliament holds **residuary powers** (Article 248), can legislate on State List subjects if requested by **two or more states (Article 252)**, and has the absolute power to legislate on any subject (even State List) to **implement international treaties (Article 253)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: There are provisions in the Constitution of India which empower the Parliament to modify or annul the operation of certain provisions of the Constitution without actually amending them. They include
1. any law made under Article 2 (relating to admission or establishment of new States)
2. any law made under Article 3 (relating to formation of new States)
3. amendment of First Schedule and Fourth Schedule
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: There are provisions in the Constitution of India which empower the Parliament to modify or annul the operation of certain provisions of the Constitution without actually amending them. They include 1. any law made under Article 2 (relating to admission or establishment of new States) 2. any law made under Article 3 (relating to formation of new States) 3. amendment of First Schedule and Fourth Schedule Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Article 4** of the Constitution, laws made under **Article 2** and **Article 3** that provide for the amendment of the **First and Fourth Schedules** are expressly stated **not to be deemed as constitutional amendments** for the purposes of Article 368.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The functions of the Committee on Estimates, as incorporated in the Constitution of India, shall be to
1. report what economies, improvements in organization, efficiency or administrative reform may be effected
2. suggest alternative policies in order to bring about efficiency and economy in administration
3. examine whether the money is well laid out within the limits of the policy implied in the estimates
4. examine the reports, if any, of the Comptroller and Auditor General on the public undertakings
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The functions of the Committee on Estimates, as incorporated in the Constitution of India, shall be to 1. report what economies, improvements in organization, efficiency or administrative reform may be effected 2. suggest alternative policies in order to bring about efficiency and economy in administration 3. examine whether the money is well laid out within the limits of the policy implied in the estimates 4. examine the reports, if any, of the Comptroller and Auditor General on the public undertakings Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Estimates Committee** reports on **economies and improvements**, suggests **alternative policies**, and verifies if money is well laid out within policy limits. Examining the CAG reports on public undertakings is the specific mandate of the **Committee on Public Undertakings**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: The principle of 'collective responsibility' under parliamentary democracy implies that
1. a motion of no-confidence can be moved in the Council of Ministers as a whole as well as an individual minister
2. no person shall be nominated to the Cabinet except on the advice of the Prime Minister
3. no person shall be retained as a member of the Cabinet if the Prime Minister says that he shall be dismissed
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The principle of 'collective responsibility' under parliamentary democracy implies that 1. a motion of no-confidence can be moved in the Council of Ministers as a whole as well as an individual minister 2. no person shall be nominated to the Cabinet except on the advice of the Prime Minister 3. no person shall be retained as a member of the Cabinet if the Prime Minister says that he shall be dismissed Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3**. **Collective responsibility** means the Council sinks or swims together. A **no-confidence motion** can only be moved against the **entire Council of Ministers**, never against an individual minister. The **Prime Minister's supreme position** dictates that ministers are appointed and dismissed solely on their advice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Forming a cooperative society is a Fundamental Right in India.
2. Cooperative societies do not fall within the ambit of the Right to Information Act, 2005.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Forming a cooperative society is a Fundamental Right in India. 2. Cooperative societies do not fall within the ambit of the Right to Information Act, 2005. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **97th Amendment Act (2011)** elevated the right to form a **cooperative society to a Fundamental Right** under Article 19(1)(c). Additionally, the Supreme Court has ruled that private cooperative societies **do not fall under the RTI Act** as they are not public authorities unless substantially funded by the government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Certain Bills cannot be introduced or proceeded with unless the recommendation of the President is received. However, no recommendation is required in some other cases. In
which one of the following cases such recommendation is not required?
questions.questionText: Certain Bills cannot be introduced or proceeded with unless the recommendation of the President is received. However, no recommendation is required in some other cases. In which one of the following cases such recommendation is not required?

options.a: For introduction of Bills and for moving amendments relating to financial matters
options.b: For introduction of a Bill relating to formation of new States or of alteration of areas of existing States
options.c: For moving of an amendment making provision for the reduction or abolition of any tax
options.d: For introduction of a Bill or moving of an amendment affecting taxation in which States are interested

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **For moving of an amendment making provision for the reduction or abolition of any tax**. While the introduction of new taxes or increasing existing taxes requires the prior recommendation of the President, an amendment that seeks merely to **reduce or abolish a tax does not require such prior recommendation**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: After a Bill has been passed by the Houses of the Parliament, it is presented to the President who may either give assent to the Bill or withhold his assent. The President may
questions.questionText: After a Bill has been passed by the Houses of the Parliament, it is presented to the President who may either give assent to the Bill or withhold his assent. The President may

options.b: Assent or reject the Bill as soon as possible
options.c: Return the Bill as soon as possible after the Bill is presented to him with a message requesting the House to reconsider the Bill
options.d: Withhold his assent even if the Bill is passed again by the Houses

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **return the Bill as soon as possible after the Bill is presented to him with a message requesting the House to reconsider the Bill**. Under **Article 111**, the President has the suspensive veto power to **return an ordinary bill** to Parliament for reconsideration. If passed again, the President is bound to give assent.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: "The Draft Constitution as framed only provides a machinery for the government of the country. It is not a contrivance to install any particular party in power as has been done in some countries. Who should be in power is left to be determined by the people, as it must be, if the system is to satisfy the test of democracy." The above passage from Constituent Assembly debates is attributed to
questions.questionText: "The Draft Constitution as framed only provides a machinery for the government of the country. It is not a contrivance to install any particular party in power as has been done in some countries. Who should be in power is left to be determined by the people, as it must be, if the system is to satisfy the test of democracy." The above passage from Constituent Assembly debates is attributed to
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Dr. B. R. Ambedkar**. This profound quote affirming that the Constitution provides a neutral democratic machinery, leaving the choice of the ruling party entirely to the electorate, was delivered by **Dr. B. R. Ambedkar** during the Constituent Assembly debates.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct regarding the Joint Session of the Houses of Parliament in India?
1. It is an enabling provision, empowering the President to take steps for resolving deadlock between the two Houses.
2. It is not obligatory upon the President to summon the Houses to meet in a joint sitting.
3. It is being notified by the President.
4. It is frequently resorted to establish the supremacy of the Lok Sabha.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct regarding the Joint Session of the Houses of Parliament in India? 1. It is an enabling provision, empowering the President to take steps for resolving deadlock between the two Houses. 2. It is not obligatory upon the President to summon the Houses to meet in a joint sitting. 3. It is being notified by the President. 4. It is frequently resorted to establish the supremacy of the Lok Sabha. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. A **Joint Sitting (Article 108)** is an enabling mechanism notified by the **President** to resolve legislative deadlocks. It is **not obligatory** for the President to invoke it.
Statement 4 is incorrect because it is **rarely resorted to** (only three times in legislative history), not frequently.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements in the context of structure of the Parliament is/are correct?
1. The Parliament of India consists of the President, the Council of States and the House of the People.
2. The President of India is directly elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both the Houses of the Parliament only.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements in the context of structure of the Parliament is/are correct? 1. The Parliament of India consists of the President, the Council of States and the House of the People. 2. The President of India is directly elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both the Houses of the Parliament only. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. Under **Article 79**, the **Parliament of India consists of the President, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha**.

Statement 2 is incorrect because the President's electoral college includes the elected members of Parliament **and** the elected members of the **State Legislative Assemblies**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha is/are correct?
1. The office of the Deputy Speaker acquired a more prominent position after the enforcement of the Constitution of India in 1950.
2. He/She is elected from amongst the members.
3. He/She holds office until he/she ceases to be a member of the House.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha is/are correct? 1. The office of the Deputy Speaker acquired a more prominent position after the enforcement of the Constitution of India in 1950. 2. He/She is elected from amongst the members. 3. He/She holds office until he/she ceases to be a member of the House. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The position gained distinct constitutional prominence post-1950. The **Deputy Speaker** is elected directly from amongst the Lok Sabha members and vacates the office if they **cease to be a member of the House**, resign, or are removed by a resolution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to Comptroller and Auditor General in India is/are correct?
1. He/She is not an officer of the Parliament but an officer under the President.
2. He/She is an 'independent constitutional authority not directly answerable to the House.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to Comptroller and Auditor General in India is/are correct? 1. He/She is not an officer of the Parliament but an officer under the President. 2. He/She is an 'independent constitutional authority not directly answerable to the House. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **CAG** is an independent constitutional authority acting as an officer under the President, **not an officer of the Parliament**. Although the CAG submits audit reports to Parliament via the President, they are **not directly answerable to the House** like a Minister.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The Departmental Committee of the Parliament of India on the Welfare of the Scheduled Castes (SCs) and the Scheduled Tribes (STs) shall
1. examine whether the Union Government has secured due representation of the SCs and the STs in the services and posts under its control
2. report on the working of the welfare programmes for the SCs and the STs in the Union Territories
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The Departmental Committee of the Parliament of India on the Welfare of the Scheduled Castes (SCs) and the Scheduled Tribes (STs) shall 1. examine whether the Union Government has secured due representation of the SCs and the STs in the services and posts under its control 2. report on the working of the welfare programmes for the SCs and the STs in the Union Territories Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **Committee on the Welfare of SCs and STs** actively monitors whether the Union Government ensures **due representation** for these communities in services and explicitly evaluates the **working of welfare programmes** targeted at them, including in Union Territories.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: As per the Cabinet Resolution of 1st January, 2015, the NITI Aayog consists of :
1. Prime Minister of India
2. Governing Council comprising of Chief Minister of all the States and Lieutenant Governors of Union Territories
3. Rural Development Minister
4. Chief Executive Officer to be appointed by the Prime Minister
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: As per the Cabinet Resolution of 1st January, 2015, the NITI Aayog consists of : 1. Prime Minister of India 2. Governing Council comprising of Chief Minister of all the States and Lieutenant Governors of Union Territories 3. Rural Development Minister 4. Chief Executive Officer to be appointed by the Prime Minister Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. The composition of the **NITI Aayog** definitively includes the **Prime Minister** as Chairperson, a **Governing Council** of CMs and Lt. Governors, and a **CEO appointed by the PM**. Specific ministers like the Rural Development Minister are not mandated core members of the structural resolution unless specially invited.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The executive is given an advance grant to meet temporary and running requirements of Government of India in the beginning of the new financial year until the demands are voted by the legislature. This is known as:
questions.questionText: The executive is given an advance grant to meet temporary and running requirements of Government of India in the beginning of the new financial year until the demands are voted by the legislature. This is known as:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Vote on Account**. Under **Article 116**, a **Vote on Account** is an advance grant approved by the Parliament to the government to cover **temporary and running expenses** for a portion of the financial year pending the final voting of demands and passage of the Appropriation Bill.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The landmark Supreme Court judgment in the case Subhash Sharma v. Union of India (1991) refers to
which one of the following basic features of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: The landmark Supreme Court judgment in the case Subhash Sharma v. Union of India (1991) refers to which one of the following basic features of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Judicial Review**. The **Subhash Sharma v. Union of India (1991)** case dealt comprehensively with the intricacies of judicial appointments and transfers, firmly affirming that the power of **Judicial Review** is an unalterable basic feature of the Indian Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Panchayats in India, as per the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. There is a provision for reservation of seats for the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
2. Panchayats are not authorised to collect taxes.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Panchayats in India, as per the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. There is a provision for reservation of seats for the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes. 2. Panchayats are not authorised to collect taxes. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. **Article 243D** constitutionally mandates the **reservation of seats for SCs and STs** in Panchayats.

Statement 2 is incorrect because under **Article 243H**, a State legislature may by law authorize a Panchayat to **levy, collect, and appropriate certain taxes, duties, and tolls**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which one of the following amendments to the Constitution of India has introduced reservations in education and in public employment for people from the Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) of society?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following amendments to the Constitution of India has introduced reservations in education and in public employment for people from the Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) of society?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Constitution (103rd Amendment) Act**. The **103rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019** amended Articles 15 and 16 to provide for a 10% reservation in educational institutions and public employment specifically for the **Economically Weaker Sections (EWS)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Which among the following Fundamental Rights is not suspended when an Emergency is declared ?
questions.questionText: Which among the following Fundamental Rights is not suspended when an Emergency is declared ?

options.a: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
options.c: Right to move freely throughout the territory of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Protection in respect of conviction for offences**. According to **Article 359**, the Fundamental Rights guaranteed by **Article 20 (protection in respect of conviction for offences)** and **Article 21 (right to life)** cannot be suspended by presidential order even during a National Emergency.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Any question pertaining to the disqualification of a member of the Lok Sabha on the ground of defection is decided by:
questions.questionText: Any question pertaining to the disqualification of a member of the Lok Sabha on the ground of defection is decided by:

options.b: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Speaker of the Lok Sabha**. Under the **Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law)**, any question regarding the disqualification of a member of a House on the ground of defection is subject to the final decision of the **Presiding Officer** of that House (the Speaker in the case of the Lok Sabha).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) of the Parliament is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) of the Parliament is not correct?

options.a: It examines the Finance Accounts of the Government of India.
options.b: Fifteen members of the Committee are elected by the Lok Sabha from amongst its members.
options.c: The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.
