options.d: In case a member of any other Committee constituted by the Government is elected to the PAC, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha decides whether he should continue to be a member of the former Committe

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.** This statement is incorrect. The **Chairperson of the Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** is not elected by its members but is strictly **appointed by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha**, conventionally from the opposition ranks.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following:
1. M.A. Ayyangar
2. G.S. Dhillon
3. Balram Jakhar
4. P.A. Sangma
How many of them were the Speaker of the Lok Sabha ?
questions.questionText: Consider the following: 1. M.A. Ayyangar 2. G.S. Dhillon 3. Balram Jakhar 4. P.A. Sangma How many of them were the Speaker of the Lok Sabha ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **4**. **All four** individuals listed have served as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha. **M.A. Ayyangar** was the 2nd Speaker, and **G.S. Dhillon**, **Balram Jakhar**, and **P.A. Sangma** also served distinguished terms in the office.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Consider the following pairs of Schedule and Content of the Constitution of India :
1. First Schedule: Forms of Oaths or Affirmations
2. Third Schedule: Allocation of seats in the Council of States
3. Fifth Schedule: Provisions related to the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes
4. Ninth Schedule: Provisions related to the administration of tribal areas in certain States
How many of the above pairs is/are correctly matched ?
questions.questionText: Consider the following pairs of Schedule and Content of the Constitution of India : 1. First Schedule: Forms of Oaths or Affirmations 2. Third Schedule: Allocation of seats in the Council of States 3. Fifth Schedule: Provisions related to the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes 4. Ninth Schedule: Provisions related to the administration of tribal areas in certain States How many of the above pairs is/are correctly matched ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1**. **Only the third pair is correctly matched** (Fifth Schedule deals with Scheduled Areas and Tribes). The First Schedule relates to States/UTs. The Third Schedule relates to Oaths. The Ninth Schedule relates to laws protected from judicial review (the Sixth Schedule deals with tribal areas in specific states).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The use of 'Electors Photo Identity Cards (EPIC)' by the Election Commission of India was started in
which one of the following years?
questions.questionText: The use of 'Electors Photo Identity Cards (EPIC)' by the Election Commission of India was started in which one of the following years?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1993**. The Election Commission of India initiated the revolutionary step of issuing **Electors Photo Identity Cards (EPIC)** in the year **1993** under the tenure of Chief Election Commissioner T.N. Seshan to curb rampant electoral fraud and impersonation.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following best describes the electoral system to elect the President of India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following best describes the electoral system to elect the President of India ?

options.b: Proportional Representation and Single Transferable Vote System

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Proportional Representation and Single Transferable Vote System**. Under **Article 55(3)**, the election of the President of India operates strictly in accordance with the system of **proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote**, executed via secret ballot.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is not correct?

options.a: Money Bill cannot be introduced in the Council of States.
options.b: The Council of States has no power to reject or amend the Money Bill.
options.c: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha has the sole and final power in deciding whether a Bill is a Money Bill or otherwise.
options.d: The Council of States has no power to discuss the Money Bill.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Council of States has no power to discuss the Money Bill.** This is incorrect. While the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) is stripped of the power to reject or structurally amend a Money Bill, it possesses the undeniable constitutional **right to discuss** it and can make non-binding recommendations within 14 days.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: By
which one of the following Constitutional Amendment Acts, was the Article 21A (Right to Education) inserted into the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: By which one of the following Constitutional Amendment Acts, was the Article 21A (Right to Education) inserted into the Constitution of India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **86th Amendment Act**. The **86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002** decisively inserted **Article 21A**, recognizing free and compulsory education for all children between the ages of 6 and 14 as a Fundamental Right.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which among the following is not the strength or chief benefit of Bicameralism ?
questions.questionText: Which among the following is not the strength or chief benefit of Bicameralism ?

options.a: Second chambers check the first chambers and prevent majoritarian rule.
options.b: It checks the powers of the executive.
options.c: The second chambers can act as a constitutional safeguard.
options.d: It often acts as a check on democratic rule, particularly when their members are non-elected or indirectly elected.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It often acts as a check on democratic rule, particularly when their members are non-elected or indirectly elected.** The capacity of an unelected or indirectly elected upper house to stall democratic will is widely cited by political scientists as a fundamental **weakness or criticism** of bicameralism, not a "strength or chief benefit".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which among the following is not a condition for the disqualification of a Member of Parliament?
questions.questionText: Which among the following is not a condition for the disqualification of a Member of Parliament?

options.a: Voluntary acquisition of citizenship of a foreign country
options.b: Holding the office of the Chairperson of the National Commission for Women
options.c: The Member abstains from voting in the House without prior permission
options.d: The Member holds the office of the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the National Coal Development Corporation Ltd.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Holding the office of the Chairperson of the National Commission for Women**. The Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act legally exempts a wide array of offices from the "office of profit" doctrine. Serving as the **Chairperson of the National Commission for Women is an exempted office**, thus not triggering disqualification.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Who among the following considered the Directive Principles of State Policy as aiming at 'furthering the goals of social exclusion'?
questions.questionText: Who among the following considered the Directive Principles of State Policy as aiming at 'furthering the goals of social exclusion'?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Granville Austin**. The prominent constitutional scholar **Granville Austin** noted that the Directive Principles of State Policy (alongside Fundamental Rights) formed the conscience of the Constitution, heavily aimed at executing a social revolution and dismantling barriers of **social exclusion**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: The provision, 'the State shall take steps to separate the Judiciary from the Executive in the public services of the State' is incorporated in which part of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: The provision, 'the State shall take steps to separate the Judiciary from the Executive in the public services of the State' is incorporated in which part of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Part-IV**. The principle instructing the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in public services is specifically enshrined in **Article 50**, which is an integral component of the **Directive Principles of State Policy under Part-IV**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct?
1. Speaker may permit any Member to address the House in his/her mother tongue, if he/she cannot adequately express in either Hindi or English.
2. Business of the Parliamentary Committees is transacted either in Hindi or in English.
3. The minutes of the Parliamentary Committees are prepared invariably in Hindi or English.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct? 1. Speaker may permit any Member to address the House in his/her mother tongue, if he/she cannot adequately express in either Hindi or English. 2. Business of the Parliamentary Committees is transacted either in Hindi or in English. 3. The minutes of the Parliamentary Committees are prepared invariably in Hindi or English. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The Constitution and parliamentary rules allow the Speaker to permit a member to address the House in their **mother tongue**. Officially, the business and written **minutes of Parliamentary Committees** are uniformly prepared and transacted in either **Hindi or English**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following is the correct meaning of the term 'State', as defined in Article 12 of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct meaning of the term 'State', as defined in Article 12 of the Constitution of India?

options.a: It refers only to the Government of India.
options.b: It refers only to the Government of India and the Parliament of India.
options.c: It refers only to the Government of India, Parliament of India, and Governments and Legislatures of each State.
options.d: It refers to the Government of India, Parliament of India, Governments and Legislatures of each State, and all local or other authorities within the territory of India.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It refers to the Government of India, Parliament of India, Governments and Legislatures of each State, and all local or other authorities within the territory of India. Article 12** provides a broad and exhaustive definition of "the State" to ensure fundamental rights are protected against the actions of practically all levels of government and **public/local authorities**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following is the largest State in terms of seats in the Rajya Sabha ?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is the largest State in terms of seats in the Rajya Sabha ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Bihar**. Representation in the Rajya Sabha is scaled to state populations. Among the options provided, **Bihar holds the highest representation with 16 seats** (compared to Karnataka's 12, Andhra Pradesh's 11, and Rajasthan's 10).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Under which Article of the Constitution of India has provision been made for reservation of seats for women in Panchayats ?
questions.questionText: Under which Article of the Constitution of India has provision been made for reservation of seats for women in Panchayats ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 243 D**. **Article 243D** introduced by the 73rd Amendment specifically mandates the **reservation of seats** for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and strictly reserves at least one-third of the total seats for **women in Panchayats**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following is not correct in respect of Directorate of Enforcement ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not correct in respect of Directorate of Enforcement ?

options.a: It is a specialized financial investigation agency under the Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance.
options.b: It enforces the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.
options.c: It enforces the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002.
options.d: It enforces the Prohibition of Benami Property Transaction Act, 1988.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It enforces the Prohibition of Benami Property Transaction Act, 1988.** The **Directorate of Enforcement (ED)** enforces major financial laws like FEMA and PMLA. However, the Prohibition of Benami Property Transactions Act is administered and enforced by the **Income Tax Department**, not the ED.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which one of the following is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not correct?

options.a: Taxes on agricultural income is a subject under the State List.
options.b: Price control is a subject under the Concurrent List.
options.c: Insurance does not come under the Union List.
options.d: 'Forests' is a subject under the Concurrent List.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Insurance does not come under the Union List.** This is demonstrably incorrect. Due to its nationwide financial implications, **Insurance** is exclusively enumerated as Entry 47 in the **Union List (List I)** of the Seventh Schedule.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not correct about the nature of India's federal system ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct about the nature of India's federal system ?

options.a: There is no equality of representation of States in the Council of States.
options.b: Sikkim was not initially included in the Union as a full-fledged State.
options.c: Special provisions have been laid down in the Constitution of India for Andhra Pradesh under Article 371 D.
options.d: The Constitution of India enables the federal government to acquire the strength of a unitary system during emergencies.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: The official key marked this question as ambiguous or **Cancelled**. Factually, all four options present broadly correct statements regarding the Indian constitutional setup: representation in the Rajya Sabha is indeed unequal (based on population); Sikkim was initially an associate state; Article 371D is for Andhra Pradesh; and emergencies do convert the system to a unitary one.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Constitution of India guarantees freedom of speech and expression. But the freedom is subject to certain reasonable restrictions imposed by the State. These restrictions may relate to which of the following?
1. Defamation
2. Decency or morality
3. Incitement to an offence
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: The Constitution of India guarantees freedom of speech and expression. But the freedom is subject to certain reasonable restrictions imposed by the State. These restrictions may relate to which of the following? 1. Defamation 2. Decency or morality 3. Incitement to an offence Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Article 19(2)**, the State holds the authority to impose reasonable restrictions on the exercise of freedom of speech and expression on explicit grounds including **defamation**, **decency or morality**, and **incitement to an offence**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which one of the following amendments in the Constitution of India made the Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following amendments in the Constitution of India made the Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question was officially **Cancelled**. The provision making the Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review was originally enacted by the **38th Amendment Act (1975)**. Since the 38th Amendment was noticeably absent from the provided options, no answer was technically correct. (The 44th Amendment later restored judicial review capabilities).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a correct statement with reference to the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a correct statement with reference to the Constitution of India?

options.a: The Constitution (Eighty-Fifth) Amendment Act has inserted the 'Right to Education' under Article 21A.
options.c: Right to Constitutional Remedies is in Part-III of the Constitution.
options.d: Writ jurisdiction of High Courts is wider than the Supreme Court of India.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Constitution (Eighty-Fifth) Amendment Act has inserted the 'Right to Education' under Article 21A.** This statement is incorrect. The monumental insertion of the Right to Education under Article 21A was effectuated by the **86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002**, not the 85th.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Food Security Act is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Food Security Act is not correct?

options.a: The Act was enacted in the year 2013.
options.b: The Act was rolled out in the year 2014.
options.c: The Act legally entitles 67 percent of the population to receive highly subsidised food grains.
options.d: The Act is not being implemented in all the States/Union Territories.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Act is not being implemented in all the States/Union Territories.** This is incorrect. The **National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013** is a nationwide law that has been rolled out and is being actively implemented across **all States and Union Territories** in India to ensure food security.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one among the following is not correct about the Secretary General of the Lok Sabha?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not correct about the Secretary General of the Lok Sabha?

options.a: The Secretary General is the advisor to the Speaker.
options.b: The Secretary General acts under the authority in the name of the Speaker.
options.c: The Secretary General works under the Speaker with delegated authority.
options.d: The Secretary General passes orders in the name of the Speaker.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Secretary General works under the Speaker with delegated authority.** The Secretary General holds a permanent administrative post with **inherent statutory and constitutional authority** derived from the rules of the House. They do not merely operate under the "delegated authority" of the Speaker.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one among the following statements pertaining to the President's term of office is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements pertaining to the President's term of office is not correct?

options.a: The President holds office for a term of five years.
options.b: The President may be removed from the office by way of impeachment.
options.c: The President may resign before the expiration of his/her term by writing to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.
options.d: The President shall, notwithstanding the expiration of his/her term, continue to hold office until his/her successor enters upon his/her offic

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The President may resign before the expiration of his/her term by writing to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.** Under **Article 56**, a sitting President must address and submit their resignation letter exclusively to the **Vice-President of India**, not the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following Articles in the Constitution of India are exceptions to the Fundamental Rights enumerated in Article 14 and Article 19?
questions.questionText: Which of the following Articles in the Constitution of India are exceptions to the Fundamental Rights enumerated in Article 14 and Article 19?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 31A and Article 31C**. Both **Article 31A** (protecting land acquisition laws) and **Article 31C** (protecting laws implementing certain Directive Principles) are explicitly designed as **constitutional exceptions**, shielding laws from being struck down for violating the Fundamental Rights guaranteed under **Article 14 and Article 19**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following is not the necessary condition for the issue of a writ of Quo Warranto?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not the necessary condition for the issue of a writ of Quo Warranto?

options.a: The office must be a public office.
options.b: The office must be created by the Statute or by the Constitution itself.
options.c: The office must not be a substantive one.
options.d: There has been a contravention of the Constitution or a Statute in appropriating such person to that offic

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The office must not be a substantive one.** This statement is fundamentally incorrect. For the courts to issue a writ of **Quo Warranto** (asking "by what authority"), it is a mandatory legal condition that the public office in question **must be a substantive one** (an independent, permanent office), not merely a temporary post.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following Commissions is related to Article 338A?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Commissions is related to Article 338A?

options.a: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes
options.b: The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
options.c: The National Commission for Backward Classes

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes**. The **89th Constitutional Amendment Act (2003)** inserted **Article 338A** into the Constitution, formally establishing a separate and independent **National Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST)**.

questions.subject: History
questions.sectionGroup: Modern
statementLines: Who among the following formed the National Liberation Federation (Liberal Party)?
questions.questionText: Who among the following formed the National Liberation Federation (Liberal Party)?

options.a: Motilal Nehru and C. R. Das
options.b: Muhammad Ali and C. R. Das
options.c: T. B. Sapru and M. R. Jayakar
options.d: M. R. Jayakar and C. R. Das

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **T. B. Sapru and M. R. Jayakar**. The **National Liberal Federation** was established in 1919 by prominent moderate leaders such as **Tej Bahadur Sapru**, Surendranath Banerjee, and **M. R. Jayakar**, who chose to leave the Congress due to differences over the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one among the following is not a character of a secular State?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not a character of a secular State?

options.b: It separates religion from the State.
options.c: A State in order to be secular must be democratic.
options.d: It must prevent religious conflict and promote religious harmony.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A State in order to be secular must be democratic.** While it is a desirable trait, it is **not conceptually or structurally mandatory** for a secular state to be a democracy. A state can be non-democratic (like an authoritarian regime) and still maintain strict **secularism** by officially separating religion and government.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: A special address by the Governor refers to the address delivered by the Governor
questions.questionText: A special address by the Governor refers to the address delivered by the Governor

options.a: When President's Rule is called for
options.b: When a national emergency necessitates dissolution of Legislative Assembly
options.c: At the commencement of the first session after general election and at the first session of each year
options.d: Whenever he/she has concluded that such is necessary

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **at the commencement of the first session after general election and at the first session of each year**. Under the explicit mandate of **Article 176**, the Governor delivers a **special address** to the state legislature only on two specific occasions: the **first session following a general election** and the **first session of every new year**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which one of the following statements in relation to Panchayats is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements in relation to Panchayats is not correct?

options.a: Legislature of a State may, by law, make provisions with respect to the composition of Panchayats.
options.b: Panchayat area means the territorial area of a Panchayat.
options.c: Gram Sabha includes all persons in the electoral rolls of village within a Panchayat.
options.d: Reservation of seats for SCs and STs has nothing to do with proportion of their population.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Reservation of seats for SCs and STs has nothing to do with proportion of their population.** This is completely false. **Article 243D** directly mandates that the **reservation of seats for SCs and STs** must be made **in proportion to their respective populations** within the given Panchayat area.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to 'protective democracy' is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to 'protective democracy' is not correct?

options.a: It propounds that citizen participation is essential in democracies.
options.b: Citizens must be able to protect themselves from governmental encroachments.
options.c: It is compatible with laissez-faire capitalism.
options.d: Political equality is understood in formal terms as equal voting rights.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It propounds that citizen participation is essential in democracies.** The model of **protective democracy** views democracy primarily as a mechanism for citizens to **protect their rights from government overreach**, rather than prioritizing active or continuous mass "citizen participation" (which is a feature of participatory democracy).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the privileges of the Members of the Parliament are correct?
1. Privileges would not be fettered by the Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India.
2. Privileges must be read subject to the Articles 20-22 and Article 32 of the Constitution of India.
3. Immunity is available in relation to both civil and criminal prosecution.
4. Immunity is available in relation to freedom of speech even in his/her private or personal capacity.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the privileges of the Members of the Parliament are correct? 1. Privileges would not be fettered by the Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India. 2. Privileges must be read subject to the Articles 20-22 and Article 32 of the Constitution of India. 3. Immunity is available in relation to both civil and criminal prosecution. 4. Immunity is available in relation to freedom of speech even in his/her private or personal capacity. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. Parliamentary **privileges (Article 105)** are independent of general freedom of speech under **Article 19(1)**, but the Supreme Court has ruled they are **subject to fundamental rights under Articles 20-22 and 32**. Immunity does **not completely shield MPs from criminal prosecution** outside the house, nor does it cover speech made in a strictly **personal capacity**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the appointment of the Members of the Parliamentary Committees is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the appointment of the Members of the Parliamentary Committees is correct?

options.d: The Members are appointed or elected on a motion made and adopted or nominated by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha or the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Members are appointed or elected on a motion made and adopted or nominated by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha or the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.** Depending on the specific committee rules, membership can be achieved via **election by the House** (like PAC), through a **formal motion**, or by **direct nomination by the Presiding Officer**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a classified category of political parties as outlined by the Election Commission of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a classified category of political parties as outlined by the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Regional Parties**. The **Election Commission of India (ECI)** officially classifies political parties strictly into three statutory categories: **National Parties, State Recognised Parties, and Registered Unrecognised Parties**. The term "Regional Parties" is a colloquial classification, not a formal ECI legal category.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following terms were added to the Preamble of the Constitution of India by the Constitutional Amendment, 1976?
1. Socialist
2. Secular
3. Integrity
4. Fraternity
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following terms were added to the Preamble of the Constitution of India by the Constitutional Amendment, 1976? 1. Socialist 2. Secular 3. Integrity 4. Fraternity Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The sweeping **42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976** added exactly three new terms to the Preamble of the Constitution: **Socialist**, **Secular**, and **Integrity**. The term "Fraternity" was already embedded in the original text of the Preamble.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Who among the following was the advisor to the Constituent Assembly?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was the advisor to the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **B. N. Rau**. Sir **Benegal Narsing Rau (B.N. Rau)** was formally appointed as the legal and **Constitutional Advisor** to the Constituent Assembly, playing a foundational role by formulating the initial draft of the Indian Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: In the Indian judicial system, writs are issued by
questions.questionText: In the Indian judicial system, writs are issued by

options.c: The Supreme Court and High Courts only
options.d: The Supreme Court, High Courts and Lower Courts

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Supreme Court and High Courts only**. Under the Indian constitutional framework, the extraordinary power to issue prerogative writs is vested exclusively in the **Supreme Court (Article 32)** and the **High Courts (Article 226)**. Lower courts are currently not empowered with writ jurisdiction.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: The Citizenship (Amendment) Act falls under
which one of the following Parts of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: The Citizenship (Amendment) Act falls under which one of the following Parts of the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Part II**. The entire subject of **Citizenship**, detailing rights at the commencement of the Constitution and giving Parliament the power to regulate it, is exclusively covered under **Articles 5 to 11**, which constitute **Part II** of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following departments is not under the Ministry of Home Affairs?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following departments is not under the Ministry of Home Affairs?

options.c: Department of Jammu and Kashmir Affairs

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Department of Legal Affairs**. The **Department of Legal Affairs** is a distinct and independent department that functions squarely under the **Ministry of Law and Justice**, not the Ministry of Home Affairs.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Under

which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India, a statement of estimated receipts and expenditure of the Government of India has to be laid before the Parliament in respect of every financial year?
questions.questionText: Under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India, a statement of estimated receipts and expenditure of the Government of India has to be laid before the Parliament in respect of every financial year?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 112**. **Article 112** strictly mandates that the President shall cause an "Annual Financial Statement" to be laid before Parliament, which contains the **estimated receipts and expenditure** (commonly known as the Union Budget) for that specific financial year.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The National Dope Testing Laboratory functions under
questions.questionText: The National Dope Testing Laboratory functions under

options.a: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
options.c: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports**. The **National Dope Testing Laboratory (NDTL)**, responsible for anti-doping testing in sports within the country, operates as an autonomous body directly under the **Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Article 371 A of the Constitution of India provides special privileges to
questions.questionText: Article 371 A of the Constitution of India provides special privileges to
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Nagaland**. **Article 371A** was specially inserted into the Constitution to grant sweeping **special privileges and protections** to the State of **Nagaland**, heavily safeguarding Naga customary laws and the ownership of local land.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: How many Zonal Councils were set up vide Part-III of the States Re-organization Act, 1956?
questions.questionText: How many Zonal Councils were set up vide Part-III of the States Re-organization Act, 1956?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Five**. The **States Reorganisation Act of 1956** statutorily established exactly **five Zonal Councils** (Northern, Central, Eastern, Western, and Southern) to promote interstate cooperation. The North Eastern Council was created much later in 1971.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which provision of the Constitution of India provides that the President shall not be answerable to any Court in India for the exercise of powers of his office?
questions.questionText: Which provision of the Constitution of India provides that the President shall not be answerable to any Court in India for the exercise of powers of his office?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 361**. **Article 361** explicitly provides overarching legal immunity, stating that the President and the Governors **shall not be answerable to any court** for the exercise and performance of the official powers and duties of their respective offices.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which law prescribes that all proceedings in the Supreme Court shall be in English language?
questions.questionText: Which law prescribes that all proceedings in the Supreme Court shall be in English language?

options.a: Article 145 of the Constitution of India
options.b: Article 348 of the Constitution of India
options.d: An Act passed by the Parliament

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 348 of the Constitution of India**. **Article 348(1)** clearly mandates that until Parliament legislates otherwise, all proceedings in the **Supreme Court and in every High Court** shall be conducted exclusively in the **English language**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: The total number of members in the Union Council of Ministers in India shall not exceed
questions.questionText: The total number of members in the Union Council of Ministers in India shall not exceed

options.a: 10% of the total number of members of the Parliament
options.b: 15% of the total number of members of the Parliament
options.c: 10% of the total number of members of the Lok Sabha
options.d: 15% of the total number of members of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **15% of the total number of members of the Lok Sabha**. Inserted by the 91st Constitutional Amendment Act, **Article 75(1A)** imposes a strict legal ceiling, dictating that the total number of Ministers (including the PM) shall not exceed **15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following States does not have a Legislative Council?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following States does not have a Legislative Council?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Arunachal Pradesh**. India practices bicameralism in only a select few states. **Arunachal Pradesh** has a strictly unicameral legislature and **does not have a Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following is not enumerated in the Constitution of India as a fundamental duty of citizens of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not enumerated in the Constitution of India as a fundamental duty of citizens of India?

options.b: To protect and improve the natural environment
options.c: To develop the scientific temper and spirit of inquiry
options.d: To promote international peace and security

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To promote international peace and security**. The promotion of international peace and security is explicitly designated as a **Directive Principle of State Policy (Article 51)** meant for the State, and is not listed as a Fundamental Duty (Article 51A) for the citizens.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following conditions laid down in the Constitution of India for the issue of a writ of Quo-Warranto is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following conditions laid down in the Constitution of India for the issue of a writ of Quo-Warranto is not correct?

options.a: The office must be public and it must be created by a Statute
options.b: The office must be a substantive one
options.c: There has been a contravention of the Constitution or a Statute in appointing such person to that office
options.d: The appointment is in tune with a statutory provision

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The appointment is in tune with a statutory provision**. The writ of **Quo Warranto** is issued to challenge the legality of a person's claim to a public office. If an appointment is completely **in tune with statutory provisions**, it is lawful, meaning the court will explicitly **refuse to issue the writ**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: It relates to the special provision for administration of certain areas in the States other than Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram.
options.b: Tribal advisory councils are to be constituted to give advice under the Fifth Schedule.
options.c: The Governor is not authorized to make regulations to prohibit or restrict the transfer of land by, or among members of the Scheduled Tribes.
options.d: The Governors of the States in which there are scheduled areas have to submit reports to the President regarding the administration of such areas.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Governor is not authorized to make regulations to prohibit or restrict the transfer of land by, or among members of the Scheduled Tribes.** This statement is constitutionally inaccurate. Under the **Fifth Schedule**, the **Governor holds extensive, direct authority** to formulate regulations specifically to prohibit or restrict the transfer of tribal lands.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Consider the following statements with regard to the formation of new States and alteration of boundaries of existing States :
1. Parliament may increase the area of any State.
2. Parliament may diminish the area of any State.
3. Parliament cannot alter the boundary of any State.
4. Parliament cannot alter the name of any State.
Which of the statements given above is/are not correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements with regard to the formation of new States and alteration of boundaries of existing States : 1. Parliament may increase the area of any State. 2. Parliament may diminish the area of any State. 3. Parliament cannot alter the boundary of any State. 4. Parliament cannot alter the name of any State. Which of the statements given above is/are not correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 and 4**. According to the explicit powers granted under **Article 3**, the Parliament unequivocally possesses the authority to increase the area, diminish the area, **alter the boundaries**, and **alter the name** of any State. Therefore, statements 3 and 4 are entirely incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Advocate General of a State in India is appointed by the President of India upon the recommendations of the Governor of the concerned State.
2. As provided in the Code of Civil Procedure, High Courts have original appellate advisory jurisdiction at the State level.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Advocate General of a State in India is appointed by the President of India upon the recommendations of the Governor of the concerned State. 2. As provided in the Code of Civil Procedure, High Courts have original appellate advisory jurisdiction at the State level. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. The **Advocate General (Article 165)** is directly appointed by the **Governor** of the State, not the President. High Courts possess broad original and appellate jurisdiction, but the Indian Constitution grants **Advisory Jurisdiction (Article 143) exclusively to the Supreme Court**, not High Courts.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following forms of Constitution contains the features of both the Unitary and Federal Constitution?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following forms of Constitution contains the features of both the Unitary and Federal Constitution?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Quasi-Federal**. A **Quasi-Federal** constitution (which is the widely accepted description of the Indian Constitution by scholars like K.C. Wheare) is a hybrid framework that blends core features of **both a unitary (centralized) and a federal (decentralized) system**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one among the following is not a part of the Fundamental Rights (Part III) of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not a part of the Fundamental Rights (Part III) of the Constitution of India?

options.a: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
options.b: Prohibition of employment of children in factories
options.c: Participation of workers in management of industries
options.d: Practice any profession, or to carry on any occupation, trade or business

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Participation of workers in management of industries**. The mandate to ensure the participation of workers in the management of industries is explicitly detailed under **Article 43A**, which falls squarely under the **Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV)**, not Fundamental Rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements as per the Constitution of India are not correct?
1. The President tenders his resignation to the Chief Justice of India.
2. The Vice-President tenders his resignation to the President of India.
3. The Comptroller and Auditor General of India is removed from his office in the like manner as the President of India.
4. A Judge of the Supreme Court can resign his office by writing under his hand addressed to the Chief Justice of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements as per the Constitution of India are not correct? 1. The President tenders his resignation to the Chief Justice of India. 2. The Vice-President tenders his resignation to the President of India. 3. The Comptroller and Auditor General of India is removed from his office in the like manner as the President of India. 4. A Judge of the Supreme Court can resign his office by writing under his hand addressed to the Chief Justice of India. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4**. Statement 1 is false (the President resigns to the **Vice-President**). Statement 3 is false (the CAG is removed in the like manner as a **Supreme Court Judge**, not the President). Statement 4 is false (a Supreme Court Judge resigns to the **President**, not the CJI). Only statement 2 is factually correct.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Rajya Sabha has exclusive jurisdiction in
questions.questionText: Rajya Sabha has exclusive jurisdiction in

options.d: Authorising Parliament to legislate on a subject in the State List

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **authorising Parliament to legislate on a subject in the State List**. Under **Article 249**, the Rajya Sabha possesses the unique and exclusive constitutional power to pass a resolution (by a two-thirds majority) authorizing Parliament to legislate on subjects generally reserved in the **State List**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Government of India Act, 1858 is/are correct?
1. The British Crown assumed sovereignty over India from the East India Company.
2. The British Parliament enacted the first statute for the governance of India under the direct rule of the British.
3. This Act was dominated by the principle of absolute imperial control without any popular participation in the administration of the country.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Government of India Act, 1858 is/are correct? 1. The British Crown assumed sovereignty over India from the East India Company. 2. The British Parliament enacted the first statute for the governance of India under the direct rule of the British. 3. This Act was dominated by the principle of absolute imperial control without any popular participation in the administration of the country. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The **Government of India Act, 1858** decisively ended Company rule, transferring absolute sovereignty to the **British Crown**. It introduced direct rule and was characterized by **absolute imperial control** without granting any democratic or popular participation to Indians.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Indian Councils Act, 1861 is/are correct?
1. The Act introduced a grain of popular element by including non-official members in the Governor-General's Executive Council.
2. The members were nominated and their functions were confined exclusively to consideration of legislative proposals placed before it by the Governor-General.
3. The Governor-General did not have effective legislative power.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Indian Councils Act, 1861 is/are correct? 1. The Act introduced a grain of popular element by including non-official members in the Governor-General's Executive Council. 2. The members were nominated and their functions were confined exclusively to consideration of legislative proposals placed before it by the Governor-General. 3. The Governor-General did not have effective legislative power. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The **1861 Act** was a landmark for introducing non-official Indian members, whose roles were heavily restricted strictly to **legislative considerations**. Statement 3 is entirely false because the Governor-General retained immense and **effective legislative power**, including an absolute veto and ordinance-making powers.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the historic Objectives Resolution, which was adopted by the Constituent Assembly, is/are correct?
1. The Objectives Resolution inspired the shaping of the Constitution through all its subsequent stages.
2. It was not just a resolution, but a declaration, a firm resolve and a pledge.
3. It provided the underlying philosophy of our Constitution.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the historic Objectives Resolution, which was adopted by the Constituent Assembly, is/are correct? 1. The Objectives Resolution inspired the shaping of the Constitution through all its subsequent stages. 2. It was not just a resolution, but a declaration, a firm resolve and a pledge. 3. It provided the underlying philosophy of our Constitution. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Masterfully drafted by Jawaharlal Nehru, the **Objectives Resolution** was a solemn pledge that laid down the foundational **philosophy**, democratic ideals, and architectural framework that heavily inspired the shaping of the Constitution through all its stages.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the duties of the Governor is/are correct?
1. The duties of the Governor as a constitutional Head of the State do not become the subject matter of questions or debate in the Parliament.

2. Where the Governor takes a decision independently of his Council of Ministers or where he acts as the Chief Executive of the State under President's rule, his actions are subject to scrutiny by the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the duties of the Governor is/are correct? 1. The duties of the Governor as a constitutional Head of the State do not become the subject matter of questions or debate in the Parliament. 2. Where the Governor takes a decision independently of his Council of Ministers or where he acts as the Chief Executive of the State under President's rule, his actions are subject to scrutiny by the Parliament. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Ordinarily, the actions of a Governor as a constitutional head (acting on state advice) are shielded from parliamentary debate. However, when acting under **President's Rule** (as an agent of the Center) or exercising independent discretion, their administrative actions become directly **subject to scrutiny by the Parliament**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India deals with the special provision with respect to the State of Assam?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India deals with the special provision with respect to the State of Assam?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 371B**. The Constitution grants several special provisions in Part XXI. **Article 371B** specifically contains special provisions with respect to the State of **Assam**, allowing the President to facilitate a committee of the state assembly consisting of members elected from tribal areas.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Provisions of
which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India apply to the State of Jammu and Kashmir?
questions.questionText: Provisions of which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India apply to the State of Jammu and Kashmir?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 370**. Historically, **Article 370** contained temporary, transitional, and massive special provisions exclusively applicable to the State of **Jammu and Kashmir**, granting it significant political autonomy until its extensive modification in 2019.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which one of the following Schedules to the Constitution of India provides for setting up of Autonomous District Councils?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Schedules to the Constitution of India provides for setting up of Autonomous District Councils?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Sixth Schedule**. The **Sixth Schedule** contains vital provisions regarding the specialized administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram through the robust establishment of self-governing **Autonomous District Councils (ADCs)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Which one of the following regarding the tenure of the elected members of the Autonomous District Council is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding the tenure of the elected members of the Autonomous District Council is correct?

options.a: Five years from the date of election
options.b: Five years from the date appointed for the first meeting of the Council after the election
options.c: Six years from the date of administration of Oath
options.d: Six years from the date of election

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Five years from the date appointed for the first meeting of the Council after the election**. Similar to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies, the tenure of an elected **Autonomous District Council** is strictly five years, officially calculated from the date of its **first appointed meeting**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Who among the following shall cause the accounts of the Autonomous District and Regional Council Funds to be audited?
questions.questionText: Who among the following shall cause the accounts of the Autonomous District and Regional Council Funds to be audited?

options.a: The Comptroller and Auditor General of India
options.b: The Chartered Accountant empanelled by the Government of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Comptroller and Auditor General of India**. The Constitution explicitly mandates that the accounts of the District and Regional Councils established under the Sixth Schedule must be kept in a form prescribed by, and strictly audited by, the **Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Who has the power of annulment or suspension of Acts and Resolutions of the Autonomous District and Regional Councils?
questions.questionText: Who has the power of annulment or suspension of Acts and Resolutions of the Autonomous District and Regional Councils?

options.c: The Chief Minister of the State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Governor**. Under the provisions of the Sixth Schedule, if the **Governor** is satisfied that an act or resolution passed by a District or Regional Council is likely to endanger the safety of India, they hold the overarching power to **annul or suspend** such acts or resolutions.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The audit reports of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India relating to the accounts of the Union shall be submitted to
questions.questionText: The audit reports of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India relating to the accounts of the Union shall be submitted to

options.b: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the President**. According to **Article 151(1)**, the audit reports of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India relating specifically to the accounts of the Union must be submitted directly to **the President**, who shall subsequently cause them to be laid before each House of Parliament.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following is not related to the powers of the Governor?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is not related to the powers of the Governor?

options.c: Summoning, proroguing and dissolving State Legislature
options.d: Power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remission of punishments

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Diplomatic and military powers**. The **Governor** is the executive head of the state with extensive legislative and pardoning powers. However, massive sovereign functions like **Diplomatic and Military powers** are explicitly and exclusively vested in the **President of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following regarding the procedure and conduct of business in the Parliament is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding the procedure and conduct of business in the Parliament is not correct?

options.b: To discuss issues of the use of police force in suppressing the Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe communities
options.c: To discuss issues in dealing with violent disturbances in an Undertaking under the control of the Union Government
options.d: To discuss issues for putting down the demands of the industrial labour

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To discuss State matters**. In India's strict federal structure, the **Parliament's rules of procedure generally prohibit raising or discussing matters that fall exclusively under the jurisdiction of a State Government**, thereby preserving the constitutional autonomy of the State legislatures.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following is not under the powers and functions of the Election Commission of India?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is not under the powers and functions of the Election Commission of India?

options.a: Superintendence, direction and control of the preparation of electoral rolls
options.b: Conduct of elections to the Parliament and to the Legislature of each State
options.c: Conduct of election to the office of the President and the Vice President
options.d: Appointment of the Regional Commissioners to assist the Election Commission in the performance of the functions conferred on the Commission

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Appointment of the Regional Commissioners to assist the Election Commission in the performance of the functions conferred on the Commission**. Under **Article 324(4)**, the constitutional power to appoint **Regional Commissioners** is explicitly vested in the **President of India** (acting in consultation with the Election Commission), not the Election Commission itself.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following criteria is not required to be qualified for appointment as Judge of the Supreme Court?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following criteria is not required to be qualified for appointment as Judge of the Supreme Court?

options.a: At least five years as a Judge of a High Court
options.b: At least ten years as an Advocate of a High Court
options.c: In the opinion of the President, a distinguished Jurist
options.d: At least twenty years as a Sub-Judicial Magistrate

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **At least twenty years as a Sub-Judicial Magistrate**. According to **Article 124(3)**, the rigorous qualifications to be a Supreme Court Judge include serving as a High Court judge for 5 years, an HC advocate for 10 years, or being a distinguished jurist. Serving as a **Sub-Judicial Magistrate is nowhere mentioned** as a constitutional qualification.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following is not among the duties of the Chief Minister?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not among the duties of the Chief Minister?

options.a: To communicate to the Governor of the State all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the affairs of the State and proposals for legislation
options.b: To furnish information relating to the administration of the State and proposals for legislation as the Governor may call for
options.c: To communicate to the President all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the State in the monthly report
options.d: To submit for the consideration of the Council of Ministers any matter on which a decision has been taken by a Minister but has not been considered by the Council, if the Governor so requires

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To communicate to the President all decisions of the Council of Ministers relating to the administration of the State in the monthly report**. Under the specific mandate of **Article 167**, the Chief Minister's constitutional duty is exclusively to communicate state government decisions to the **Governor of the State**, not directly to the President.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following is not considered a part of the Legislature of States?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not considered a part of the Legislature of States?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Chief Minister**. According to **Article 168**, the **Legislature of a State** consists strictly of the **Governor** and either one or two Houses (the **Legislative Assembly** and the **Legislative Council**). The Chief Minister is the head of the executive branch, not structurally a constituent part of the Legislature itself.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following regarding the ordinance-making power of the Governor is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding the ordinance-making power of the Governor is not correct?

options.a: It is not a discretionary power.
options.b: The Governor may withdraw the ordinance anytime.
options.c: The ordinance power can be exercised when the Legislature is not in session.
options.d: The aid and advice of ministers is not required for declaring the ordinanc

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The aid and advice of ministers is not required for declaring the ordinance.** This statement is wholly false. The ordinance-making power (Article 213) is **not a discretionary power**; the Governor must strictly act on the binding **aid and advice of the Council of Ministers** when promulgating or withdrawing any ordinance.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following features were borrowed by the Constitution of India from the British Constitution?
1. Rule of Law
2. Law-making Procedure
3. Independence of Judiciary
4. Parliamentary System
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following features were borrowed by the Constitution of India from the British Constitution? 1. Rule of Law 2. Law-making Procedure 3. Independence of Judiciary 4. Parliamentary System Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. From the British Constitution, India successfully borrowed the **Rule of Law**, the structured **Law-making Procedure**, and the **Parliamentary System** of government. The foundational principle of the **Independence of Judiciary** was primarily borrowed from the **US Constitution**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one of the following Schedules of the Constitution of India has fixed the number of Members of the Rajya Sabha to be elected from each State?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Schedules of the Constitution of India has fixed the number of Members of the Rajya Sabha to be elected from each State?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Fourth Schedule**. The **Fourth Schedule** of the Indian Constitution explicitly details the state-wise and UT-wise allocation of seats and permanently fixes the total number of representatives that can be elected to the **Rajya Sabha (Council of States)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: The Fundamental Rights guaranteed in the Constitution of India can be suspended only by
questions.questionText: The Fundamental Rights guaranteed in the Constitution of India can be suspended only by

options.b: An Act passed by the Parliament
options.c: An amendment to the Constitution of India
options.d: The judicial decisions of the Supreme Court

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **a proclamation of National Emergency**. Under **Articles 358 and 359**, the enforcement of certain Fundamental Rights (except Articles 20 and 21) can be suspended by a Presidential order exclusively during the active operation of a formally declared **Proclamation of National Emergency**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the 73rd and 74th Constitution Amendment Act is/are correct?
1. It makes it mandatory for all States to establish a three-tier system of Government.
2. Representatives should be directly elected for five years.
3. There should be mandatory reservation of one-third of all seats in all Panchayats at all levels for women.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the 73rd and 74th Constitution Amendment Act is/are correct? 1. It makes it mandatory for all States to establish a three-tier system of Government. 2. Representatives should be directly elected for five years. 3. There should be mandatory reservation of one-third of all seats in all Panchayats at all levels for women. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. Under these historic amendments, members are **directly elected for a five-year term**, and there is a mandatory reservation of at least **one-third of seats for women**.
Statement 1 is incorrect because states with a population of **less than 20 lakhs** are exempt from forming the intermediate level, meaning a 3-tier system is not universally mandatory.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A Joint Sitting of the Parliament is resorted to, for resolving the deadlock between two Houses of the Parliament for passing which of the following Bills?
1. Money Bill
2. Constitutional Amendment Bill
3. Ordinary Bill
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: A Joint Sitting of the Parliament is resorted to, for resolving the deadlock between two Houses of the Parliament for passing which of the following Bills? 1. Money Bill 2. Constitutional Amendment Bill 3. Ordinary Bill Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. Under Article 108, a **Joint Sitting** can ONLY be convened to resolve legislative deadlocks regarding an **Ordinary Bill** (or certain financial bills). It cannot be used for a **Money Bill** (where Lok Sabha reigns supreme) or a **Constitutional Amendment Bill** (which requires special majority passage in each House separately).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The Directive Principles of State Policy are meant for promoting social and economic democracy in India.
2. The Fundamental Rights enshrined in Part III of the Constitution of India are ordinarily subject to reasonable restrictions.
3. Secularism is one of the basic features of Constitution of any country.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. The Directive Principles of State Policy are meant for promoting social and economic democracy in India. 2. The Fundamental Rights enshrined in Part III of the Constitution of India are ordinarily subject to reasonable restrictions. 3. Secularism is one of the basic features of Constitution of any country. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The **DPSP** are specifically intended to establish a welfare state by promoting **social and economic democracy**. The **Fundamental Rights** are not absolute and are subject to enumerated **reasonable restrictions**. Statement 3 is false, as **Secularism is not inherently a basic feature of *every* country's constitution** (e.g., theocratic states), though it is a basic feature of India's.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The President of India is elected by an Electoral College comprising of elected members of which of the following?
1. Both the Houses of the Parliament
2. The Legislative Assemblies of States
3. The Legislative Councils of States
4. The Legislative Assemblies of NCT of Delhi and Puducherry
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The President of India is elected by an Electoral College comprising of elected members of which of the following? 1. Both the Houses of the Parliament 2. The Legislative Assemblies of States 3. The Legislative Councils of States 4. The Legislative Assemblies of NCT of Delhi and Puducherry Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. Under Article 54, the electoral college for the President consists strictly of the **elected members of both Houses of Parliament**, the **elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of the States**, and the **elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of NCT of Delhi and Puducherry**. Members of Legislative Councils do not participate.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Consider the following statements about the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes under the provisions of the Constitution of India :
1. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the Government educational institutions.
2. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the private educational institutions aided by the State.
3. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the private educational institutions not aided by the State.
4. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the minority educational institutions as described in Article 30.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
