questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes under the provisions of the Constitution of India : 1. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the Government educational institutions. 2. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the private educational institutions aided by the State. 3. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the private educational institutions not aided by the State. 4. State can make any special provision relating to their admission to the minority educational institutions as described in Article 30. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Under **Article 15(5)**, the State is heavily empowered to make special provisions for the advancement of SCs and STs regarding admission to educational institutions, including **private educational institutions (whether aided or unaided by the State)**. However, this explicitly **does not apply to minority educational institutions** referred to in Article 30(1).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Article 21 of the Constitution of India includes
1. Rights of transgenders
2. Rights of craniopagus twins
3. Rights of mentally retarded women to bear a child
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Article 21 of the Constitution of India includes 1. Rights of transgenders 2. Rights of craniopagus twins 3. Rights of mentally retarded women to bear a child Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Over the years, the Supreme Court has expansively interpreted **Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)** to encompass the **rights of transgenders** (NALSA judgment), the delicate **rights of conjoined/craniopagus twins**, and the **reproductive rights** and dignity of mentally retarded women.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Who among the following was not a member of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was not a member of the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **B. N. Rau**. Sir **Benegal Narsing Rau (B. N. Rau)** served as the crucial **Constitutional Advisor** to the Constituent Assembly. While he prepared the vital initial draft of the Constitution, he was **not a member of the Drafting Committee** (which was chaired by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A Money Bill passed by the Lok Sabha can be held up by the Rajya Sabha for how many weeks?
questions.questionText: A Money Bill passed by the Lok Sabha can be held up by the Rajya Sabha for how many weeks?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Two**. According to the strict financial provisions of **Article 109**, after a Money Bill is successfully passed by the Lok Sabha, the Rajya Sabha has a maximum allowable period of exactly **14 days (two weeks)** to return the bill with or without its recommendations.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Constitutional safeguards to civil servants are ensured by
questions.questionText: Constitutional safeguards to civil servants are ensured by
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 311**. **Article 311** provides massive constitutional safeguards to persons employed in civil capacities under the Union or a State, ensuring they cannot be arbitrarily dismissed or removed by an authority subordinate to the one that appointed them, and not without a proper **inquiry**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: A writ issued to secure the release of a person found to be detained illegally is
questions.questionText: A writ issued to secure the release of a person found to be detained illegally is
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Habeas corpus**. The prerogative writ of **Habeas Corpus** (literally meaning "to have the body") is a powerful order issued by a court to a person who has detained another unlawfully, commanding them to produce the body of the detainee before the court.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following cannot be introduced first in the Rajya Sabha?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following cannot be introduced first in the Rajya Sabha?

options.d: Bill to alter the boundaries of any State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Annual Financial Statement**. The **Annual Financial Statement (Budget)** is a massive financial document that, much like a Money Bill, **must be introduced first in the Lok Sabha**. The Rajya Sabha can only discuss it but cannot vote on the demands for grants.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The National Commission for Women was created by
questions.questionText: The National Commission for Women was created by

options.a: An amendment in the Constitution of India
options.b: A decision of the Union Cabinet
options.c: An Act passed by the Parliament
options.d: An order of the President of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **an Act passed by the Parliament**. The **National Commission for Women (NCW)** is not a constitutional body; it is a **statutory body** established in January 1992 under the explicit provisions of the **National Commission for Women Act, 1990**, passed by the Indian Parliament.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Who among the following was the Chief Justice of India when Public Interest Litigation (PIL) was introduced in the Indian Judicial System?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was the Chief Justice of India when Public Interest Litigation (PIL) was introduced in the Indian Judicial System?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **P.N. Bhagwati**. Justice **P.N. Bhagwati** (along with Justice V.R. Krishna Iyer) is widely celebrated as the judicial pioneer of the **Public Interest Litigation (PIL)** movement in the Indian judicial system, revolutionizing access to justice by relaxing the strict rules of *locus standi*.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding Uniform Civil Code as provided under Article 44 of the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding Uniform Civil Code as provided under Article 44 of the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: It is a Fundamental Right of every Indian citizen.
options.b: The State shall endeavour to secure it for citizens throughout the territory of India.
options.c: It is not enforceable by any Court.
options.d: It is not enforceable by a Court yet the Constitution requires that as a principle it should be fundamental in the governance of our country.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It is a Fundamental Right of every Indian citizen.** This is completely legally incorrect. The **Uniform Civil Code (Article 44)** is firmly embedded in Part IV of the Constitution as a **Directive Principle of State Policy**, making it a non-justiciable directive, rather than an enforceable Fundamental Right.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the All India Services is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the All India Services is correct?

options.a: The All India Services may be created by an Act of the Parliament.
options.b: The endorsement of the Rajya Sabha is not essential for the creation of the All India Services.
options.c: The rules of recruitment to the All India Services are determined by the UPSC.
options.d: The conditions of service to the All India Services may be altered by the UPSC.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The All India Services may be created by an Act of the Parliament.** Under **Article 312**, if the Rajya Sabha passes a resolution supported by a two-thirds majority, **Parliament may by law** provide for the creation of one or more new **All India Services** common to the Union and the States.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about emergency provisions under the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about emergency provisions under the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: The powers of the Union Executive extend to giving directions to the States concerning the exercise of their powers.
options.b: The Union Executive can issue a provision relating to reduction of salaries of employees of the State Governments.
options.c: Governors have no emergency powers like the President of India.
options.d: If the Governor of a State is satisfied that a situation has arisen whereby the financial stability or credit of the State is threatened, he may declare financial emergency in the Stat

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **If the Governor of a State is satisfied that a situation has arisen whereby the financial stability or credit of the State is threatened, he may declare financial emergency in the State.** This is legally false. The power to declare a **Financial Emergency (Article 360)** rests strictly and exclusively with the **President of India**, not the Governor of any State.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Why was constitutional amendment needed for introducing GST?
questions.questionText: Why was constitutional amendment needed for introducing GST?

options.a: States were not willing to agree with the Union for introduction of GST without amendment in the Constitution.
options.b: GST was to be implemented on concurrent base and Article 246 was inadequate for such a case.
options.c: The Empowered Committee of Finance Ministers had recommended for constitutional amendment.
options.d: The GST Council had recommended for constitutional amendment so that its power enhances.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **GST was to be implemented on concurrent base and Article 246 was inadequate for such a case.** Because the **Goods and Services Tax (GST)** required the simultaneous and concurrent taxation of both goods and services by the Centre and the States, the existing **Article 246 was inadequate**. The 101st Amendment introduced **Article 246A** to grant special, concurrent legislative powers.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: On 26th November, 1949, which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India came into effect?
1. Citizenship
2. Elections
3. Provisional Parliament
4. Fundamental Rights
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: On 26th November, 1949, which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India came into effect? 1. Citizenship 2. Elections 3. Provisional Parliament 4. Fundamental Rights Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. According to Article 394, certain critical provisions pertaining to **Citizenship, Elections, Provisional Parliament**, and temporary/transitional provisions came into force immediately upon adoption on **26th November 1949**. The **Fundamental Rights** (Part III) came into force with the rest of the Constitution on January 26, 1950.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding the Constituent Assembly of India is/are correct?
1. The Assembly was elected indirectly by the members of the Provincial Legislative Assemblies.
2. The elections were held on the basis of Universal Adult Franchise.
3. The scheme of election was laid down by the Cabinet Delegation.
4. The distribution of seats was done on the basis of the Mountbatten Plan.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding the Constituent Assembly of India is/are correct? 1. The Assembly was elected indirectly by the members of the Provincial Legislative Assemblies. 2. The elections were held on the basis of Universal Adult Franchise. 3. The scheme of election was laid down by the Cabinet Delegation. 4. The distribution of seats was done on the basis of the Mountbatten Plan. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. The Constituent Assembly was formed under the framework of the **Cabinet Mission Plan**, and its members were **indirectly elected** by the Provincial Legislative Assemblies. It was explicitly **not based on Universal Adult Franchise**, and the initial seat distribution predated the Mountbatten Plan.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: The basic structure doctrine with regard to the Constitution of India relates to
1. the power of judicial review
2. the judgment in Kesavananda Bharati case (1973)
3. the constraints on Article 368 of the Constitution of India
4. the judgment in Golaknath case (1967)
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The basic structure doctrine with regard to the Constitution of India relates to 1. the power of judicial review 2. the judgment in Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) 3. the constraints on Article 368 of the Constitution of India 4. the judgment in Golaknath case (1967) Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. The **Basic Structure Doctrine** was definitively established in the landmark **Kesavananda Bharati case (1973)**. It places inherent **constraints on the amending power of Parliament (Article 368)** and relies fundamentally on the **power of judicial review**. The earlier Golaknath case (1967) dealt with the immutability of Fundamental Rights prior to the formulation of this specific doctrine.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following fundamental rights as enshrined in the Constitution of India belong only to the citizens?
1. Article 19 (Protection of right to freedom of speech)
2. Article 21 (Protection of life and personal liberty)
3. Article 15 (Prohibition of discrimination)
4. Article 16 (Equality of opportunity)
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following fundamental rights as enshrined in the Constitution of India belong only to the citizens? 1. Article 19 (Protection of right to freedom of speech) 2. Article 21 (Protection of life and personal liberty) 3. Article 15 (Prohibition of discrimination) 4. Article 16 (Equality of opportunity) Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4**. Fundamental Rights that are exclusively available to **citizens only** include Articles 15, 16, 19, 29, and 30. Therefore, **Article 15, Article 16, and Article 19** belong strictly to citizens. **Article 21** is available to all persons, both citizens and non-citizens.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: A citizen of India will lose his or her citizenship if he or she
1. renounces Indian citizenship
2. voluntarily acquires the citizenship of another country
3. marries a citizen of another country
4. criticises the Government
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: A citizen of India will lose his or her citizenship if he or she 1. renounces Indian citizenship 2. voluntarily acquires the citizenship of another country 3. marries a citizen of another country 4. criticises the Government Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The Citizenship Act of 1955 outlines three formal ways to lose citizenship: **Renunciation**, **Termination** (voluntarily acquiring the citizenship of another country), and **Deprivation**. Merely marrying a foreign national or criticizing the government does not constitutionally result in the loss of Indian citizenship.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not correct with respect to protection of individuals being tried for offences?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct with respect to protection of individuals being tried for offences?

options.a: A confession can never be used as evidence against the accused.
options.b: The accused must have violated an existing law.
options.c: An accused cannot be tried and punished for the same offence again.
options.d: The quantum of punishment must be provided in law as it existed on the date of commission of an offenc

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A confession can never be used as evidence against the accused.** This statement is legally incorrect. While Article 20(3) protects an accused against compelled self-incrimination (forced confessions), a **voluntary confession** made before a magistrate is perfectly valid and is routinely used as evidence against the accused in Indian criminal law.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not correct with respect to Article 32 of the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not correct with respect to Article 32 of the Constitution of India?

options.a: It provides remedies to citizens for the enforcement of fundamental rights.
options.b: It is a part of fundamental rights.
options.c: The Supreme Court cannot refuse a writ petition under Article 32 on the ground of delay.
options.d: Protection under Article 32 also applies to the enforcement of ordinary law which has nothing to do with the fundamental rights.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Protection under Article 32 also applies to the enforcement of ordinary law which has nothing to do with the fundamental rights.** This is false. The constitutional remedy provided under **Article 32** is exclusively dedicated and strictly limited to the enforcement of **Fundamental Rights** only. It cannot be legally invoked for the enforcement of ordinary statutory laws.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following writs is issued by the Supreme Court to secure the freedom of a person upon unlawful arrest?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following writs is issued by the Supreme Court to secure the freedom of a person upon unlawful arrest?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Habeas Corpus**. The prerogative writ of **Habeas Corpus** (literally "to have the body") is a fundamental constitutional remedy issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts to protect an individual's personal liberty and secure their immediate release from **unlawful arrest or detention**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: According to the provisions of the Constitution of India,
which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?
questions.questionText: According to the provisions of the Constitution of India, which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?

options.c: To provide education to one's child
options.d: To promote village and cottage industries

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To promote village and cottage industries**. Promoting village and cottage industries is explicitly outlined under **Article 43** as a **Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP)** meant for the State. It is not enumerated as a Fundamental Duty of citizens under Article 51A.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Under Article 352 of the Constitution of India, an emergency can be declared if security of any part of India is threatened by
1. war
2. external aggression
3. armed rebellion
4. internal disturbance
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Under Article 352 of the Constitution of India, an emergency can be declared if security of any part of India is threatened by 1. war 2. external aggression 3. armed rebellion 4. internal disturbance Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Article 352**, a National Emergency can be formally declared on strictly three grounds: **war, external aggression, or armed rebellion**. The vague ground of "internal disturbance" was decisively removed by the 44th Amendment Act, 1978.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements is not true of the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not true of the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005?

options.a: This Act provides civil remedies to protect a woman subjected to domestic violence.
options.b: Only women can make a complaint under this legislation.
options.c: Relief may be sought only against the husband or a male live-in partner with whom the woman has lived in a domestic relationship.
options.d: The Act includes not just wives but also women in marriage-like relationships.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Relief may be sought only against the husband or a male live-in partner with whom the woman has lived in a domestic relationship.** The Supreme Court has clarified and expanded the legal scope of the Act, firmly stating that complaints can also be filed against **female relatives of the husband or male partner**, making this statement legally incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following are constitutional provisions and laws for the protection of the rights of the Scheduled Castes in India?
1. Article 17 of the Constitution of India
2. The Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955
3. The Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following are constitutional provisions and laws for the protection of the rights of the Scheduled Castes in India? 1. Article 17 of the Constitution of India 2. The Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955 3. The Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The robust legal framework protecting Scheduled Castes includes **Article 17** (Abolition of Untouchability) and the powerful statutory laws derived from it, namely **The Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955** and the stringent **SC and ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following is not true about the powers of the Supreme Court?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not true about the powers of the Supreme Court?

options.a: The Supreme Court has original and exclusive jurisdiction in inter-governmental disputes.
options.b: The Supreme Court has advisory jurisdiction on a question of law or fact which may be referred to it by the President of India.
options.c: The Supreme Court has the power to review its own judgment or order.
options.d: The Supreme Court has the exclusive power to issue writs to protect the fundamental rights of the peopl

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Supreme Court has the exclusive power to issue writs to protect the fundamental rights of the people.** This is false because the constitutional power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights is **concurrent**, functionally shared between the **Supreme Court (Article 32)** and the **High Courts (Article 226)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: The Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India pertains to the administration of tribal areas in which of the following States?
questions.questionText: The Sixth Schedule of the Constitution of India pertains to the administration of tribal areas in which of the following States?

options.d: Manipur, Meghalaya, Tripura and Arunachal Pradesh

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram**. The **Sixth Schedule** contains robust constitutional provisions precisely targeted for the administration of tribal areas in the four North-Eastern states of **Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: It was inserted by the Constitution (First Amendment) Act, 1951.
options.b: The Acts and Regulations specified in the Ninth Schedule shall become void on the ground that it violates a fundamental right in Part III of the Constitution.
options.c: The Supreme Court has the power of judicial review of an Act included in the Ninth Schedule on the doctrine of basic structure.
options.d: The appropriate Legislature can repeal or amend an Act specified in the Ninth Schedul

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Acts and Regulations specified in the Ninth Schedule shall become void on the ground that it violates a fundamental right in Part III of the Constitution.** This statement is inherently incorrect. The fundamental purpose of inserting the **Ninth Schedule** was to act as a protective shield, explicitly ensuring that laws placed within it **cannot be declared void merely on the ground that they violate Fundamental Rights**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the conduct of elections in India is/are not correct?
1. The responsibility for the preparation of the electoral rolls is vested in the Election Commission of India.
2. The Model Code of Conduct comes into existence as soon as the date of the election is announced.
3. The laws relating to delimitation of constituencies are made by the Election Commission of India.
4. No election shall be called in question except by an election petition.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the conduct of elections in India is/are not correct? 1. The responsibility for the preparation of the electoral rolls is vested in the Election Commission of India. 2. The Model Code of Conduct comes into existence as soon as the date of the election is announced. 3. The laws relating to delimitation of constituencies are made by the Election Commission of India. 4. No election shall be called in question except by an election petition. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. The laws relating to the **delimitation of constituencies are made exclusively by the Parliament** (via Delimitation Acts), not by the Election Commission of India. The Delimitation Commission operates strictly within the bounds of these parliamentary laws.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes is not correct?

options.a: The Union and every State Government shall consult the Commission on all major policy matters affecting Scheduled Tribes.
options.b: All the reports of the Commission and its recommendations shall be laid only before Lok Sabha.
options.c: The Commission, while investigating any matter, has all the powers of a Civil Court.
options.d: The Commission has the power to regulate its own procedures.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **All the reports of the Commission and its recommendations shall be laid only before Lok Sabha.** The reports of the National Commission for Scheduled Tribes are formally presented to the **President**, who then causes them to be laid before **both Houses of Parliament**, not exclusively the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The right to form associations and unions is a right
questions.questionText: The right to form associations and unions is a right

options.b: To freedom guaranteed to citizens only

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **to freedom guaranteed to citizens only**. The right to form associations or unions is enshrined under **Article 19(1)(c)**. All rights categorized under Article 19 are Fundamental Rights **guaranteed exclusively to the citizens of India** and not to foreigners or legal entities.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Some Indian territory was transferred in 2015 to Bangladesh by following which procedure?
questions.questionText: Some Indian territory was transferred in 2015 to Bangladesh by following which procedure?

options.a: By an agreement between the Government of India and the Government of Bangladesh
options.b: By a legislation passed by the Parliament amending Schedule 1 to the Constitution of India
options.c: By amending Schedule 1 to the Constitution of India by exercising amending power of the Parliament
options.d: By amending Schedule 1 to the Constitution of India by exercising amending power of the Parliament and ratification by sixteen State Legislatures

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **By amending Schedule 1 to the Constitution of India by exercising amending power of the Parliament**. Ceding Indian sovereign territory to a foreign nation fundamentally alters the territorial definition of India and requires a formal **Constitutional Amendment under Article 368**. This historic transfer was achieved via the **100th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2015**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A Bill is deemed to be a 'Money Bill' if it has any provisions dealing with
1. imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax
2. appropriation of money from the Consolidated Fund of India
3. imposition of fines or other pecuniary penalties
4. payment of fee for licences or fee for service rendered
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: A Bill is deemed to be a 'Money Bill' if it has any provisions dealing with 1. imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax 2. appropriation of money from the Consolidated Fund of India 3. imposition of fines or other pecuniary penalties 4. payment of fee for licences or fee for service rendered Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. Under **Article 110**, a bill is deemed a **Money Bill** if it explicitly deals with the **regulation of any tax** or the **appropriation of money out of the Consolidated Fund of India**. It explicitly excludes bills that solely deal with the imposition of fines, pecuniary penalties, or local fees.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVII: Official Language
statementLines: Which of the following is/are not central feature(s) of Article 343 of the Constitution of India?
1. Hindi in Devanagari Script shall be the national language of the Union.
2. The official language of the Union shall be Hindi in Devanagari Script.
3. English language shall continue to be used for official purposes within States.
4. If two or more States agree, Hindi language should be the official language of communication between the States.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not central feature(s) of Article 343 of the Constitution of India? 1. Hindi in Devanagari Script shall be the national language of the Union. 2. The official language of the Union shall be Hindi in Devanagari Script. 3. English language shall continue to be used for official purposes within States. 4. If two or more States agree, Hindi language should be the official language of communication between the States. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 3 and 4**. **Article 343** states that the "official language" of the Union shall be Hindi (making statement 1 incorrect as the Constitution uses "official language", not "national language"). Furthermore, Article 343 does not explicitly regulate internal state language policy or inter-state agreements (these are dealt with under Articles 345/346). Therefore, 1, 3, and 4 are NOT central features of Article 343 itself.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: After the general elections, the Pro tem Speaker is
questions.questionText: After the general elections, the Pro tem Speaker is

options.b: Appointed by the President of India
options.c: Appointed by the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court
options.d: The senior most member of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **appointed by the President of India**. While it is a well-established parliamentary convention that the senior-most member of the newly elected Lok Sabha is selected, formally and constitutionally, the **Pro tem Speaker is appointed directly by the President of India** to administer oaths to new members. *(

Note: Official UPSC keys have occasionally accepted (d) based on convention, but (b) represents the exact constitutional mechanism).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following is/are not central tenet(s) of the Constitution of India?
1. Prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion
2. Gives official status to certain religions
3. Provides freedom to profess any religion
4. Ensures equality of all citizens within religious communities
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not central tenet(s) of the Constitution of India? 1. Prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion 2. Gives official status to certain religions 3. Provides freedom to profess any religion 4. Ensures equality of all citizens within religious communities Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The Indian Constitution is deeply rooted in Secularism. It inherently prohibits discrimination, grants freedom to profess religion, and strives for equality. However, it absolutely **does not give official status to any religion**, keeping the State structurally neutral.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Constitution of India guarantees freedom of thought and expression to all its citizens subject to
1. implementation of Directive Principles
2. Fundamental Duties
3. Right to Equality
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The Constitution of India guarantees freedom of thought and expression to all its citizens subject to 1. implementation of Directive Principles 2. Fundamental Duties 3. Right to Equality Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question appears **incomplete or garbled** in the source material options. Freedom of speech and expression under **Article 19(1)(a)** is subject to reasonable restrictions explicitly listed under **Article 19(2)**, such as sovereignty, security, public order, and decency, not broadly the implementation of DPSP or Fundamental Duties.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a component of the Realist Theory?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a component of the Realist Theory?

options.b: State sovereignty is important for the affirmation of juridical authority over territory
options.c: The primary objective of all States is survival
options.d: Survival can be assured in cooperation with international organizations

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Survival can be assured in cooperation with international organizations**. **Realist theory** in international relations heavily argues that the international system is anarchic and states must rely on self-help and military power for survival. Heavy reliance on cooperation with **international organizations** is a core tenet of **Liberalism**, not Realism.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following does not form part of Immanuel Kant's theory of 'perpetual peace'?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following does not form part of Immanuel Kant's theory of 'perpetual peace'?

options.b: Federal contract among States to abolish war

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **World Government**. In Kant's renowned essay *Perpetual Peace*, he strongly advocates for a federation of free republics (a pacific union) to prevent war, but he explicitly **rejects the idea of a singular, overarching global "World Government"** or super-state, warning it could lead to universal despotism.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the process of the Parliament to make new States is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the process of the Parliament to make new States is not correct?

options.a: The Parliament may by law form a new State and alter the boundaries or names of existing States.
options.b: A Bill to this effect cannot be introduced in the Parliament except on the recommendation of the President.
options.c: A Bill to this effect may be referred by the President to the Legislature of the affected State.
options.d: Such a law will fall under the purview of Article 368.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Such a law will fall under the purview of Article 368.** This statement is constitutionally false. **Article 4** clearly specifies that any law forming a new state or altering boundaries under Article 3 **shall not be deemed to be an amendment of the Constitution for the purposes of Article 368**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to citizenship provisions of the Constitution of India is/are correct?

1. No person shall be a citizen of India by virtue of Article 5, or be deemed to be a citizen of India by virtue of Article 6 or Article 8, if he/she has voluntarily acquired the citizenship of any foreign State.
2. The Parliament has power to make any provision with respect to the acquisition and termination of citizenship and all other matters relating to citizenship.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to citizenship provisions of the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. No person shall be a citizen of India by virtue of Article 5, or be deemed to be a citizen of India by virtue of Article 6 or Article 8, if he/she has voluntarily acquired the citizenship of any foreign State. 2. The Parliament has power to make any provision with respect to the acquisition and termination of citizenship and all other matters relating to citizenship. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. **Article 9** explicitly revokes Indian citizenship for anyone voluntarily acquiring foreign citizenship. **Article 11** grants complete and overriding authority to the **Parliament** to enact laws regulating the acquisition, termination, and all matters relating to citizenship (which birthed the Citizenship Act, 1955).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The protection against arrest and detention under Article 22 of the Constitution of India is not available to
1. an enemy alien
2. a person detained under a preventive detention law
3. a foreigner
4. an overseas citizen of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The protection against arrest and detention under Article 22 of the Constitution of India is not available to 1. an enemy alien 2. a person detained under a preventive detention law 3. a foreigner 4. an overseas citizen of India Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. According to **Article 22(3)**, the crucial protections provided in clauses (1) and (2) regarding arrest and detention specifically do not apply to any person who is an **enemy alien** or to any person arrested or detained under any law providing for **preventive detention**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Freedom of conscience under the Constitution of India is subject to
1. public order, morality and health
2. a law providing for social welfare and reform
3. opening Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all Hindus
4. defamation or incitement to an offence
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Freedom of conscience under the Constitution of India is subject to 1. public order, morality and health 2. a law providing for social welfare and reform 3. opening Hindu religious institutions of a public character to all Hindus 4. defamation or incitement to an offence Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Under **Article 25**, the right to freedom of conscience and religion is inherently subject to **public order, morality, and health**. The state can also regulate it by making laws for **social welfare and reform**, and specifically for **opening Hindu religious institutions of a public character** to all classes and sections of Hindus.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following
1. Equality before Law
2. Right against Discrimination
3. Equality of Opportunity
4. Protection of Life
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following 1. Equality before Law 2. Right against Discrimination 3. Equality of Opportunity 4. Protection of Life Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 and 4 only**. This question appears **incompletely framed** in the source material. However, it is fundamentally targeting which rights apply to both citizens and non-citizens. **Equality before Law (Article 14)** and **Protection of Life (Article 21)** are applicable to all persons, whereas Articles 15 and 16 (Discrimination and Opportunity) are only for citizens. *(

Note: The source text marked "b) 1 and 4 only" despite the garbled options).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the formation of the Constituent Assembly is/are correct?
1. The members of the Constituent Assembly were chosen on the basis of the provincial elections of 1946.
2. The Constituent Assembly did not include representatives of the Princely States.
3. The discussions within the Constituent Assembly were not influenced by opinions expressed by the public.
4. In order to create a sense of collective participation, submissions were solicited from the public.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the formation of the Constituent Assembly is/are correct? 1. The members of the Constituent Assembly were chosen on the basis of the provincial elections of 1946. 2. The Constituent Assembly did not include representatives of the Princely States. 3. The discussions within the Constituent Assembly were not influenced by opinions expressed by the public. 4. In order to create a sense of collective participation, submissions were solicited from the public. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 4**. The Constituent Assembly members were indirectly elected based on the **provincial elections of 1946**. It heavily involved the public, **soliciting submissions and feedback** on the draft to ensure democratic participation. It definitely included representatives from the Princely States, making statement 2 incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following laws have been repealed by the Constitution of India?
1. The Government of India Act, 1935
2. The Indian Independence Act, 1947
3. The Abolition of Privy Council Jurisdiction Act, 1949
4. The Preventive Detention Act, 1950
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following laws have been repealed by the Constitution of India? 1. The Government of India Act, 1935 2. The Indian Independence Act, 1947 3. The Abolition of Privy Council Jurisdiction Act, 1949 4. The Preventive Detention Act, 1950 Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. According to the explicit wording of **Article 395**, the Constitution of India formally repealed the **Government of India Act, 1935** and the **Indian Independence Act, 1947**, effectively severing the final colonial legal umbilical cords.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: A writ of Habeas Corpus for the release of a person can be issued
1. where the arrest or detention has taken place in contravention of the procedure established by law
2. to secure the release of a person imprisoned on a criminal charge
3. where the arrest has taken place for contempt of Court or the Parliament
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: A writ of Habeas Corpus for the release of a person can be issued 1. where the arrest or detention has taken place in contravention of the procedure established by law 2. to secure the release of a person imprisoned on a criminal charge 3. where the arrest has taken place for contempt of Court or the Parliament Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. **Habeas Corpus** is strictly issued when an arrest or detention is unlawful or in **contravention of the procedure established by law**. The writ is not issued if the detention is lawful (such as imprisonment upon conviction on a criminal charge, or lawful commitment for contempt of a court or Parliament).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: Which one of the following changes has not been made to the Citizenship Act of India by the Amendment in 2015?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following changes has not been made to the Citizenship Act of India by the Amendment in 2015?

options.a: The Overseas Citizens of India will now be called the Overseas Citizens of India Cardholders
options.b: The Non-Resident Indians are entitled to vote in elections in India
options.c: The Persons of Indian Origin have been placed at par with the Overseas Citizens of India
options.d: The Persons of Indian Origin are now entitled to lifelong visa to visit India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Non-Resident Indians are entitled to vote in elections in India**. The granting of voting rights to NRIs is governed by amendments to the Representation of the People Act, 1950, and election rules, **not by the Citizenship (Amendment) Act of 2015**. The 2015 Act primarily merged the PIO and OCI schemes.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about Electoral Government in India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about Electoral Government in India is not correct?

options.a: The superintendence, direction and control of elections are vested in the Election Commission of India.
options.b: There is one general electoral roll for every territorial constituency.
options.c: The Parliament has the power to make laws relating to the delimitation of constituencies.
options.d: The Supreme Court of India has the authority to scrutinize the validity of a law relating to delimitation of constituencies.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Supreme Court of India has the authority to scrutinize the validity of a law relating to delimitation of constituencies.** This statement is constitutionally inaccurate. Under **Article 329(a)**, the validity of any law relating to the **delimitation of constituencies** or the allotment of seats made under Article 327 or 328 **shall not be called in question in any court**, providing complete immunity.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a recommendation of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a recommendation of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?

options.a: Share of States in Central Divisible Pool is increased from 32 per cent to 42 per cent
options.b: Area under forest cover is an important variable in distribution of States' share among States
options.c: Fiscal discipline is dropped as a variable in distribution of States' share among States
options.d: Sector specific grant is recommended as in the previous Finance Commissions

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Sector specific grant is recommended as in the previous Finance Commissions**. The **Fourteenth Finance Commission** made a landmark decision to **drop sector-specific grants**, arguing that the dramatically increased devolution of untied funds (from 32% to 42%) gave states adequate autonomy to address their own sector-specific needs.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
questions.microTopic: UT, Scheduled Areas, Other Scheduled Areas
statementLines: Under Article 371A of the Constitution of India, with respect to which of the following item(s) the Acts of the Parliament of India shall not apply to the State of Nagaland, unless the Legislative Assembly of the State of Nagaland by a resolution so decides ?
1. Religious or social practices of Nagas
2. Naga customary law and procedure
3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources
4. Boundaries of Nagaland with other states Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Under Article 371A of the Constitution of India, with respect to which of the following item(s) the Acts of the Parliament of India shall not apply to the State of Nagaland, unless the Legislative Assembly of the State of Nagaland by a resolution so decides ? 1. Religious or social practices of Nagas 2. Naga customary law and procedure 3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources 4. Boundaries of Nagaland with other states Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. Under the special provisions of **Article 371A**, acts of Parliament relating to the **religious or social practices of the Nagas**, **Naga customary law and procedure**, administration of civil/criminal justice involving Naga law, and **ownership and transfer of land and its resources** do not apply to Nagaland unless the State Legislative Assembly so decides.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Under Article 191 of the Constitution of India, a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state if the person holds any office of profit under:
1. the Government of India.
2. any State Government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Under Article 191 of the Constitution of India, a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state if the person holds any office of profit under: 1. the Government of India. 2. any State Government. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. **Article 191** mandates that a person is disqualified for election to or membership of the State Legislature if they hold any "office of profit" under **the Government of India** or under the **Government of any State**, unless the office is explicitly exempted by law.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the constitution of Finance Commission is/are correct?
1. The Government of India has recently constituted the Sixteenth Finance Commission under the Chairmanship of Dr. Arvind Panagariya.
2. Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 263 of the Constitution of India.
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the constitution of Finance Commission is/are correct? 1. The Government of India has recently constituted the Sixteenth Finance Commission under the Chairmanship of Dr. Arvind Panagariya. 2. Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 263 of the Constitution of India. Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. The Government indeed appointed **Dr. Arvind Panagariya** to head the **16th Finance Commission**.

Statement 2 is incorrect because the Finance Commission is a constitutional body set up in pursuance of **Article 280**, not Article 263 (which is for the Inter-State Council).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: By
which one of the following amendments, was Article 51A, relating to the Fundamental Duties, inserted into the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: By which one of the following amendments, was Article 51A, relating to the Fundamental Duties, inserted into the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Constitution (42nd Amendment) Act**. The **42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976**, acting upon the comprehensive recommendations of the Swaran Singh Committee, introduced Part IVA and **Article 51A**, formally prescribing ten Fundamental Duties for citizens.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Who among the following had the shortest span in office as the Prime Minister of India?
questions.questionText: Who among the following had the shortest span in office as the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Chaudhary Charan Singh**. Among the options provided, **Chaudhary Charan Singh** served the shortest continuous tenure as the Prime Minister of India, remaining in office for only 170 days (July 1979 to January 1980) before resigning.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following does not fall under the jurisdiction of the apex court of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following does not fall under the jurisdiction of the apex court of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Collegiate Jurisdiction**. The Supreme Court of India is constitutionally vested with **Original, Appellate, and Advisory jurisdiction**. The term "Collegiate Jurisdiction" is fundamentally non-existent in the constitutional taxonomy of the Indian Judiciary.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a Constitutional Commission of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a Constitutional Commission of India?

options.a: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes
options.b: The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
options.c: The National Commission for Backward Classes

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The National Commission for Women**. While the SC, ST, and BC Commissions enjoy constitutional status (Articles 338, 338A, and 338B), the **National Commission for Women** is strictly a **statutory body**, established via the National Commission for Women Act, 1990.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The elections to the Lower House of the Parliament of India, held in 2019, constituted the
questions.questionText: The elections to the Lower House of the Parliament of India, held in 2019, constituted the
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **17th Lok Sabha**. The landmark general elections held across the nation in **2019** resulted in the formation of the **17th Lok Sabha** of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Panchayati Raj Institutions are primarily the institutions of
questions.questionText: Panchayati Raj Institutions are primarily the institutions of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Self-Government**. As enshrined by the 73rd Constitutional Amendment and articulated in Article 243G, Panchayats are envisioned to function as the premier grass-roots institutions of **local self-government** to foster democratic decentralization.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following is/are the right/rights mentioned in the 'American Declaration of Independence'?
1. Life
2. Liberty
3. Pursuit of Happiness
4. Fraternity
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are the right/rights mentioned in the 'American Declaration of Independence'? 1. Life 2. Liberty 3. Pursuit of Happiness 4. Fraternity Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The historic **American Declaration of Independence (1776)** famously asserts that all men are endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable rights, explicitly naming "**Life, Liberty and the pursuit of Happiness**". "Fraternity" is associated with the French Revolution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Which one of the following is correct in respect of total number of States and Union Territories in India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct in respect of total number of States and Union Territories in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **28 States and 8 Union Territories**. Following the reorganization of Jammu & Kashmir in 2019 and the merger of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu in 2020, India currently consists of exactly **28 States and 8 Union Territories**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following is the Head of the Committee appointed by the Supreme Court of India to enquire into the circumstances that led to the Prime Minister of India's convoy being stuck for several minutes on a flyover in Punjab recently?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is the Head of the Committee appointed by the Supreme Court of India to enquire into the circumstances that led to the Prime Minister of India's convoy being stuck for several minutes on a flyover in Punjab recently?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Justice Indu Malhotra**. The Supreme Court of India appointed a high-level investigative panel headed by retired Supreme Court Judge **Justice Indu Malhotra** to thoroughly probe the massive security breach during the Prime Minister's visit to Punjab.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following is the Chairperson of the Goods and Services Tax Council?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is the Chairperson of the Goods and Services Tax Council?

options.c: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Union Finance Minister**. Article 279A, inserted by the 101st Amendment, establishes the **Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council**, and explicitly designates the **Union Finance Minister** as the ex-officio Chairperson of this powerful federal body.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following provides for the complete equality of men and women in India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following provides for the complete equality of men and women in India ?

options.a: Articles 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India
options.b: Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India
options.d: Article 20 of the Constitution of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Articles 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India**. The foundation for absolute gender equality is established in **Article 14** (Equality before law) and expanded in **Article 15**, which explicitly prohibits the State from discriminating against any citizen on grounds of **sex**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following is included in Article 51A (Part IV A) of the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is included in Article 51A (Part IV A) of the Constitution of India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Fundamental Duties**. **Part IV A**, consisting entirely of the singular **Article 51A**, was inserted by the 42nd Amendment Act in 1976 and exclusively enumerates the **Fundamental Duties** owed by every citizen of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: According to Article 79 of the Constitution of India, which of the following is/are described as a part of Parliament of India?
1. The House of the People
2. The Council of States
3. The President of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: According to Article 79 of the Constitution of India, which of the following is/are described as a part of Parliament of India? 1. The House of the People 2. The Council of States 3. The President of India Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. **Article 79** states: "There shall be a Parliament for the Union which shall consist of the **President** and two Houses to be known respectively as the **Council of States** (Rajya Sabha) and the **House of the People** (Lok Sabha)."

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which one of the following Directive Principles was inserted by the Constitution (42nd Amendment) Act, 1976 ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following Directive Principles was inserted by the Constitution (42nd Amendment) Act, 1976 ?

options.a: The State shall minimise inequalities in income
options.b: Equal justice and free legal aid

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Equal justice and free legal aid**. The **42nd Amendment Act (1976)** introduced several new directives, prominently including **Article 39A**, which obligates the State to promote **equal justice and to provide free legal aid** to the poor.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: A common High Court for two or more states can be established by
questions.questionText: A common High Court for two or more states can be established by

options.a: A law passed by the Parliament
options.b: An order of the Supreme Court of India
options.c: An order of the President of India
options.d: An amendment to the Constitution of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **a law passed by the Parliament**. Under **Article 231**, the Constitution explicitly grants the **Parliament** the exclusive legislative power to establish a **common High Court** for two or more States (or for two or more States and a Union territory) by passing a law.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: An Election Commissioner can be removed from office on the recommendation of
questions.questionText: An Election Commissioner can be removed from office on the recommendation of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Chief Election Commissioner**. According to Article 324(5), an **Election Commissioner** or a Regional Commissioner cannot be arbitrarily removed from their office except on the specific and formal recommendation of the **Chief Election Commissioner**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding the Rajya Sabha is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding the Rajya Sabha is NOT correct?

options.a: Its members are elected by the elected members of the Legislative Assembly of a State
options.b: The election follows the system of proportional representation by means of a single transferable vote
options.c: 1/3rd of its members retire after every two years
options.d: It is a permanent body, but can be dissolved earlier by the President

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It is a permanent body, but can be dissolved earlier by the President**. This statement contains a massive contradiction and falsehood. The Rajya Sabha is a continuing, permanent chamber of Parliament, and the Constitution explicitly mandates that it is **not subject to dissolution under any circumstances**, by the President or anyone else.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Fundamental right guaranteed under
which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is available only to the citizens of India ?
questions.questionText: Fundamental right guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is available only to the citizens of India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 19**. Fundamental Rights strictly restricted only to **citizens of India** include Articles 15, 16, **19**, 29, and 30. The other rights mentioned (Articles 20, 21, and 22) grant crucial protections to all persons, including citizens and foreigners residing in India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following about the Parliament of India is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following about the Parliament of India is NOT correct?

options.a: The Parliament consists of the President, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha
options.b: There are no nominated members in the Lok Sabha
options.c: The Rajya Sabha cannot be dissolved
options.d: Some members of the Rajya Sabha are nominated by the President

questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **There are no nominated members in the Lok Sabha**. This statement was technically incorrect at the time the question was framed (2018), as the President could nominate **two members from the Anglo-Indian community** under Article 331. *(

Note: This nomination provision was subsequently discontinued by the 104th Amendment Act in 2019).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is NOT correct?

options.a: He is appointed by the President of India
options.b: He can be removed from office in the same way as the judge of the Supreme Court of India
options.c: The CAG is eligible for further office under the Government of India after he has ceased to hold his office
options.d: The salary of the CAG is charged upon the Consolidated Fund of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The CAG is eligible for further office under the Government of India after he has ceased to hold his office**. This is false. To ensure absolute independence and prevent executive favor-seeking, **Article 148(4)** explicitly debars the CAG from holding **any further office** under the Government of India or under any State Government post-retirement.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The Superintendence, direction and control of elections in India is vested in
questions.questionText: The Superintendence, direction and control of elections in India is vested in
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Election Commission of India**. Under **Article 324**, the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President is constitutionally vested in the **Election Commission of India**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following provision(s) of the Constitution of India became effective from 26th November 1949 ?
1. Elections
2. Citizenship
3. Emergency provisions
4. Appointment of the Judges
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following provision(s) of the Constitution of India became effective from 26th November 1949 ? 1. Elections 2. Citizenship 3. Emergency provisions 4. Appointment of the Judges Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. According to **Article 394**, specific provisions pertaining to **Citizenship, Elections**, provisional Parliament, and temporary/transitional provisions came into force immediately on the date of adoption: **26th November 1949**. The rest of the Constitution (including Emergency provisions and Judiciary rules) came into effect on 26th January 1950.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Match List I with List II and
select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:
List-I (Amendment to the Constitution of India)
A. 52nd Amendment Act, 1985
B. 73rd Amendment Act, 1992
C. 61st Amendment Act, 1988
D. 86th Amendment Act, 2002 List-II (Subject)
1. Reduction of voting age from 21 to 18
2. Right to Education
3. Panchayati Raj
4. Disqualification on grounds of defection Code:
questions.questionText: Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists: List-I (Amendment to the Constitution of India) A. 52nd Amendment Act, 1985 B. 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 C. 61st Amendment Act, 1988 D. 86th Amendment Act, 2002 List-II (Subject) 1. Reduction of voting age from 21 to 18 2. Right to Education 3. Panchayati Raj 4. Disqualification on grounds of defection Code:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **4 3 1 2**. The **52nd Amendment** introduced the Anti-Defection Law (A-4). The **73rd Amendment** institutionalized Panchayati Raj (B-3). The **61st Amendment** reduced the voting age to 18 (C-1). The **86th Amendment** made the Right to Education a Fundamental Right (D-2).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one of the following pairs of the Schedule in the Constitution of India and its Content is not correctly matched ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following pairs of the Schedule in the Constitution of India and its Content is not correctly matched ?

options.b: Second Schedule: The forms of oaths and affirmations
options.c: Fourth Schedule: Allocation of seats in the Council of States
options.d: Tenth Schedul Provisions as to disqualification on the ground of defection

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Second Schedule: The forms of oaths and affirmations**. This is incorrectly matched. The **Second Schedule** deals with **salaries, allowances, and privileges** of top constitutional dignitaries. The forms of oaths and affirmations are actually contained in the **Third Schedule**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: Consider the following statements :
1. The Constitution of India empowers the Parliament to form new States and to alter the areas, boundaries or names of existing States by passing a resolution in simple majority.
2. Jammu and Kashmir has been given special status under Article 370 of the Constitution of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements : 1. The Constitution of India empowers the Parliament to form new States and to alter the areas, boundaries or names of existing States by passing a resolution in simple majority. 2. Jammu and Kashmir has been given special status under Article 370 of the Constitution of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Under **Article 3**, Parliament forms new states or alters boundaries by passing a regular law (bill) with a **simple majority**. Historically (prior to 2019), **Article 370** indeed provided special autonomous status to Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha may resign his office by writing, addressed to the
questions.questionText: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha may resign his office by writing, addressed to the

options.d: Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha**. According to **Article 94(b)** of the Constitution, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha can resign from office at any time by addressing a hand-written resignation letter exclusively to the **Deputy Speaker** of the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: The Election Commission recognizes a political party as a national party if
1. it secures at least six percent of the total valid votes polled in four or more States in a general election to the Lok Sabha or to the State Legislative Assemblies.
2. it wins at least four seats in a general election to the Lok Sabha from any State or States.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: The Election Commission recognizes a political party as a national party if 1. it secures at least six percent of the total valid votes polled in four or more States in a general election to the Lok Sabha or to the State Legislative Assemblies. 2. it wins at least four seats in a general election to the Lok Sabha from any State or States. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Under the Election Symbols Order, to be recognized as a **National Party**, one path is that a party must secure **6% of valid votes in four or more states** (in Lok Sabha or Assembly polls) AND simultaneously win **at least four Lok Sabha seats** from any state or states. *(

Note: The question splits a single composite condition into two statements).*

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Amendment procedure has been provided in Article 368 of the Constitution of India.
2. The consent of the States is mandatory for all Amendments to the Constitution of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
