questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Amendment procedure has been provided in Article 368 of the Constitution of India. 2. The consent of the States is mandatory for all Amendments to the Constitution of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. **Article 368** lays down the procedure for amending the Constitution. Statement 2 is entirely false; the **consent (ratification) of half the States is required ONLY for a specific subset** of amendments that alter the federal structure (like Presidential elections, SC/HC powers, or 7th Schedule lists). The vast majority of amendments do not require state consent.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is/are correct?
1. The CAG can attend the sittings of the Committee on Public Accounts.
2. The CAG can attend the sittings of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
3. The jurisdiction of CAG is co-extensive with powers of the Union Government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India is/are correct? 1. The CAG can attend the sittings of the Committee on Public Accounts. 2. The CAG can attend the sittings of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. 3. The jurisdiction of CAG is co-extensive with powers of the Union Government. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 only**. The CAG acts as a "friend, philosopher, and guide" to the **Public Accounts Committee** and attends its sittings. The CAG **cannot attend general sittings of the Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha**. The jurisdiction of the CAG goes beyond the Union Government, extending comprehensively to the accounts of all **State Governments** as well.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements relating to election as the President of India is/are correct?
1. A person above the age of thirty-five years is eligible for election as the President of India.
2. The President of India is eligible for re-election for more than one term.
3. A person is not eligible for election as the President of India if the person holds an office of profit.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements relating to election as the President of India is/are correct? 1. A person above the age of thirty-five years is eligible for election as the President of India. 2. The President of India is eligible for re-election for more than one term. 3. A person is not eligible for election as the President of India if the person holds an office of profit. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Under **Articles 57 and 58**, a Presidential candidate must have completed **35 years of age**, and must **not hold any office of profit** under the government. Furthermore, an incumbent or former President is perfectly **eligible for re-election** for any number of terms.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The citizens of India do not have
which one of the following Fundamental Rights ?
questions.questionText: The citizens of India do not have which one of the following Fundamental Rights ?

options.a: Right to reside and settle in any part of India
options.b: Right to acquire, hold and dispose of property

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to acquire, hold and dispose of property**. This right was originally a Fundamental Right under Article 19(1)(f), but the **44th Amendment Act of 1978** officially abolished it, demoting it to a mere constitutional/legal right under **Article 300A**.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Human Resource Development
statementLines: The concept of demographic transition provides a model for
questions.questionText: The concept of demographic transition provides a model for

options.a: Analyzing government responses to changing pattern of population growth
options.b: Explaining major patterns of global population movement throughout the history
options.c: Analyzing spatial variations in the distribution, growth and movement of population over time
options.d: Examining the relationship between economic development and natural population change

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **examining the relationship between economic development and natural population change**. The **Demographic Transition Model** is a demographic phenomenon that illustrates how a population shifts from high birth and death rates to low birth and death rates as a country transitions from a pre-industrial to an industrialized **economic system**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following is/are not fundamental right(s) under the Constitution of India ?
1. Right to education.
2. Right to work.
3. Right to form associations.
4. Right to practise any profession.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not fundamental right(s) under the Constitution of India ? 1. Right to education. 2. Right to work. 3. Right to form associations. 4. Right to practise any profession. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Right to Work** is primarily a **Directive Principle of State Policy (Article 41)** and is not classified as a justiciable Fundamental Right. The Right to Education (Article 21A), forming associations (Article 19), and practicing professions (Article 19) are all recognized Fundamental Rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: The Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India contains
questions.questionText: The Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India contains

options.a: Provisions regarding the administration of tribal areas
options.b: The Union List, the State List and the Concurrent List
options.d: Provisions about the disqualification of Members of Legislatures on grounds of defection

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the Union List, the State List and the Concurrent List**. The **Seventh Schedule**, associated with Article 246, precisely details the constitutional division of legislative powers between the Union and the States by categorizing subjects into these **three comprehensive lists**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Article 368 of the Constitution of India deals with
questions.questionText: Article 368 of the Constitution of India deals with

options.a: The powers of the Parliament of India to amend the Constitution
options.c: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha
options.d: Official Language of the Union of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the powers of the Parliament of India to amend the Constitution**. **Article 368**, the sole article in Part XX, clearly establishes the **power of the Parliament** to amend the Constitution and outlines the detailed procedures required to enact such amendments.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Which among the following is not a basic feature of the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: Which among the following is not a basic feature of the Constitution of India ?

options.d: The unquestioned right of the Parliament to amend any part of the Constitution

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The unquestioned right of the Parliament to amend any part of the Constitution**. The landmark Kesavananda Bharati judgment established that Parliament does **not have an unquestioned or absolute right** to amend the Constitution; its amending powers are strictly limited by the doctrine of the **"Basic Structure"**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one among the following is not a fundamental duty?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not a fundamental duty?

options.a: To abide by the Constitution of India and respect the national flag and national anthem
options.c: To protect the Sovereignty, Unity and Integrity of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To render compulsory military service**. While Article 51A(d) instructs citizens "to defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so," the Indian Constitution does **not mandate compulsory military service (conscription)** as an absolute Fundamental Duty.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following are the basic features of the Constitution (73rd Amendment) Act?
1. Provides for a three-tier structure of panchayats in the village, intermediary and district levels.
2. Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes and women in all the tiers of panchayats.
3. Election to panchayats under the supervision of the State Election Commissions.
4. Introduction of the 11th Schedule to the Constitution.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the basic features of the Constitution (73rd Amendment) Act? 1. Provides for a three-tier structure of panchayats in the village, intermediary and district levels. 2. Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes and women in all the tiers of panchayats. 3. Election to panchayats under the supervision of the State Election Commissions. 4. Introduction of the 11th Schedule to the Constitution. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. The monumental **73rd Amendment Act** introduced a standardized **three-tier Panchayati Raj system**, mandated **reservations for SC/ST and women** (33%), established independent **State Election Commissions** to conduct polls, and added the **11th Schedule** detailing 29 functional items for Panchayats.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements are true for the Fundamental Right to Life and Personal Liberty as guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution of India?
1. The Right is available to citizens as well as aliens.
2. It covers protection against arbitrary executive and legislative action.
3. It includes the right to live with human dignity.
4. It can be taken away according to the procedure established by law.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are true for the Fundamental Right to Life and Personal Liberty as guaranteed under Article 21 of the Constitution of India? 1. The Right is available to citizens as well as aliens. 2. It covers protection against arbitrary executive and legislative action. 3. It includes the right to live with human dignity. 4. It can be taken away according to the procedure established by law. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. **Article 21** applies to **all persons** (citizens and aliens). It provides protection against **both executive action and arbitrary legislative action** (post-Maneka Gandhi reading). The Supreme Court has vastly expanded it to include the **right to live with human dignity**. Lastly, it expressly states that liberty can be deprived according to **"procedure established by law."**

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Constitution of India guarantees the Fundamental Right to Freedom of Religion to all its citizens. Which among the following is not true for this Right?
questions.questionText: The Constitution of India guarantees the Fundamental Right to Freedom of Religion to all its citizens. Which among the following is not true for this Right?

options.a: It gives freedom of conscience and freedom to profess, practice and propagate any religion
options.b: It gives freedom to establish and maintain institution for religious and charitable purposes
options.c: The Right is subject to public order, morality, and health
options.d: The State cannot make any law which abrogates this Right for citizens

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The State cannot make any law which abrogates this Right for citizens**. This is incorrect. The Fundamental Right to Freedom of Religion is **not absolute**. Under Articles 25(2) and 26, the State **can make laws** regulating secular, economic, or political activities associated with religious practices, and can intervene for **social welfare and reform**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 may be seen as giving effect to which of the following Fundamental Rights?
1. Equality before the Law
2. Right against Discrimination
3. Abolition of Untouchability
4. Right to Freedom of Religion
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 may be seen as giving effect to which of the following Fundamental Rights? 1. Equality before the Law 2. Right against Discrimination 3. Abolition of Untouchability 4. Right to Freedom of Religion Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. The **SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act** is a powerful legislative mechanism designed directly to enforce **Article 17** (Abolition of Untouchability) and uphold the principles of **Article 14** (Equality before Law) and **Article 15** (Protection against discrimination). It has no direct legal linkage to the Freedom of Religion (Article 25).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part II: Citizenship
statementLines: Under which of the following Acts is the National Population Register being created?
questions.questionText: Under which of the following Acts is the National Population Register being created?

options.a: The Citizenship Act of India, 1955 as amended in 2004

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Citizenship Act of India, 1955 as amended in 2004**. The statutory framework authorizing the creation of the **National Population Register (NPR)** is provided under Section 14A of the **Citizenship Act, 1955**, which was significantly amended in 2004 to mandate the compulsory registration of every citizen.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following rights under the Constitution of India is/are Fundamental Right(s) ?
1. Right to work
2. Right to privacy
3. Right to free and compulsory education in the age bracket of six to eighteen years Select the answer using the code given below
questions.questionText: Which of the following rights under the Constitution of India is/are Fundamental Right(s) ? 1. Right to work 2. Right to privacy 3. Right to free and compulsory education in the age bracket of six to eighteen years Select the answer using the code given below
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The Supreme Court (Puttaswamy case) officially declared the **Right to Privacy** a Fundamental Right under Article 21. The "Right to work" is a **Directive Principle** (Article 41). The right to education (Article 21A) is guaranteed for children aged **6 to 14 years**, not 6 to 18 years, making statement 3 factually incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which one of the following committees is NOT associated with decentralization of Panchayati Raj institutions in India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following committees is NOT associated with decentralization of Panchayati Raj institutions in India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Abhijit Sen Committee**. The Balwant Rai Mehta, Ashok Mehta, and L.M. Singhvi Committees are foundational historical pillars that recommended and shaped the **Panchayati Raj** decentralization in India. The **Abhijit Sen Committee** was primarily associated with long-term food policy and agricultural economics, not Panchayati Raj.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements :

1. The duties of the Attorney General of India are to give advice to the Government of India upon such legal matters, and to perform such other duties of a legal character, as may from time to time be referred to or assigned to him by the President of India
2. The Solicitor General of India is the secondary Law Officer of the country, who assists the Attorney General, and is himself assisted by several Additional Solicitor Generals of India
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements : 1. The duties of the Attorney General of India are to give advice to the Government of India upon such legal matters, and to perform such other duties of a legal character, as may from time to time be referred to or assigned to him by the President of India 2. The Solicitor General of India is the secondary Law Officer of the country, who assists the Attorney General, and is himself assisted by several Additional Solicitor Generals of India Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Under Article 76, the **Attorney General** is the primary legal advisor to the GoI, performing duties assigned by the President. Below the AG, the **Solicitor General of India** serves as the statutory secondary law officer, specifically appointed to assist the Attorney General, and is in turn supported by Additional Solicitor Generals.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following Committees does NOT consist of Members from the Upper House of the Parliament?
1. Public Accounts Committee
2. Estimates Committee
3. Committee on Public Undertakings Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following Committees does NOT consist of Members from the Upper House of the Parliament? 1. Public Accounts Committee 2. Estimates Committee 3. Committee on Public Undertakings Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. Both the **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** and the **Committee on Public Undertakings (CoPU)** consist of 22 members (15 from the Lok Sabha and 7 from the Rajya Sabha). However, the **Estimates Committee** consists of 30 members, all of whom are elected **exclusively from the Lok Sabha**, leaving the Upper House completely unrepresented.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: In the passing of a Money Bill, the Rajya Sabha has limited powers in that it shall return the Bill, with or without any recommendation, within the stipulated time of
questions.questionText: In the passing of a Money Bill, the Rajya Sabha has limited powers in that it shall return the Bill, with or without any recommendation, within the stipulated time of
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **14 days**. According to **Article 109** of the Constitution, once a Money Bill is passed by the Lok Sabha, the Rajya Sabha must return it, with or without recommendations, within a strict maximum period of **14 days**. If it fails to do so, the bill is deemed passed by both Houses.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. Freedoms under this Article are not absolute
2. Reasonable restrictions can be imposed on the exercise of freedoms under this Article Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India is/are correct? 1. Freedoms under this Article are not absolute 2. Reasonable restrictions can be imposed on the exercise of freedoms under this Article Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The six fundamental freedoms guaranteed under **Article 19(1)** (speech, assembly, movement, etc.) are explicitly **not absolute**. Under clauses (2) to (6) of Article 19, the State is constitutionally empowered to impose **reasonable restrictions** on these freedoms on grounds like sovereignty, public order, and morality.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Consider the following statements about Attorney-General in India :
1. She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India
2. She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote
3. She/he is appointed by the President of India
4. She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law
How many of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about Attorney-General in India : 1. She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India 2. She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote 3. She/he is appointed by the President of India 4. She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law How many of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **3**. Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct: The AG is the **highest law officer**, possesses the right to speak in Parliament **without voting** (Article 88), and is **appointed by the President** (Article 76).
Statement 4 is incorrect; the Attorney General is **not a whole-time government counsel** and is legally permitted to take up private legal practice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part I: The Union and its territory
questions.microTopic: Union, States and their Territory, UTs
statementLines: The Constitution (35th Amendment) Act of 1974 is related to
which one of the following States ?
questions.questionText: The Constitution (35th Amendment) Act of 1974 is related to which one of the following States ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Sikkim**. The **35th Constitutional Amendment Act of 1974** formally terminated the protectorate status of **Sikkim** and uniquely conferred upon it the status of an "Associate State" of the Indian Union (which was later upgraded to full statehood via the 36th Amendment).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which one of the following best describes the electoral system of India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following best describes the electoral system of India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **First-Past-the-Post System**. For direct elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies in India, the country primarily employs the **First-Past-the-Post (FPTP)** or simple majority system, where the candidate who secures the highest number of votes in a constituency is declared the outright winner.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following is a non-justiciable right ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a non-justiciable right ?

options.d: Right to life and personal liberty

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to adequate livelihood**. The "right to an adequate means of livelihood" is enshrined in **Article 39(a)**, which is a **Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP)**. As per Article 37, Directive Principles are fundamentally **non-justiciable** and cannot be legally enforced by any court. Options b, c, and d are enforceable Fundamental Rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a writ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a writ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Severability**. Mandamus, Habeas Corpus, and Certiorari are three of the five powerful prerogative **writs** issued by the Supreme Court and High Courts under Articles 32 and 226. **Severability** is a judicial doctrine (Doctrine of Severability) used to strike down only the unconstitutional parts of a law, not a writ itself.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a part of Fundamental Rights ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a part of Fundamental Rights ?

options.b: Right to establish educational institutions by minorities
options.c: Right to be conferred with titles

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to be conferred with titles**. Far from being a right, **Article 18** explicitly guarantees the **Abolition of Titles**, aggressively prohibiting the State from conferring any titles (except military or academic distinctions) to ensure democratic equality.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a function of the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a function of the Constitution of India ?

options.a: To ensure participation of good people in politics
options.b: To guarantee a set of rights to citizens
options.c: To define the power of the different organs of government
options.d: To create conditions for a just society

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To ensure participation of good people in politics**. The Constitution is a legal and structural document. It establishes government organs, defines powers, guarantees fundamental rights, and sets out ideals for a just society. However, it cannot inherently **ensure or mandate the moral character ("good people")** of those who choose to participate in politics.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a feature of the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a feature of the Constitution of India ?

options.a: It provides a set of basic rules.
options.b: It specifies the power of the Government.
options.c: It is the supreme law of the land.
options.d: It specifies the supremacy of the judiciary.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It specifies the supremacy of the judiciary.** The Indian Constitution is characterized by the **supremacy of the Constitution** itself, establishing a fine balance between Parliamentary sovereignty and Judicial review. It **does not establish absolute judicial supremacy**, unlike the American system.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney General of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney General of India is not correct?

options.a: He has the right of audience only in the Supreme Court of India.
options.b: He shall receive such remuneration as the President may determine.
options.c: He shall be qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
options.d: He shall give advice to the Government of India on all legal matters.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **He has the right of audience only in the Supreme Court of India.** This statement is constitutionally false. Under **Article 76(3)**, in the performance of his official duties, the Attorney General possesses the right of audience in **all courts within the territory of India**, not exclusively the Supreme Court.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Who among the following described the Directive Principles of State Policy as the novel feature of the Constitution of India ?
questions.questionText: Who among the following described the Directive Principles of State Policy as the novel feature of the Constitution of India ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **B.R. Ambedkar**. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar famously described the **Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP)** as a **"novel feature"** of the Indian Constitution, emphasizing their crucial role in providing instructions to future governments to establish economic and social democracy.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Indian Councils Act of 1861 is/are correct?
1. It enlarged the Governor General's Council for the purpose of making laws.
2. The Governor General was not authorised to increase the number of members.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Indian Councils Act of 1861 is/are correct? 1. It enlarged the Governor General's Council for the purpose of making laws. 2. The Governor General was not authorised to increase the number of members. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. The **Indian Councils Act of 1861** significantly enlarged the Viceroy's Executive Council by adding "non-official" members for the purpose of making laws.
Statement 2 is incorrect because the **Governor-General was explicitly authorized to nominate between 6 to 12 additional members** to the council.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Article 46 of the Constitution of India refers to the promotion of educational and economic interests of
questions.questionText: Article 46 of the Constitution of India refers to the promotion of educational and economic interests of

options.b: Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other weaker sections.
options.c: Displaced persons from large irrigation projects.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other weaker sections**. **Article 46**, a vital Directive Principle of State Policy, commands the State to promote with special care the educational and economic interests of the **weaker sections of the people, and, in particular, of the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes**, protecting them from social injustice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part VIII & X: Union Territories and Scheduled Areas
statementLines: The provisions of the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India shall not apply to the administration and control of the Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in
1. Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand and Odisha
2. Assam and Tripura
3. Meghalaya and Mizoram
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: The provisions of the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India shall not apply to the administration and control of the Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in 1. Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand and Odisha 2. Assam and Tripura 3. Meghalaya and Mizoram Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The **Fifth Schedule** explicitly deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas in all states *except* the four North-Eastern states covered exclusively by the **Sixth Schedule**. Therefore, the Fifth Schedule **does not apply to Assam, Tripura, Meghalaya, and Mizoram** (AMTM).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one among the following is not a Fundamental Right under the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is not a Fundamental Right under the Constitution of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to citizenship**. The Constitution of India comprehensively regulates citizenship under Part II (Articles 5-11), but obtaining or holding **Citizenship itself is not enumerated as a justiciable Fundamental Right** under Part III of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Central Vigilance Commission was established on the recommendation of
which one of the following Committees?
questions.questionText: The Central Vigilance Commission was established on the recommendation of which one of the following Committees?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Santhanam Committee**. The Government of India established the **Central Vigilance Commission (CVC)** in 1964 as the apex integrity institution directly based on the robust recommendations of the **Committee on Prevention of Corruption, headed by K. Santhanam**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Every Judge of the Supreme Court of India is appointed by
questions.questionText: Every Judge of the Supreme Court of India is appointed by
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **the President of India**. According to the explicit wording of **Article 124(2)** of the Constitution, every Judge of the Supreme Court of India is formally **appointed by the President** by warrant under his hand and seal (even though the President acts on the binding advice of the Collegium system).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in the Constitution of India?

options.a: Equal justice and free legal aid
options.b: Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Protection of personal law**. While the DPSP strongly advocates for a **Uniform Civil Code (Article 44)** which would theoretically standardize personal laws, the explicit constitutional **"protection of personal law" is nowhere listed** as a Directive Principle of State Policy in Part IV.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: The word 'socialist' was inserted into the Preamble to the Constitution of India through
which one of the following Amendment Acts?
questions.questionText: The word 'socialist' was inserted into the Preamble to the Constitution of India through which one of the following Amendment Acts?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **42nd Amendment Act**. The sweeping **42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976**, enacted during the Internal Emergency, formally inserted three new defining words into the Preamble: **'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity'**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following is the ex officio Chairman of the North Eastern Council?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is the ex officio Chairman of the North Eastern Council?

options.d: The Union Minister of State (Independent Charg , Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Union Home Minister**. The **North Eastern Council (NEC)** is the nodal agency for the economic and social development of the North Eastern Region. Following parliamentary amendments, the **Union Home Minister** now serves as its designated **ex officio Chairman**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Article 352 of the Constitution of India contains provisions related to
questions.questionText: Article 352 of the Constitution of India contains provisions related to

options.b: Failure of constitutional machinery in States
options.c: Suspension of the enforcement of fundamental rights

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **general emergency**. **Article 352** empowers the President to issue a Proclamation of Emergency when the security of India is threatened by war, external aggression, or armed rebellion. This is commonly referred to in broad constitutional terminology as a **National Emergency or General Emergency**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Who among the following can attend the meetings of both Houses of Parliament while not being a member of either House ?
questions.questionText: Who among the following can attend the meetings of both Houses of Parliament while not being a member of either House ?

options.c: The Comptroller and Auditor General of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Attorney General of India**. Under the specific privileges granted by **Article 88**, the **Attorney General of India** holds the unique constitutional right to speak in, and take part in the proceedings of, **both Houses of Parliament** (and any joint sittings), despite not being an elected member.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The creation of the institution of Lokpal was first recommended by
questions.questionText: The creation of the institution of Lokpal was first recommended by
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Administrative Reforms Commission**. The **First Administrative Reforms Commission (**1966-19**70)**, headed initially by Morarji Desai, extensively analyzed citizen grievances and became the first official body to recommend the creation of the independent ombudsman institutions of the **Lokpal and Lokayukta** in India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements about the Constitution of India :
1. A Member of Parliament enjoys freedom of speech in the Parliament as a Parliamentary Privilege protected by the Constitution of India.
2. The Constitution has vested the power to amend the Constitution in the Parliament.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about the Constitution of India : 1. A Member of Parliament enjoys freedom of speech in the Parliament as a Parliamentary Privilege protected by the Constitution of India. 2. The Constitution has vested the power to amend the Constitution in the Parliament. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Under **Article 105(1)**, freedom of speech in Parliament is a guaranteed Parliamentary Privilege. Concurrently, **Article 368** explicitly vests the constituent power to formally **amend the Constitution entirely in the Parliament**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Union Executive in India is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Union Executive in India is correct?

options.a: According to the Constitution of India, the total number of members of the Council of Ministers cannot exceed 20% of the total number of Members of the House of the People
options.b: The rank of the different Ministers is determined by the President
options.c: The Ministers are appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister
options.d: Only a member of either House of Parliament can be appointed as a Minister

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Ministers are appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister**. This is explicitly mandated by **Article 75(1)** of the Constitution. Statement (a) is incorrect (the cap is **15%**, not 20%). Statement (b) is incorrect (the PM determines ranks/portfolios). Statement (d) is incorrect (a non-MP can be appointed as a Minister for up to **six months**).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is correct?

options.a: A Bill shall be deemed to be a Money Bill only if it provides for imposition of fines or penalties
options.b: A Money Bill shall be introduced in the Rajya Sabha
options.c: The Rajya Sabha can reject the Money Bill
options.d: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha finally decides if it is a Money Bill, should any dispute about it arise

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Speaker of the Lok Sabha finally decides if it is a Money Bill, should any dispute about it arise**. Under the absolute powers granted by **Article 110(3)**, if any question arises whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not, the formal decision and certification of the **Speaker of the Lok Sabha** is final and binding.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which one of the following powers of the Prime Minister of India is codified in the Constitution of India itself?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following powers of the Prime Minister of India is codified in the Constitution of India itself?

options.a: The power of advising the President as regards the appointment of other Ministers
options.b: The power to allocate business amongst the Ministers
options.c: The power to summon the meeting of the Cabinet
options.d: The power to transfer a Minister from one Department to another

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The power of advising the President as regards the appointment of other Ministers**. This power is explicitly codified and written directly into the text of the Constitution under **Article 75(1)**, which states that "the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the **advice of the Prime Minister**." The other powers stem from convention or subordinate business rules.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: The word 'Secular' was inserted into the Constitution of India by:
questions.questionText: The word 'Secular' was inserted into the Constitution of India by:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **42nd Amendment Act**. The monumental **42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976**, widely regarded as a mini-constitution enacted during the Emergency, explicitly inserted the terms 'Socialist' and **'Secular'** into the Preamble of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The two provisions of the Constitution of India that most clearly express the power of judicial review are:
questions.questionText: The two provisions of the Constitution of India that most clearly express the power of judicial review are:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 32 and Article 226**. The bedrock of **Judicial Review** in India stems from the writ jurisdictions of constitutional courts. **Article 32** allows citizens to move the Supreme Court, and **Article 226** empowers the High Courts to aggressively review and strike down unconstitutional executive or legislative actions violating Fundamental Rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: An emergency under Article 352 of the Constitution of India can be declared only during:
questions.questionText: An emergency under Article 352 of the Constitution of India can be declared only during:

options.a: War, external aggression or internal disturbance.
options.b: War, external aggression or armed rebellion.
options.c: Failure of Constitutional Machinery in the State.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **War, external aggression or armed rebellion.** Following the critical reforms of the **44th Amendment Act (1978)**, a National Emergency under **Article 352** can be constitutionally proclaimed by the President strictly on only three grounds: **war, external aggression, or armed rebellion**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: The Fourth Schedule to the Constitution of India deals with:
questions.questionText: The Fourth Schedule to the Constitution of India deals with:

options.a: Provisions related to the administration of tribal areas.
options.b: Allocation of seats in the Council of States.
options.c: The Union List, the State List and the Concurrent List.
options.d: Recognized languages of the Union of India.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **allocation of seats in the Council of States.** The **Fourth Schedule** of the Indian Constitution explicitly lists the allocation of seats for each State and Union Territory in the **Council of States (Rajya Sabha)**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Who, among the following Presidents of India, sent back the Post Office (Amendment) Bill, 1986 for the reconsideration of the Parliament?
questions.questionText: Who, among the following Presidents of India, sent back the Post Office (Amendment) Bill, 1986 for the reconsideration of the Parliament?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question is historically and technically flawed, leading to its cancellation in official keys. President **Giani Zail Singh** exercised a "pocket veto" on the Post Office (Amendment) Bill, 1986 by keeping it pending indefinitely. It was actually his successor, President **R. Venkataraman**, who formally returned the bill to the government for reconsideration in 1990.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Under which one among the following Articles of the Constitution of India, a member of a Civil Service, whether of the Union or of a State, seeks protection from unlawful dismissal from service ?
questions.questionText: Under which one among the following Articles of the Constitution of India, a member of a Civil Service, whether of the Union or of a State, seeks protection from unlawful dismissal from service ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 311**. **Article 311** acts as a crucial constitutional safeguard for civil servants, legally ensuring that no person employed in a civil capacity under the Union or a State can be dismissed, removed, or reduced in rank by an **authority subordinate to the one that appointed them**, nor without a proper departmental **inquiry**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against the State only
2. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable in favor of citizens only
3. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against both, the state and individuals
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against the State only 2. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable in favor of citizens only 3. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against both, the state and individuals Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. Fundamental Rights under Part III are multi-dimensional: some protect strictly against the **State** (e.g., Article 14), some are exclusively for **citizens only** (Articles 15, 16, 19, 29, 30), and some are uniquely enforceable against **both the State and private individuals** (e.g., Article 17 against untouchability and Article 23 against human trafficking).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Under which one among the following Articles of the Constitution of India, the seats are reserved for the Scheduled Caste and the Scheduled Tribe in every Municipality?
questions.questionText: Under which one among the following Articles of the Constitution of India, the seats are reserved for the Scheduled Caste and the Scheduled Tribe in every Municipality?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **243-T**. Inserted by the 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, **Article 243-T** explicitly mandates that **seats must be reserved for the Scheduled Castes and the Scheduled Tribes** in every Municipality in direct proportion to their population within the municipal area.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The abolition of the Official Secrets Act, 1923 was recommended by the Second Administrative Reforms Commission
2. The Right to Information Act was enacted in 2005 Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. The abolition of the Official Secrets Act, 1923 was recommended by the Second Administrative Reforms Commission 2. The Right to Information Act was enacted in 2005 Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. To usher in an era of transparency, the **Second Administrative Reforms Commission (ARC)** prominently recommended repealing the archaic and colonial **Official Secrets Act, 1923**. The landmark **Right to Information (RTI) Act** was successfully enacted by Parliament in **2005**.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.sectionGroup: Fiscal Policy, Budget, Taxation
statementLines: Consider the following statements :
1. The 15th Finance Commission used demographic variables as a criterion for horizontal devolution, unlike the 14th Finance Commission
2. Both the 14th and 15th Finance Commissions considered 'forest cover' as a criterion for horizontal devolution
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements : 1. The 15th Finance Commission used demographic variables as a criterion for horizontal devolution, unlike the 14th Finance Commission 2. Both the 14th and 15th Finance Commissions considered 'forest cover' as a criterion for horizontal devolution Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. *(

Note: The official key indicated (c), though technical interpretations vary).* The **15th Finance Commission** shifted entirely to 2011 demographic data, penalizing states that controlled population less effectively than under the 14th FC. Both commissions utilized ecological indicators, with the 14th formally using "Forest Cover" (7.5% weight) and the 15th tweaking it slightly to "Forest and Ecology" (10% weight).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XVIII: Emergency Provisions
statementLines: Which of the following statements about National Emergency is/are correct?
1. A Proclamation of Emergency may be made by the President only when the security of India or any part thereof is threatened by war or external aggression or armed rebellion.
2. The Government of India acquires power to give directions to a State on any matter.
3. The Proclamation of Emergency does not suspend the State legislature.
4. The Proclamation of Emergency can continue for a maximum period of six months at a time only if approved by resolutions of both the Houses of Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about National Emergency is/are correct? 1. A Proclamation of Emergency may be made by the President only when the security of India or any part thereof is threatened by war or external aggression or armed rebellion. 2. The Government of India acquires power to give directions to a State on any matter. 3. The Proclamation of Emergency does not suspend the State legislature. 4. The Proclamation of Emergency can continue for a maximum period of six months at a time only if approved by resolutions of both the Houses of Parliament. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1, 2 and 3 only**. A National Emergency (Article 352) is declared strictly for **war, external aggression, or armed rebellion**. During it, the Union's executive power extends to giving directions to states on **any matter**. However, the **State legislatures are NOT suspended** (they operate concurrently with Parliament).
Statement 4 is incorrect because while approval is needed every six months, there is **no maximum period limit**; an emergency can be extended indefinitely.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the impeachment of a Judge of the Supreme Court of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the impeachment of a Judge of the Supreme Court of India is not correct?

options.a: A motion addressed to the President, signed by at least 100 members of both the Houses of the Parliament is delivered to the Speaker.
options.b: The motion is investigated by a Committee of three (2 Judges of the Supreme Court and a distinguished Jurist).
options.c: If the Committee finds the Judge guilty of misbehaviour or that he suffers from incapacity, the motion together with the report of the Committee is taken up for consideration in the House where the motion is pending.
options.d: The Judge will be removed after the President gives his order for removal.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A motion addressed to the President, signed by at least 100 members of both the Houses of the Parliament is delivered to the Speaker.** This procedure is incorrect. Under the Judges (Inquiry) Act, 1968, a removal motion requires the signatures of either **100 members of the Lok Sabha** (given to the Speaker) OR **50 members of the Rajya Sabha** (given to the Chairman). It does not require 100 members from *both* Houses combined.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which among the following deals with Habeas Corpus ?
questions.questionText: Which among the following deals with Habeas Corpus ?

options.a: State of Uttar Pradesh v. Raj Narain and Ors. (1975)
options.b: Additional District Magistrate, Jabalpur v. Shivakant Shukla (1976)
options.c: Mrs. Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India (1978)

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Additional District Magistrate, Jabalpur v. Shivakant Shukla (1976)**. This infamous judgment is universally known as the **Habeas Corpus case**. During the 1975 Emergency, the Supreme Court controversially ruled that a person's right to approach the court for a writ of Habeas Corpus to challenge unlawful detention was entirely suspended.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which among the following statements regarding the powers of the High Courts under Article 226 of the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which among the following statements regarding the powers of the High Courts under Article 226 of the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: It can issue writs of habeas corpus, mandamus, quo warranto, certiorari, and prohibition.
options.b: Writs can be issued to enforce any rights conferred by Part-III and for any other purpose.
options.c: This power can derogate the power conferred on the Supreme Court under Article 32(2).
options.d: Writ can be issued to any authority under its jurisdiction.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **This power can derogate the power conferred on the Supreme Court under Article 32(2).** This is constitutionally false. **Article 226(4)** explicitly states that the power conferred on a High Court to issue writs **shall not be in derogation** of the equivalent power conferred on the Supreme Court by clause (2) of Article 32.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which among the following is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which among the following is not correct?

options.a: Legislation on criminal law, barring exceptions, is a subject under the Concurrent List.
options.b: Bankruptcy and Insolvency are subjects under the Concurrent List.
options.c: Inter-State trade and commerce is a subject under the State List.
options.d: Banking is exclusively under the Union List.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Inter-State trade and commerce is a subject under the State List.** This statement is factually incorrect. To ensure uniform national economic policies, **Inter-State trade and commerce** falls squarely under the **Union List (Entry 42)**. Only intra-state trade generally falls under the State List.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Right to Information Act, 2005 is/are correct?
1. There shall be not more than twelve (12) Information Commissioners.
2. The Chief Information Commissioner shall hold office for a term of five years.
3. The Chief Information Commissioner is eligible for reappointment.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Right to Information Act, 2005 is/are correct? 1. There shall be not more than twelve (12) Information Commissioners. 2. The Chief Information Commissioner shall hold office for a term of five years. 3. The Chief Information Commissioner is eligible for reappointment. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: This question was officially **Cancelled**. Under the RTI Act, there shall be **not more than ten (10)** Information Commissioners (making statement 1 wrong). Following the RTI Amendment Act 2019, the term of the CIC is no longer a fixed five years but is **prescribed by the Central Government** (currently 3 years, making statement 2 outdated). The CIC is **not eligible for reappointment** (making statement 3 wrong). Thus, all statements are incorrect.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Who among the following was the first pro-tem Speaker to be unanimously elected as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha ?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was the first pro-tem Speaker to be unanimously elected as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Somnath Chatterjee**. In a unique parliamentary transition during the 14th Lok Sabha (2004), veteran parliamentarian **Somnath Chatterjee** was initially appointed as the **Pro-tem Speaker** to administer oaths to newly elected members and was subsequently **unanimously elected as the regular Speaker** of the Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The landmark case of D.
C. Wadhwa vs. State of Bihar in the Supreme Court is related to
which one of the following powers of the Governor?
questions.questionText: The landmark case of D. C. Wadhwa vs. State of Bihar in the Supreme Court is related to which one of the following powers of the Governor?

options.d: To revise the emoluments and allowances of the MLAs

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To repromulgate ordinances**. In the landmark **D. C. Wadhwa vs. State of Bihar (1987)** judgment, the Supreme Court severely criticized and struck down the rampant executive practice of successively **re-promulgating ordinances** without ever presenting them to the state legislature for debate, labeling it a subversion of the democratic process and a "fraud on the Constitution."

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
statementLines: Which of the following is/are source/ sources of Law in India?
1. Constitution of India
2. Statutes
3. Customary Law
4. Case Law
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are source/ sources of Law in India? 1. Constitution of India 2. Statutes 3. Customary Law 4. Case Law Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2, 3 and 4**. The Indian legal framework is highly comprehensive, drawing its enforceable authority from the **Constitution** (the supreme law of the land), **Statutes** (laws enacted by Parliament or State legislatures), **Case Law** (judicial precedents established by higher constitutional courts), and legally recognized local **Customary Law** (especially in personal, family, and tribal matters).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Prime Minister, Chief Minister, Council of Ministers, Cabinet Executive
statementLines: Which among the following is/are the function/functions of the Cabinet Secretariat?
1. To ensure interministerial coordination
2. Management of major crisis situations in the country
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which among the following is/are the function/functions of the Cabinet Secretariat? 1. To ensure interministerial coordination 2. Management of major crisis situations in the country Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. The **Cabinet Secretariat** functions directly under the Prime Minister of India. Its core administrative functions include facilitating smooth **inter-ministerial coordination** to resolve policy differences and taking charge of coordinating the **management of major crisis situations** in the country on behalf of the highest executive levels.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements about the Committee on Public Undertakings is/are correct?
1. There are more members from the Rajya Sabha than the Lok Sabha in the Committee.
2. The Chairperson of the Committee is appointed by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about the Committee on Public Undertakings is/are correct? 1. There are more members from the Rajya Sabha than the Lok Sabha in the Committee. 2. The Chairperson of the Committee is appointed by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Chairperson of the Committee on Public Undertakings** is exclusively appointed by the **Speaker of the Lok Sabha** from amongst its Lok Sabha members. Statement 1 is factually incorrect because the committee comprises 22 members in total: **15 from the Lok Sabha** and only **7 from the Rajya Sabha**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following is correct in respect of the appointment of District Judges?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct in respect of the appointment of District Judges?

options.a: They are appointed by the Chief Justice of the concerned High Court.
options.b: They are appointed by the President of India on the advice of the Chief Justice of the concerned High Court.
options.c: They are appointed by the Governor in consultation with the High Court of the concerned State.
options.d: They are appointed by the Supreme Court on the advice of the concerned High Court.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **They are appointed by the Governor in consultation with the High Court of the concerned State.** According to the clear provisions of **Article 233**, appointments of persons to be, and the posting and promotion of, **District Judges** in any State shall be formally made by the **Governor of the State** in explicit consultation with the **High Court** exercising jurisdiction in relation to that State.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Swaran Singh Committee recommended the insertion of
which one of the following provisions into the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: The Swaran Singh Committee recommended the insertion of which one of the following provisions into the Constitution of India?

options.c: Nomination of Anglo-Indian Members to the Lok Sabha
options.d: Impeachment of the President of India

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Fundamental Duties**. Constituted in 1976 during the Internal Emergency, the **Sardar Swaran Singh Committee** specifically recommended the inclusion of a separate chapter delineating citizens' obligations. This led directly to the insertion of Part IVA and **Article 51A (Fundamental Duties)** via the 42nd Amendment Act.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following powers of the Supreme Court is also conferred on a High Court?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following powers of the Supreme Court is also conferred on a High Court?

options.b: Power of doing complete justice in any cause or matter pending before the court
options.d: Power to allow/refuse Special Leave Petitions

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Power of court of record**. Just as the Supreme Court operates as a **Court of Record (Article 129)**, every High Court is also constitutionally established as a **Court of Record (Article 215)**, thereby possessing the inherent power to punish for its contempt. Advisory power (Art 143), complete justice (Art 142), and SLP (Art 136) are entirely exclusive to the Supreme Court.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: The Constitution of India does not guarantee
which one of the following as a fundamental right?
questions.questionText: The Constitution of India does not guarantee which one of the following as a fundamental right?

options.d: Right to carry on trade and business

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Right to property**. Originally guaranteed as a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(f) and Article 31, the **Right to Property** was decisively removed from the list of Fundamental Rights by the Janata Party government through the **44th Amendment Act, 1978**. It now exists merely as a constitutional right under **Article 300A**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Supreme Court is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Supreme Court is not correct?

options.a: Under Article 129 and Article 144 of the Constitution of India, the Supreme Court has been vested with power to punish for contempt of court.
options.b: The Supreme Court has been vested with advisory jurisdiction.
options.c: The Supreme Court may refuse to provide opinion to the President, if so asked, in certain circumstances.
options.d: Law declared by the Supreme Court is binding on all courts within the territory of India.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Under Article 129 and Article 144 of the Constitution of India, the Supreme Court has been vested with power to punish for contempt of court.** While **Article 129** indeed designates the SC as a Court of Record with powers to punish for contempt, **Article 144** actually states that "all authorities, civil and judicial, in the territory of India shall act in aid of the Supreme Court," and has nothing to do with granting contempt powers.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about Public Accounts Committee is correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about Public Accounts Committee is correct?

options.a: The Committee consists of not more than 15 members from the Lok Sabha and 10 members from the Rajya Sabha.
options.b: The Finance Minister is an ex officio member of the Committee.
options.c: A member of the Committee is given a term for one year.
options.d: The term of the office of the members of the Committee from the Rajya Sabha is extended even when they retire from the Rajya Sabha.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A member of the Committee is given a term for one year.** The **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** is reconstituted annually based on proportional representation, so the absolute tenure of its members is exactly **one year**. It consists of 22 members (15 from Lok Sabha, 7 from Rajya Sabha), and a sitting Minister is strictly barred from being elected to it.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Who can be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court?
questions.questionText: Who can be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court?

options.a: An advocate who has practised for at least ten years in any court
options.b: A Judge of a High Court for at least three years
options.c: A distinguished jurist in the opinion of the President of India
options.d: A person who has held a judicial office for at least fifteen years

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A distinguished jurist in the opinion of the President of India**. Under **Article 124(3)**, a person is constitutionally qualified to be appointed as a Supreme Court Judge if they are a citizen of India and have been a High Court judge for 5 years, an advocate of a High Court for 10 years, or are, in the subjective opinion of the **President, a distinguished jurist**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Who among the following fixes the date of election of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
questions.questionText: Who among the following fixes the date of election of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?

options.c: The Secretary General of the Lok Sabha
options.d: The Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The President of India**. According to the established Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the **President of India fixes the official date** for holding the election to the office of the **Speaker**. (Subsequently, the newly elected Speaker fixes the date for the election of the Deputy Speaker).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about 'personal liberty' is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about 'personal liberty' is not correct?

options.a: State does not have the authority to deprive any person within the territory of India of his/her personal liberty without any rational basis.
options.b: Basis of depriving a person of his/her personal liberty must be in accordance with procedures established by law.
options.c: Personal liberty can be secured by the judicial writ of Habeas Corpus.
options.d: The majority view of the Supreme Court in A. K. Gopalan vs. State of Madras case invented 'due process of law'.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The majority view of the Supreme Court in A. K. Gopalan vs. State of Madras case invented 'due process of law'.** This is historically false. In the **A.K. Gopalan case (1950)**, the Supreme Court took a highly restrictive view and expressly **rejected the broader American concept of "due process of law"**, strictly adhering to the literal interpretation of "procedure established by law". The broader "due process" interpretation was actually established decades later in the landmark *Maneka Gandhi case (1978)*.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Directive Principles of State Policy is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Directive Principles of State Policy is not correct?

options.a: They are not enforceable in a court of law.
options.b: They can override all the fundamental rights.
options.c: They can be related to social and economic justice.
options.d: They can be related to giving free legal aid.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **They can override all the fundamental rights.** The **Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP)** are inherently non-justiciable guidelines. The Supreme Court (in landmark cases like Minerva Mills) has firmly established a delicate balance between Part III and Part IV; the **DPSP cannot be utilized to fundamentally override or destroy all Fundamental Rights**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is not correct?

options.a: The term 'Secular Democratic Republic' was not there in the Preamble originally.
options.b: The Preamble contains the basic structure of the Constitution of India.
options.c: By itself, the Preamble is not enforceable in a court of law.
options.d: The Supreme Court has held that the Preamble cannot be considered as an aid to the legal interpretation of the Constitution, where the language is ambiguous.

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Supreme Court has held that the Preamble cannot be considered as an aid to the legal interpretation of the Constitution, where the language is ambiguous.** This statement is entirely incorrect. In several landmark judgments (most notably starting from Kesavananda Bharati), the Supreme Court definitively held that the **Preamble is an extremely important document and must be heavily used as an aid to legal interpretation** whenever the constitutional language is found to be ambiguous.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The Electoral College for electing the President of India does not include which of the following?
1. All elected members of the Rajya Sabha
2. All elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of States
3. All members of the Legislative Councils of States
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.questionText: The Electoral College for electing the President of India does not include which of the following? 1. All elected members of the Rajya Sabha 2. All elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of States 3. All members of the Legislative Councils of States Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **3 only**. The Presidential electoral college exclusively and symmetrically comprises elected MPs and elected MLAs. It definitively **does not include any members of the Legislative Councils of States (MLCs)**, whether elected or nominated, to maintain electoral parity among states, as not all states have a bicameral legislature.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: The Constitution of India contains no provision for the constitution of municipalities in every State for
questions.questionText: The Constitution of India contains no provision for the constitution of municipalities in every State for
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **an Urban Panchayat**. The **74th Amendment Act (Article 243Q)** comprehensively provides for exactly three types of municipalities in every State: a **Nagar Panchayat** (for transitional areas), a **Municipal Council** (for smaller urban areas), and a **Municipal Corporation** (for larger urban areas). There is absolutely no constitutional provision for an entity called an "Urban Panchayat".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Consider the following quote:
