"On the 26th January, 1950, we are going to enter a life of contradictions. In politics we will have equality and in social and economic life we will have inequality."
To whom among the following leaders is this quote attributed?
questions.questionText: Consider the following quote: "On the 26th January, 1950, we are going to enter a life of contradictions. In politics we will have equality and in social and economic life we will have inequality." To whom among the following leaders is this quote attributed?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **B. R. Ambedkar**. This profound socio-political warning about the impending reality of India—achieving political equality (one man, one vote) but inheriting deep-rooted structural social and economic inequality—was famously delivered by **Dr. B. R. Ambedkar** in his monumental concluding speech to the Constituent Assembly on November 25, 1949.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which one of the following powers has not been conferred on the President of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following powers has not been conferred on the President of India?

options.b: To decide at his/her discretion any question regarding disqualification of a Member of the Parliament
options.c: To grant pardon to a person who has been punished by a court-martial
options.d: To remove a Judge of the High Court

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **To decide at his/her discretion any question regarding disqualification of a Member of the Parliament**. Under Article 103, while the President formally decides questions on the disqualification of MPs, **the President cannot act at their own personal discretion**. The President must obtain the binding opinion of the **Election Commission** and act accordingly.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: In relation to the fundamental duties enshrined in the Constitution of India,
which one of the following is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: In relation to the fundamental duties enshrined in the Constitution of India, which one of the following is NOT correct?

options.a: The fundamental duties were not originally a part of the Constitution
options.b: To safeguard public property and abjure violence are fundamental duties
options.c: They are not enforceable through writs
options.d: They may not be used for determining constitutionality of laws

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **They may not be used for determining constitutionality of laws**. This is incorrect. Over the years, the Supreme Court has affirmatively ruled that **Fundamental Duties (Article 51A) can be actively used by courts to determine the constitutional validity of a law**. If a law seeks to give practical effect to a fundamental duty, it may be considered "reasonable" under Article 14 or 19.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: The Governor of a state may exercise discretionary powers when
1. he or she is not convinced by the advice of the Council of Ministers
2. the Constitution requires him/her to act in his/her discretion in certain matters
3. he or she is appointed as the administrator of an adjoining Union Territory
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: The Governor of a state may exercise discretionary powers when 1. he or she is not convinced by the advice of the Council of Ministers 2. the Constitution requires him/her to act in his/her discretion in certain matters 3. he or she is appointed as the administrator of an adjoining Union Territory Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. The Governor holds situational discretion (e.g., dismissing a ministry if they lose majority and the Governor is **not convinced by their advice** regarding the assembly), explicit **constitutional discretion** (e.g., reserving bills under Article 200, Sixth Schedule matters), and operates entirely independently when concurrently appointed as the **administrator of an adjoining Union Territory**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements with regard to Censure Motion is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements with regard to Censure Motion is NOT correct?

options.a: A leave of the House is required to move it
options.b: The Government is free to fix time and date for its discussion
options.c: It can also be moved against the entirety of the Council of Ministers
options.d: The Speaker decides whether the motion is in order

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **A leave of the House is required to move it**. Unlike a No-Confidence Motion, which requires the explicit prior leave of the House (support of at least 50 members) to even be admitted for debate, a **Censure Motion does not legally require the prior leave of the House** to be moved. It must, however, state explicit reasons for its adoption.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following is NOT a correct feature of a Money Bill ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a correct feature of a Money Bill ?

options.a: It can be introduced only in the Lok Sabha
options.b: A Deputy Speaker can also certify a Money Bill in case of office of Speaker falling vacant
options.c: A Speaker's authority is final as to whether a bill is Money Bill or not
options.d: It can be referred to a joint committee of the two Houses

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It can be referred to a joint committee of the two Houses**. This is incorrect. Because the Lok Sabha has absolute, overriding authority over a Money Bill, and the Rajya Sabha can only delay it by a maximum of 14 days, there is **no provision for a joint sitting or a joint committee** to resolve deadlocks over a **Money Bill**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: The idea of social justice requires that
1. equal treatment be supplemented with principle of proportionality
2. due and equal consideration is given to all individuals
3. equality of treatment be transcended to recognise special needs
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: The idea of social justice requires that 1. equal treatment be supplemented with principle of proportionality 2. due and equal consideration is given to all individuals 3. equality of treatment be transcended to recognise special needs Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3 only**. The underlying democratic principle of **social justice** demands that society gives **due and equal consideration to all individuals**, but it also dictates that strict formal equality of treatment must be transcended to **recognize and accommodate the special vulnerabilities and needs** of marginalized or weaker sections (e.g., through affirmative action or protective discrimination).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney-General of India is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney-General of India is NOT correct?

options.a: The person must be qualified to be appointed as a judge of the Supreme Court
options.b: He/she enjoys right of audience only in the Supreme Court and High Courts in India
options.c: He/she holds office during the pleasure of the President
options.d: He/she receives remuneration as determined by the President

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **He/she enjoys right of audience only in the Supreme Court and High Courts in India**. This is incorrect. Under **Article 76(3)**, in the performance of their official duties, the **Attorney General has the right of audience in ALL courts** throughout the territory of India, including district and lower tribunals, not just the Supreme Court and High Courts.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following is/are correct in relation to the Panchayats ?
1. Only the Parliament may endow Panchayats with powers and authority
2. A state may devolve powers to Panchayats in preparation of plans for economic development and social justice
3. A state may devolve powers to Panchayats in implementation of schemes for economic development and social justice
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are correct in relation to the Panchayats ? 1. Only the Parliament may endow Panchayats with powers and authority 2. A state may devolve powers to Panchayats in preparation of plans for economic development and social justice 3. A state may devolve powers to Panchayats in implementation of schemes for economic development and social justice Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3**. Under **Article 243G**, it is fundamentally the **State Legislature** (not the Union Parliament) that may by law endow the Panchayats with powers and authority to function as institutions of self-government. These devolved powers explicitly include the **preparation of plans** and the **implementation of schemes** for economic development and social justice.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: In relation to the powers of the Union Government of India,
which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
questions.questionText: In relation to the powers of the Union Government of India, which one of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.a: The Government of India may undertake legislative functions of a territory outside of India by agreement with the Government of that territory
options.b: The Union Government may not entrust a State Government with functions over matters where executive power of the Union extends
options.c: The Union Government may direct States for construction and maintenance of means of communication of military importance
options.d: The Governor may, with consent of Government of India, entrust its officers with functions on matters which fall within the executive power of the State

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Union Government may not entrust a State Government with functions over matters where executive power of the Union extends**. This is incorrect. Under the cooperative federalism principles of **Article 258(1)**, the President (representing the Union Government) **may, with the consent of the State Government, entrust** either conditionally or unconditionally to that Government or its officers, executive functions falling squarely under the Union's domain.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: As per provisions of the Constitution of India,
which one of the following is correct ?
questions.questionText: As per provisions of the Constitution of India, which one of the following is correct ?

options.a: Only Parliament has the power to legislate over a subject under the Concurrent List
options.b: Both Parliament and State Legislatures have the power to legislate over a subject under the Concurrent List
options.c: A legislation made under the Concurrent List by Parliament should be ratified by half of the State Legislatures so as to become a law of the country
options.d: Only State Legislatures are empowered to make laws under Concurrent List

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both Parliament and State Legislatures have the power to legislate over a subject under the Concurrent List**. The fundamental defining feature of the **Concurrent List (List III)** in the Seventh Schedule is that **both the Union Parliament and State Legislatures** hold concurrent, overlapping jurisdiction to enact laws regarding those subjects (with Union laws generally prevailing in case of conflict).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which of the following statement(s) regarding passing a law under the State List is/are correct ?
1. Even in the sphere (State List) exclusively reserved for the States, Parliament can legislate under certain circumstances
2. Parliament cannot legislate a subject under the State List under any circumstances
3. A resolution supported by two-thirds of the members present and voting is required to be passed by Rajya Sabha to pass a law under the State List
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statement(s) regarding passing a law under the State List is/are correct ? 1. Even in the sphere (State List) exclusively reserved for the States, Parliament can legislate under certain circumstances 2. Parliament cannot legislate a subject under the State List under any circumstances 3. A resolution supported by two-thirds of the members present and voting is required to be passed by Rajya Sabha to pass a law under the State List Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. Under exceptional constitutional circumstances (e.g., National Emergency, President's Rule, or implementation of international treaties), **Parliament can indeed legislate on State List subjects**. Specifically, under **Article 249**, the Rajya Sabha can authorize Parliament to legislate on a State List subject in the national interest by passing a resolution supported by **two-thirds of the members present and voting**. Therefore, statement 2 is completely false.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statement(s) regarding promulgation of ordinance is/are correct ?
1. President can promulgate ordinances which are of temporary duration
2. An ordinance promulgated by the President has to be ratified by Parliament within six weeks of its re-assembly
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statement(s) regarding promulgation of ordinance is/are correct ? 1. President can promulgate ordinances which are of temporary duration 2. An ordinance promulgated by the President has to be ratified by Parliament within six weeks of its re-assembly Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Both 1 and 2**. Under **Article 123**, the President can promulgate an **ordinance** when both Houses of Parliament are not in session. Such ordinances have the same force and effect as an Act but are inherently of **temporary duration**. They must legally be approved (**ratified**) by both Houses of Parliament **within six weeks of its re-assembly**, otherwise, they automatically cease to operate.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: State Legislatures Types, Committees, Procedures, Bills, etc.
statementLines: According to the Delimitation Commission of India and subject to Article 333 of the Constitution of India, what is the maximum number of members that a State Legislative Assembly may have ?
questions.questionText: According to the Delimitation Commission of India and subject to Article 333 of the Constitution of India, what is the maximum number of members that a State Legislative Assembly may have ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **500**. According to the explicit limits set in **Article 170(1)** of the Constitution, the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) of each State shall consist of not more than **500 members** and not less than 60 members, chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Who among the following was the first non-official member to be elected as Speaker of the Central Legislative Assembly on 24-8-1925 ?
questions.questionText: Who among the following was the first non-official member to be elected as Speaker of the Central Legislative Assembly on 24-8-1925 ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Vithalbhai J Patel**. In a historic milestone for Indian political representation during British rule, **Vithalbhai J. Patel**, a prominent leader of the Swaraj Party, broke the colonial monopoly and became the **first Indian and first non-official member to be elected as the President (Speaker)** of the Central Legislative Assembly in 1925.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct with regard to Speaker/Deputy Speaker, Lok Sabha ?
1. On dissolution of the Lok Sabha, both the Speaker and Deputy Speaker cease to be members of the House.
2. Both Speaker and Deputy Speaker vacate their office on dissolution of the Lok Sabha
3. Only Deputy Speaker vacates his office and Speaker shall not vacate his office until immediately before the first meeting of the Lok Sabha after dissolution
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct with regard to Speaker/Deputy Speaker, Lok Sabha ? 1. On dissolution of the Lok Sabha, both the Speaker and Deputy Speaker cease to be members of the House. 2. Both Speaker and Deputy Speaker vacate their office on dissolution of the Lok Sabha 3. Only Deputy Speaker vacates his office and Speaker shall not vacate his office until immediately before the first meeting of the Lok Sabha after dissolution Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 3 only**. When the Lok Sabha is dissolved, **all members (including the Speaker and Deputy Speaker) cease to be members of the House**. However, as a special constitutional exception under Article 94 to maintain continuity, the **Speaker does not immediately vacate his office**, retaining it until immediately before the first meeting of the newly elected Lok Sabha. The Deputy Speaker immediately vacates the office upon dissolution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: With regard to Panel of Chairperson, Lok Sabha, which of the following statements is/are correct ?
1. Panel of Chairperson is drawn from the ruling party only
2. Panel of Chairperson is nominated by different political parties and appointed by Speaker, Lok Sabha
3. Panel of Chairperson consists of 10 members and one of them presides over the House when both Speaker and Deputy Speaker are not there
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: With regard to Panel of Chairperson, Lok Sabha, which of the following statements is/are correct ? 1. Panel of Chairperson is drawn from the ruling party only 2. Panel of Chairperson is nominated by different political parties and appointed by Speaker, Lok Sabha 3. Panel of Chairperson consists of 10 members and one of them presides over the House when both Speaker and Deputy Speaker are not there Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 and 3**. The **Panel of Chairpersons** in the Lok Sabha is **appointed by the Speaker** from among the members (traditionally nominating members from across various political parties, not just the ruling party). The panel consists of up to **10 members**, and any one of them can validly preside over the House in the physical absence of both the Speaker and Deputy Speaker.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: A member giving notice of a resolution for removal of Speaker, Lok Sabha should be addressed to the
questions.questionText: A member giving notice of a resolution for removal of Speaker, Lok Sabha should be addressed to the
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Secretary General, Lok Sabha**. As per the established Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, any member intending to move a formal resolution for the **removal of the Speaker** must give written notice addressed formally to the **Secretary General of the Lok Sabha**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding Public Accounts Committee is correct ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding Public Accounts Committee is correct ?

options.a: It consists of fifteen members from Lok Sabha and seven members from Rajya Sabha
options.b: It consists of twenty members from Lok Sabha and ten members from Rajya Sabha
options.c: The term of members of Public Accounts Committee is five years
options.d: Finance Minister is the Chairperson of the Public Accounts Committee

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **It consists of fifteen members from Lok Sabha and seven members from Rajya Sabha**. The **Public Accounts Committee (PAC)** has a fixed total strength of 22 members, comprising exactly **15 members elected from the Lok Sabha** and **7 members from the Rajya Sabha**. The term is strictly one year, and a Minister cannot be its member or chairperson.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements with regard to the Speaker, Lok Sabha is/are correct ?
1. The election of the Speaker of Lok Sabha is conducted by the Election Commission of India
2. The Speaker on assuming his office is not required to make and subscribe oath or affirmation
3. The Speaker does not exercise a casting vote in case of equality of votes
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements with regard to the Speaker, Lok Sabha is/are correct ? 1. The election of the Speaker of Lok Sabha is conducted by the Election Commission of India 2. The Speaker on assuming his office is not required to make and subscribe oath or affirmation 3. The Speaker does not exercise a casting vote in case of equality of votes Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The **Speaker** has already taken a general oath as an ordinary Member of Parliament and is thus **not required to take a separate, specific oath** upon assuming the Speaker's office. The election is overseen by the Pro-tem Speaker, not the Election Commission. The Speaker crucially **does exercise a casting vote** under Article 100 precisely in the case of an equality of votes.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Foundations & Framework
statementLines: Who was the President of the Constituent Assembly ?
questions.questionText: Who was the President of the Constituent Assembly ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Rajendra Prasad**. Following the temporary two-day tenure of the oldest member Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha, **Dr. Rajendra Prasad** was formally and unanimously elected as the permanent **President of the Constituent Assembly** of India on December 11, 1946.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney-General of India is NOT correct ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney-General of India is NOT correct ?

options.a: He shall have the right to speak in the Houses of Parliament
options.b: He shall have a right of audience in all Courts in the territory of India in the performance of his official duties
options.c: He must have the same qualifications as are required to be a Judge of the Supreme Court
options.d: He is a whole-time counsel for the Government

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **He is a whole-time counsel for the Government**. While the Attorney General is the highest law officer in India, he is **not considered a full-time government servant** (whole-time counsel). Because of this, the AG is legally permitted to continue private legal practice, provided it does not conflict with the interests of the Government of India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements relating to short notice questions asked in the Legislature :
1. These relate to matters of urgent public importance and can be asked for oral answer at a notice less than 10 days
2. Short notice questions can be admissible if the Minister concerned agrees to answer to it
3. Short-notice questions are asked during question hour
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements relating to short notice questions asked in the Legislature : 1. These relate to matters of urgent public importance and can be asked for oral answer at a notice less than 10 days 2. Short notice questions can be admissible if the Minister concerned agrees to answer to it 3. Short-notice questions are asked during question hour Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. **Short Notice Questions** address matters of urgent public importance and are permitted to bypass the standard 10-day notice rule. They are only admitted if the **Minister agrees to answer them on short notice**. Crucially, they are normally answered **immediately after the Question Hour** ends, not during it.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XV: Elections
statementLines: Which one of the following is NOT true of the 10th schedule of the Constitution of India pertaining to disqualification of members of the Parliament and State legislatures ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT true of the 10th schedule of the Constitution of India pertaining to disqualification of members of the Parliament and State legislatures ?

options.a: The schedule lays down that elected members may be disqualified on the grounds of defection
options.b: Disqualification on ground of defection does not apply in cases of merger with another political party
options.c: Cases of dispute are decided by the speaker or Chairman of the House concerned
options.d: The Supreme Court of India is the final arbiter in cases which remain unresolved

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Supreme Court of India is the final arbiter in cases which remain unresolved**. Under the **Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law)**, the Presiding Officer (Speaker or Chairman) acts as a tribunal to decide cases of defection, and their decision is final. The Supreme Court's role is restricted strictly to **judicial review** of the Speaker's final decision on grounds of mala fides or perversity, not as a primary "final arbiter" stepping in for unresolved cases.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which of the following pairs of list and contents is/are correctly matched ?
1. State list : Public health and sanitation
2. Union list : Citizenship, naturalisation and aliens
3. Concurrent list : Legal, medical and other professions
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following pairs of list and contents is/are correctly matched ? 1. State list : Public health and sanitation 2. Union list : Citizenship, naturalisation and aliens 3. Concurrent list : Legal, medical and other professions Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 3**. All pairs are correctly matched according to the constitutional distribution in the **Seventh Schedule**. **Public health and sanitation** is firmly under the State List. **Citizenship and aliens** are exclusively under the Union List. **Legal, medical, and other professions** fall under the Concurrent List, allowing both central and state regulation.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which of the following is NOT provided for under the Directive Principles of State Policy ?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT provided for under the Directive Principles of State Policy ?

options.a: Right to work, to education and to public assistance in certain cases
options.b: Uniform Civil Code for the citizens
options.d: Participation of workers in the management of local-self government

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Participation of workers in the management of local-self government**. The DPSP (specifically Article 43A) directs the State to secure the **participation of workers in the management of industries**, not in local self-government institutions. Rights to work, UCC, and judicial separation are explicitly mentioned in Articles 41, 44, and 50 respectively.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.microTopic: Directive Principles of State Policy DPSP
statementLines: Which one of the following statements regarding the Directive Principles of State Policy is NOT correct ?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding the Directive Principles of State Policy is NOT correct ?

options.a: State shall follow the Directive Principles of State Policy both in the matter of administration as well as in the making of laws
options.b: The Directive Principles of State Policy embody the object of the State under the republican Constitution
options.c: The Directive Principles of State Policy have precedence over the Fundamental Rights in case of conflict between the two
options.d: The Directive Principles of State Policy are not enforceable in the Courts

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **The Directive Principles of State Policy have precedence over the Fundamental Rights in case of conflict between the two**. In landmark judgments (like ChampakamDorairajan and Minerva Mills), the Supreme Court has ruled that in cases of direct conflict, **Fundamental Rights generally prevail over Directive Principles**, establishing the bedrock principle that DPSP cannot override Part III.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: Which of the following are the defining features of liberal democracy ?
1. Constitutional government based on formal, usually legal, rules
2. Guarantees of civil liberties and individual rights
3. It invests political authority with potentially unlimited power
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the defining features of liberal democracy ? 1. Constitutional government based on formal, usually legal, rules 2. Guarantees of civil liberties and individual rights 3. It invests political authority with potentially unlimited power Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. A **liberal democracy** is inherently characterized by a structured **constitutional government** and the robust, enforceable protection of **civil liberties and individual rights**. It explicitly operates on the concept of limited government, meaning it fundamentally **rejects the idea of granting unlimited power** to any political authorities.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part IX, IXA, IXB: Local Governments
questions.microTopic: Local Government Panchayats, Municipalities, Cooperative Societies
statementLines: Which of the following is/are true regarding Urban Local Bodies in India ?
1. The Constitution 74th Amendment Act envisages three types of urban local bodies, namely Municipal Corporation, Municipality and City Council
2. Municipal Corporations are established in cities with population greater than 1 million
3. Bombay and Calcutta Corporations were the first Municipal Corporations that were established during British time
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are true regarding Urban Local Bodies in India ? 1. The Constitution 74th Amendment Act envisages three types of urban local bodies, namely Municipal Corporation, Municipality and City Council 2. Municipal Corporations are established in cities with population greater than 1 million 3. Bombay and Calcutta Corporations were the first Municipal Corporations that were established during British time Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. The 74th Amendment Act envisages Nagar Panchayats, Municipal Councils (Municipalities), and **Municipal Corporations** (typically established for larger urban areas with a population > 1 million). Statement 3 is historically incorrect because **Madras (Chennai)** was the very first Municipal Corporation established in India in 1688, predating Calcutta and Bombay.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following statements are correct regarding the election of the President of India ?
1. He is elected indirectly
2. He is elected through an electoral college consisting of all elected MPs and MLAs
3. He is elected directly
4. Members of the Legislative Council have no right to vote in the Presidential election
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct regarding the election of the President of India ? 1. He is elected indirectly 2. He is elected through an electoral college consisting of all elected MPs and MLAs 3. He is elected directly 4. Members of the Legislative Council have no right to vote in the Presidential election Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. The President of India is **elected indirectly** by an electoral college consisting exclusively of the **elected MPs and elected MLAs** (including those of Delhi and Puducherry). Consequently, nominated members and all **Members of the Legislative Council (MLCs)** are strictly barred from voting in the Presidential election.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which of the following are related to Right to Equality under the provisions of Articles 14 – 18 of the Constitution of India ?
1. Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
2. Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
3. Educational and cultural rights to minorities
4. Abolition of untouchability
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following are related to Right to Equality under the provisions of Articles 14 – 18 of the Constitution of India ? 1. Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth 2. Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment 3. Educational and cultural rights to minorities 4. Abolition of untouchability Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1, 2 and 4**. The **Right to Equality (Articles 14-18)** covers the prohibition of discrimination (**Article 15**), equality of opportunity in public employment (**Article 16**), and the abolition of untouchability (**Article 17**). Educational and cultural rights to minorities are a separate category entirely, falling under **Articles 29-30**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: Which of the following is/are correct regarding the office of Governor in India ?
1. One Governor can act as Governor of more than one State
2. The Governor has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, remission of punishment to persons convicted under the State Law
3. The Governor is appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Chief Minister of the respective State
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are correct regarding the office of Governor in India ? 1. One Governor can act as Governor of more than one State 2. The Governor has the power to grant pardons, reprieves, remission of punishment to persons convicted under the State Law 3. The Governor is appointed by the President on the recommendation of the Chief Minister of the respective State Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 2 only**. By virtue of the 7th Amendment Act, **one person can act as the Governor of more than one State**. Under Article 161, the Governor holds the **power to grant pardons** for offenses against State Laws. Statement 3 is entirely false; while the President appoints the Governor, there is absolutely **no constitutional requirement to act on the recommendation of the State's Chief Minister**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The major cleavage in global human rights discourse has been between :
questions.questionText: The major cleavage in global human rights discourse has been between :

options.d: Civil and political rights v. economic, social and cultural rights

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **civil and political rights v. economic, social and cultural rights**. The primary historic and ideological cleavage in international human rights discourse (often reflecting the Cold War divide between Western nations and the Soviet bloc) has been the prioritization of first-generation **civil and political rights** versus second-generation **economic, social, and cultural rights**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Which of the following statements related to Money Bills is not correct ?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements related to Money Bills is not correct ?

options.a: It cannot be introduced in the Council of States
options.b: If any question arises whether the Bill is Money Bill or not, the decision of the Speaker is final
options.c: In case of deadlock over a Money Bill, the President can summon a joint sitting of the Parliament
options.d: A Money Bill cannot be introduced except on the recommendation of the President

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **In case of deadlock over a Money Bill, the President can summon a joint sitting of the Parliament**. This is constitutionally incorrect. The Constitution grants the Lok Sabha absolute overriding powers over **Money Bills**. Since the Rajya Sabha cannot reject it (it can only delay it by 14 days), a legislative deadlock is impossible. Thus, **a joint sitting (Article 108) is explicitly prohibited** for Money Bills.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Which of the following statements regarding the residuary powers under the Constitution of India is/are correct ?
1. Residuary powers have been given to the Union Parliament
2. In the matter of residuary power, the Constitution of India follows the Constitution of Australia
3. Schedule 7 of the Constitution of India provides a list of residuary powers
4. The Government of India Act 1935 placed residuary powers in the hands of the Governor General
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements regarding the residuary powers under the Constitution of India is/are correct ? 1. Residuary powers have been given to the Union Parliament 2. In the matter of residuary power, the Constitution of India follows the Constitution of Australia 3. Schedule 7 of the Constitution of India provides a list of residuary powers 4. The Government of India Act 1935 placed residuary powers in the hands of the Governor General Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **1 and 4**. Under Article 248, **residuary powers are exclusively vested in the Union Parliament**. Historically, the **Government of India Act 1935 placed residuary powers strictly in the hands of the Governor-General**. Statement 2 is false as India's model of vesting residuary powers with the Center follows Canada, not Australia. Statement 3 is false because residuary powers are, by definition, those subjects *not listed* in Schedule 7.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: The Ex-Officio Members of the National Human Rights Commission of India include:
questions.questionText: The Ex-Officio Members of the National Human Rights Commission of India include:

options.a: Chairpersons of National Commission for Minorities, National Commission for Scheduled Castes, National Commission for Scheduled Tribes, National Commission for Women
options.b: Chairpersons of National Commission for Minorities, National Commission for Scheduled Castes, National Commission for Scheduled Tribes only
options.c: Chairpersons of National Commission for Scheduled Castes, National Commission for Scheduled Tribes; National Commission for Women only
options.d: Chairpersons of National Commission for Minorities, National Commission for Scheduled Castes, National Commission for Scheduled Tribes, National Commission for Protection of Child Rights

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Chairpersons of National Commission for Minorities, National Commission for Scheduled Castes, National Commission for Scheduled Tribes, National Commission for Women**. Under the original framework of the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993, the chairpersons of these four vital national commissions serve as automatic, **ex-officio members** of the NHRC to ensure broad representation and coordination on human rights issues.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
statementLines: Which Article of the Constitution of India safeguards one's right to marry the person of one's choice?
questions.questionText: Which Article of the Constitution of India safeguards one's right to marry the person of one's choice?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Article 21**. In the *Hadiya* case (Shafin Jahan v. Asokan K.M., 2018), the Supreme Court unequivocally ruled that the **right to marry a person of one's choice is an integral part of the Right to Life and Personal Liberty** guaranteed under **Article 21** of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
statementLines: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements:
1. No High Court shall have the jurisdiction to declare any central law to be constitutionally invalid.
2. An amendment to the Constitution of India cannot be called into question by the Supreme Court of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Constitution of India, consider the following statements: 1. No High Court shall have the jurisdiction to declare any central law to be constitutionally invalid. 2. An amendment to the Constitution of India cannot be called into question by the Supreme Court of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Neither 1 nor 2**. High Courts possess full power under **Article 226** to declare a central law unconstitutional. (A 42nd Amendment restriction on this was repealed by the 43rd Amendment). Constitutional amendments are also subject to **judicial review** by the Supreme Court to check if they violate the "Basic Structure".

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Institutions & Governance
statementLines: In the context of polity,
which one of the following would you accept as the most appropriate definition of liberty?
questions.questionText: In the context of polity, which one of the following would you accept as the most appropriate definition of liberty?

options.a: Protection against the tyranny of political rulers
options.c: Opportunity to do whatever one likes

questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **Opportunity to develop oneself fully**. In political theory, positive liberty is defined not merely as the absence of restraints, but affirmatively as the provision of conditions and **opportunities that allow individuals to develop their human potential and personality fully**.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part V & VI: The Union and The States
questions.microTopic: Parliament Procedures, Proceedings, Officers, Bills, Committees, Houses, etc.
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. In the first Lok Sabha, the single largest party in the opposition was the Swatantra Party.
2. In the Lok Sabha, a "Leader of the Opposition" was recognised for the first time in 1969.
3. In the Lok Sabha, if a party does not have a minimum of 75 members, its leader cannot be recognised as the Leader of the Opposition.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. In the first Lok Sabha, the single largest party in the opposition was the Swatantra Party. 2. In the Lok Sabha, a "Leader of the Opposition" was recognised for the first time in 1969. 3. In the Lok Sabha, if a party does not have a minimum of 75 members, its leader cannot be recognised as the Leader of the Opposition. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown: Explanation: **2 only**. The single largest opposition party in the first Lok Sabha was the **Communist Party of India (CPI)**, not the Swatantra Party. The first official Leader of the Opposition was recognized in **1969** (Ram Subhag Singh). The quorum required to be recognized as the principal opposition party is 10% of the total strength, which is **55 members in the Lok Sabha**, not 75.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XX: Amendment of the Constitution
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Parliament of India can place a particular law in the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India.
2. The validity of a law placed in the Ninth Schedule cannot be examined by any court and no judgement can be made on it.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Parliament of India can place a particular law in the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India. 2. The validity of a law placed in the Ninth Schedule cannot be examined by any court and no judgement can be made on it. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Explanation: **1 only**. Parliament has the power to place laws in the **Ninth Schedule** via Article 31B. However, following the landmark *I.R. Coelho* judgment (2007), the Supreme Court ruled that laws placed in the Ninth Schedule after April 24, 1973 (the date of the Kesavananda Bharati verdict) **are open to judicial review** if they violate the Basic Structure. Thus,

statement 2 is incorrect.
